inscription.head:
No. 1.
(A.R. No. B. 551 of 1961-62.)
BHŌGĀVATI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab crected horizontally near the temple of the Hanumān.

It states that when Akāḷavarsha was ruling over the empire, his subordinate Jagattuṅgadēva
administered Eḍadoṟe-2000 as aṇugajīvitā. The record being damaged seems to register a land grant
by certain gāvuṇḍas probably to Vāchaspatibhaṭṭa, the sthānapati of Pallavēśvara.

In the date portion only the cyclic year Virōdhikrit is mentioned. If it is equated with
Śaka 693 then the date 771-772 A.D. suits with the reign period of king Akāḷavarsha Kṛishṇa I.

l.note: The letter Lu is written below the line.

inscription.head:
No. 2.
(A.R. No. B. 546 of 1961-62.)
BALLAṬGI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup near the Mosque [front face].

This record in cursive characters refers to the rule of Nityavarsha Śrī Prithvīvallabha
[Probably Rāshṭrakūṭa Indra III]. It mentions a mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara (name not clear) and the place
Baḷḷaniṭṭage. The purport seems to record a grant but not clear.

It may be placed to early 10th century A.D. on the basis of a reference to Nityavarsha
but not certain.

inscription.head:
No. 3.
(A.R. No. B. 823 of 1962-63.)
NUGḌŌṆI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar in front of the Basavēśvara temple.

This damaged record in 10th century characters belongs to the reign period of [Rāshṭrakūṭa]
Kannaradēva. It seems to refer to a grant of land but not clear.

l.note: Letter 'na' is written below the line.
Stops here abruptly.

inscription.head:
No. 4.
(A.R. No. B. 138 of 1962-63.)
EGUVA BANDAPALLE, RAYACHOTI TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone (No. 2) beside the ruined Śiva temple on the road-side.

This damaged hero-stone records the death of a hero while fighting with Vikramayya. The
king Kāma-Vaidumba is described as mahārā[jādhi]rāja and paramēśvara. There is a mention of
Koṇḍu[vu]ra Narapa • • •

l.ka: • la gaṇḍatriṇētra śrī kāmaveyduṁba mahārā[jādhi]rāja paramēsvara
• • dgaḷḷidiredeḍe tarenijelvu galudī[vāje] • • ga • • le • • galuṁ •

inscription.head:
No. 5.
(A.R. No. 39 of 1961-62.)
GAṄGĀPURAM, MAHABUBNAGAR TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Three sides of a slab by the side of Benakayya-guḍi in front of the Chennakēśava temple.

Top portion of the slab is broken and lost. This partially damaged record refers to the
construction of a Jina-griha and the consecration of the image of Chenna Pārśvanātha there in
by mahāvaḍḍa-vyavahāri maṇigāra Kāḷiseṭṭi of Tūbāḷa and to a gift of income from levies on
commodities such as areca etc., for the worship and offerings to the Jina Pārśvanātha.

It refers to the Chālukya dynasty of whom Tailapa is mentioned and contains an eu-
logy of Nayakīrtidēva who was probably the recipient of the gift. There is a description of the
city Gangāpuram where the Jina-griha was built. Also it mentions officers like sunkādhikāri and
aḍḍasuṅkad-adhikāri.

Information regarding the date is partially lost as the top portion of the slab is broken
and lost• The extant portion of it provides [Śrī]mukha, Phālguṇa śu • • Brihaspativāra, uttarāyaṇa
saṁkrānti. It is in characters of the 10th century A.D. [Tentatively the cyclic year Śrīmukha can
be equated with Śaka 895 on the basis of only Tailapa being mentioned in the record].

edition.h4: First Face-Top portion is broken and lost.

inscription.head:
No. 6.
(A.R. No. B. 235 of 1962-63.)
DŪḌKI, BANASWADA TALUK, NIZAMABAD DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the four faces of a pillar lying beside the house of Alauddin Phakir.

The top portion of the pillar is broken and lost. It seems to record a gift made to a
certain Chandrarāsi by the king Tailaparasa and his ministers.

There is a reference at the beginning of the record to a Śivabhakta Nārāyaṇa and his
lineage. Also there is a reference to a dīkshāguru whose particulars are not clear.
It is in characters of about the 10th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 7.
(A.R. No. B. 459 of 1961-62.)
DHARMAPURI, MOMINABAD TALUK, BHIR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Impression received from the Deputy Director, Archaeology, Aurangabad, Maharashtra.

It is records the grant of a wet land of one mattar extent by the side of a tank, one
garden to its west and a house to god Kāḷēśvara by Dēmarasa, son of Mukutiya-maṁneya
Muṁjarāṇe. The Five-hundred svāmis of mahāgrahāra of Lokkiguṁḍi is stated to be the
protectors of this grant. The record mentions the god named Gavarēśvara.

It is dated in Jagadēkamalla year 2, Siddhārthin, Kārtika, śu. 15, Ādivāra [Śaka 941]. It
corresponds to 1019 A.D., October 16, Friday.

inscription.head:
No. 8.
(A.R. No. B. 534 of 1961-62.)
BALLAṬGI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup near the Agasikaṭṭi.

It states that during the reign of Chālukya Jagadēkamalla, his queen Mahādēvi installed
the god Traipurushadēva at Baḷḷaniṭṭage and made a grant of land to the temple. It is said that
the land so granted was earlier obtained from Lōkāditya who was administering the manneya of
Ayyaṇavāḍi-300. It is said that the queen was then camping at Purvvaravaḷḷi.

It is dated Śaka 944, Dundubhi, Chaitra, śu. 13 Thursday. It corresponds to 1022 A.D., March
18, Sunday.

inscription.head:
No. 9.
(A.R. No. B. 542 of 1961-62.)
BALLAṬGI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying near the School.

It states that during the reign of Chālukya Jagadēkamalla, his queen Sōmaladēvi made
a grant of 24 mattar land, a garden of one matter, 5 houses and one oil-mill to the basadi of
Bhattagrāma-Baḷḷaniṭṭage when she was camping at Pandirkke. The village Bhattagrāma Baḷḷaniṭṭage
is said to have been situated with in Eḍadoṟe-2000. The rod of measurement Piriyakōl also
mentioned here.

The record is dated Śaka 951. Śukla, Pushya ba. 1, Wednesday uttarāyana sankrānti. It
corresponds to 1029 A.D., December 24, Wednesday.
The slab is broken on top and right corner.

inscription.head:
No. 10.
(A.R. No. B. 545 of 1961-62.)
BALLAṬGI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in the locality called Daṇḍada māgi.

It states that Sōmaladēvi, the queen of Chālukya Jagadēkamalladēva while camping at
Ēttagiri, made a grant of land and a house site with a house at Bhattagrāma Baḷḷaniṭṭage to
Madhusūdhana Bhaṭṭa of Digbhige. The land of 100 mattar extent was measured by the measuring
rod Piriyakōlu.

It is dated śaka 953 Prajāpati, Śrāvaṇa, Lunar eclipse, Wednesday. It corresponds to 1031
A.D., August 5. The Lunar eclipse occurred on this day (Thursday).

l.ka: śrī phaḷabhōga bhāginī dvitīya lakṣmī viśiṣṭa-
tasya tasya tadā phalaṁ || svadattāṁ parada-

inscription.head:
No. 11.
(A.R. No. B. 828 of 1962-63.)
SUṄKANŪR, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup near the temple of Maṇṇa-Basavaṇṇa.

It states that when the Chālukya king Jagadēkamalladēva was ruling over the empire,
queen Bijjaladēvi while camping at Poṭṭaḷakeṟe, made some grant probably to a temple built by
Nāgale at Duggapavaṭṭi. The details of which are lost.

It is dated Śaka 95[3], [Bahudhānya], Māgha, śu. [2], sankrānti, Sunday. The given Śaka
year do not tally with the cyclic year. Cyclic year Prajāpati occurred in Śaka 953 and that of
Bahudhānya in Śaka 960. [Śaka 95[3] (Prajāpati) 1032 A.D. and Bahudhānya (Śaka 960) 1038 A.D.].
If to be considered on the basis of the Śaka year 95[3] (cyclic year Prajāpati) as in the previous
record [No. 10] then the tithi corresponds to 1032 A.D., January 17, Monday not Sunday. This may
be the intended date of the record.

[The tithi Bahudhānya, Māgha, śu. 2 corresponds to 1038 A.D. December 30, Saturday].
There was no sankrānthi on the day of the tithi in both the cyclic years. [Both the dates fall with
in the reign period of the king Jayasiṁha II].

The slab has the sculptures of a huge three-hooded Nāga erect on its tail and a cow at
the bottom.

edition.h4: On the proper right side of the image of hooded Nāga.
On the proper left side of the image of hooded Nāga.

inscription.head:
No. 12.
(A.R. No. 71 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the same pillar, Museum No. 88.

It records that during the reign of Chālukya Jagadēkamalla manevarggaḍe
Nannapayya, the possessor of traya-guṇa made a gift of the village Gāḍicheṟuvu situated within
Ānemārga-70 adjoining Koḷḷipāke-7000 for the worship and offerings to the god Nanni-Nārayaṇa.
Refers to Surēndradēva, probably the sthānapati of the place. The said gift was made on the
occasion of Solar eclipse.

It is dated Śaka 954, Āngīrasa, Āshāḍha, Amāvāsye, Monday. It corresponds to 1032 A.D.,
July 10, Monday. There was Solar eclipse on that day.

l.note: Note-(1) Lines 23-27 are engraved
Note-(1) Lines 23-27 are engraved on the top of the record to the right side of the

inscription.head:
No. 13.
(A.R. No. B. 831 of 1962-63.)
TAḌKAL, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Fragmentary slab built into the wall of the agasi.

This very much damaged record belongs to the reign of Chālukya Jagadēkamalla who
was ruling the empire from the capital [Ēta]giri. Probably it recorded grant of 15 mattar land to
some one [name lost] and 12 mattar land to a Bhaṭara [name lost]. Perhaps these grants are to a
god and to its sthānapati respectively.

It is dated Śaka 955, Śrīmukha, Chaitra, Amāvāsye, Sunday, sankrānti. Probably it
corresponds to 1033 A.D., April 2 f.d.t. 25, Monday.

inscription.head:
No. 14.
(A.R. No. B. 536 of 1961-62.)
BALLAṬGI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in two pieces kept near the Paikar-bāvi.

It states that king Chālukya Jagadēkamalla while camping at Poṭṭalakeṟe, made a grant
to god Chandēśvara of Bhattagrāma Baḷḷaniṭṭage for worship and offerings, music, singing and
dancing and for feeding of ascetics in the temple. It is said that the grant was made at the in-
stance of Sadyōjātā Paṇḍita. The record informs that Baḷḷaniṭṭage was situated in the division of
Mosaḷīkal-300, a kaṁpaṇa of Eḍadoṟe-2000.

The record is dated Śaka 958, Dhātu, Pushya śu. 3, Thursday, uttarāyaṇa sankrānti. It
corresponds to 1036 A.D., December 23, Thursday.

l.note: Rest is broken and lost.

inscription.head:
No. 15.
(A.R. No. B. 825 of 1962-63.)
NUGḌŌṆI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in the field Known as Munnappana-hola.

It states that when queen Sōmaladēvi was camping at Poṭṭḷakeṟe, she made a grant of
20 mattar land, a garden of one mattar extent, a house site and a oil-mill for a perpetual lamp
to the god Baṅkēśvara of Baḷḷaniṭṭige, a bhattagrāma (village) of Mosaḷikal-300 situated in the
administrative division Eḍadoṟe-2000. The grant was made at the request of Jaychanḍariti Mārabbe,
after worshipping Gōkarṇnarāsijīya. Perhaps Gōkarṇnarāsijīya received the grant made in favour
of the god by the queen.

The grant seems to have been made on Pushya ba. Wednesday, uttarāyaṇa sankrānti
was recorded on Māgha ba. Thursday in the cyclic year Yuva of Śaka 957. The first date may
correspond with 24th December 1035 A.D., Wednesday and the second with 22 January 1036 A.D.,
[Kuṁbha sankramaṇa occured on this day but not mentioned in the record].

l.ka: • ndada gōkarṇnarāsi jīyargge hastōdakaṁ geydu ka-
radu lekka[voḷa] sammandadiṁ māgha bahuḷa bṛha-
diṁ sarvvanamasyaṁ biṭṭa nela mattarippatu tōṁṭa ma-

l.note: Stops here abruptly.

inscription.head:
No. 16.
(A.R. No. B. 538 of 1961-62.)
BALLAṬGI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On two pieces of a slab lying near the temple of Rāmalinga.

The record is broken and damaged. It registers a grant of land to the temple of Chāmēśvara
for worship and offerings to the god, repairs and maintenance of the temple, construction of a sabhā-
manṭapa, for the performances such as music, singing and dancing and three oil mills for burning
perpetual lamps in the temple by probably the queen (name list) of Chālukya Trailōkyamalladēva. The
grant was probably made over to a Kāḷāmukha saint. There is a mention of Mosaḷikal-300 a kaṁpaṇa
of Eḍadoṟe-2000 and the place Māḷavūra. The record at the end tells us that the engraver-Vōja also
has been given 5 mattar land. The measuring rod Piriyakōlu, and Bāguḷa-māna are mentioned here.

It is dated in the cyclic year Īśvara, Pushya, ba. 5 Monday. It corresponds to 1037 A.D.,
December 29. But the weekday was Thursday. The date corresponds to the reign period of
Chālukya Jayasiṁha II who (name lost in this damaged record) is mentioned as Trailōkyamalladēva.

inscription.head:
No. 17.
(A.R. No. B. 779 of 1962-63.)
NIPPĀṆI, CHIKKODI TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup outside the Mallikārjuna temple.

This damaged record is belongs to the reign period of Chālukya Jagadēkamalla [Jayasiṁha
II] and states that the queen Sōmaladēvi while administering a division [name not clear] installed
the image of goddess Sarasvati at Supāṇi and gifted 300 mattar of land and two gardens for the
worship and offerings of the goddess and for feeding students and teachers of an educational
institution attached to the temple. Further, she made a gift of 50 gadyāṇa also.

As post script another gift has been recorded in the inscription. Durgasimha, the
ṁane-verggaḍe of the queen Sōmaladēvi, built the Sarasvati maṇḍapa [probably in the same teM-
ple] at Supāṇi [modern Nippāṇi].

The record is dated Śaka 960, Bahudhānya, Vaiśākha ba. 5, Thursday. It corresponds to
1038 A.D., April 26. The week day was however Wednesday.

inscription.head:
No. 18.
(A.R. No. B. 529 of 1961-62.)
BĀGALWĀḌ, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Fragment of a stone, lying in the temple of Bangārada Basavaṇṇa.

This fragment in characters of the 11th century A.D. contains only a portion of the praśasti
and the first syllable of the name Jagadēkamalla.

inscription.head:
No. 19.
(A.R. No. B. 541 of 1961-62.)
BALLAṬGI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab near the Girijavvana Gadduge.

The record in the 11th century A.D. characters, states that mahāsāmanta Lōkarasa of the
Haihaya family made a land grant to the sculptor Nēmōja at Moṟaba. Lōkarasa is described as
the son of Chālukya king Jagadēkamalla.

l.ka: śrī svasti gaṁgājaḷa dhautavastra nirmmaḷa parama • • • • •

inscription.head:
No. 20.
(A.R. No. B. 556 of 1961-62.)
HARVI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying out side the village.

In this very badly damaged inscription only Śrīmat-Jagadēkamalla can be made out. The
purport of this 11th century A.D. character record is not clear. This Jagadēkamalla is the same as
Chālukya Jayasiṁha II.

inscription.head:
No. 21.
(A.R. No. B. 599 of 1961-62.)
MĀNVI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in the lane called Bankuṇṭa.

This broken slab belonging to the reign period of Chālukya Jagadēkamalla contains the
praśasti of a mahāmaṇḍlēśvara (name lost) of the Haihaya family. It is in characters of the 11th
century A.D.

edition.p: [Top portion with a few lines of writing is broken and lost].

inscription.head:
No. 22.
(A.R. No. 27 of 1961-62.)
JAṬAPRŌLE, KOLLAPUR TALUK, MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On another pillar lying in front of the Vēṇugōpālaswāmy temple.

Bottom portion of the pillar is broken and lost resulting in loss of a few lines of writing
on all the three faces where the record is engraved.

The record registers a land grant to Lakuḷēśvara-bhaṭṭāraka of Rāmatīrtha made probably
by the king Jagadēkamalla while he was camping at Sindaya-taḍi in Taṟekāḍu-nāḍu. Mentions Et-
tapi in Vaḍḍavaṇa-500.

The date portion is partially lost and only (Phā)lguṇa, śu. 10, Wednesday can be made out.
It is in characters of the 11th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 23.
(A.R. No. 80 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar No. 1-pillar dugout from the main street in the village.

When Chālukya Trailōkyamalladēva was ruling over the empire from Kollāpura and
mahāmandaḷēśvara Kommaṇayyarasa was administering Koḷḷipāke-7000 it seems records that some
taxes were remitted by the seṭṭis, gāmuṇḍas and the prajes of Koḷḷipāke. The record was written
by Jakkarāja.

The record is dated Śaka 969 Vyaya, Chaitra, śu. 1, Monday. It corresponds to 1046 A.D.,
March 11, Tuesday.

inscription.head:
No. 24.
(A.R. No. 99 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALAGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar in the compound of the Jaina basadi.

Mentions the rule of Chālukya Āhavamalla and records the gift of the income from the
levy on salt for the up keep of the tank Piriyakere by the six Seṭṭis and one hundred-and-twenty
nagara [nakhara] probably of Koḷḷipāke.

It is dated Śaka 9(6)9 Sarvajit, Kārttika śu. 15 Monday corresponding to 1047 A.D.,
November 5, Thursday.

inscription.head:
No. 25.
(A.R. No. 81 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar (No. 2) dugout from the main street in the village.

It states that when Chālukya Trailōkyamalladēva was ruling the empire from Paiṭhāṇa, a
new tank was constructed to the west of Suvvipāke by Gangamarāja, Chanḍimayya and Kāḷimayya
seṭṭi. They were granted 60 mattar of land irrigated by this tank by Somi-bhaṭṭāraka, the
sthānapati of Podarupāka which belonged to (god) Teṁkala-Saṁkēśvara of Koḷḷipāke. Rabbarōja
wrote this record.

The top portion of the pillar is broken resulting in loss of some information including
the date where only Śaka 97(2), Vikṛiti is available. This may correspond to 1050-51 A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 26.
(A.R. No. B. 531 of 1961-62.)
BĀGALWĀḌ, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab buried into the bed of the stream.

The record is broken on all sides, hence, much of the information is lost. It seems to
register a grant for a charitable Picoṭṭa by the queen (name lost) of king Trailōkyamalla. It
mentions Bāguḻa paḻa. Probably Bāguḻa-paḻa was the ancient name of the present village Bāgalwāḍ.

In the date portion only Śaka 975 • • • • • 5, Thursday is available. It may correspond to
1053-54 A.D.

l.note: Read vAradandu.

inscription.head:
No. 27.
(A.R. No. B. 116 of 1961-62.)
BŌDHAN, BODHAN TALUK, NIZAMABAD DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar lying in the Inspection Bungalow.

It records that while Chālukya Trailōkyamalladēva was ruling over the empire, perggaḍe
Jōgapayya got reconstructed the temple of god Indra-Nārāyaṇa, consecrated the image of Vishṇu
therein and erected a Garuḍa pillar also. Further, it states that the perggaḍe made several grants
such as two villages-Piriya Māvuḍige and Kiriya Māvuḍige situated in Pūseya-70, house-sites,
oil-mills, a flower garden and income from the toll on salt for the worship of the god and
maintenance of the temple. The grants were made free of all imposts. The temple, it is
stated, was formerly constructed in the capital Bōdhana by the emperor Indra vallabha of the
Rāshṭrakūṭa family. The record was written by Girivajra Nāgadēva.

It is dated Śaka 978, Durmukhi, Jyēshṭha, śu. 13 Thursday corresponding to 1056 A.D., May
29, Wednesday (not Thursday).

inscription.head:
No. 28.
(A.R. No. B. 540 of 1961-62.)
BALLAṬGI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in the field of Saranappa.

It carries an eulogy of Śrī-Chāmaladēvi, the queen of Chālukya Traiḷōkamalladēva and
daughter of Bamma. It states that when the queen was camping at Saṟimale for a holy-bath in
the confluence of the rivers Tuṅgabhadrā and Kṛishṇavēṇi, Bhāskara-Seṭṭi made a land grant in
Bhattagrāma-Baḷḷaniṭṭage situated in Mosaḷikal-300, a kaṁpaṇa of Eḍadoṟe-2000. The measuring rod
Hiriyakōl is mentioned here.

It is dated Śaka 980, Viḷambi, Vaiśākha, śu. 3, Sunday Akshaya-tritiya. It corresponds to
1058 A.D., March 30, Monday.

inscription.head:
No. 29.
(A.R. No. B. 238 of 1962-63.)
PEṆṬA [KHUND], BODHAN TALUK, NIZAMABAD DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On three faces of a slab lying in the field of Gangaram Patel in Survey No. 14.

It records a gift of income from several goods (named) and also of a garden, well and a
mango-grove purchased from Divākara-bhaṭṭa of Bāḷeya, by the ubhayadēsis of Bāḷeya-Sāvaḷigeya-
Pēṇṭhe, to the god Nakharēśvara of the village.

It is dated Śaka 980, Viḷambi, Phālguṇa śu. 1 Monday corresponding to 1059 A.D., February
16. [On the previous day viz., Māgha Amāvāsya, there occurred a solar eclipse].

inscription.head:
No. 30.
(A.R. No. B. 176 of 1962-63.)
ALAMPUR, ALAMPUR TALUK, MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar (No. 3) kept near the navagrahas of the Bālabrahmēśvara temple.

This badly damaged record refers to the rule of Chālukya Trailōkyamalladēva and seems
to register a grant to a deity (name lost).

It is dated Śaka 984 Shubhakrātu, Mārgasira, śu, rest of the information is lost. It may be
equated with 1062 A.D., November.

inscription.head:
No. 31.
(A.R. No. 26 of 1961-62.)
JAṬAPRŌLE, KOLLAPUR TALUK, MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar now lying in front of the Vēṇugōpālasvāmy temple.

It refers to the rule of emperor Trailōkyamalla ruling from the capital Kalyāṇa and to
mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Chiddana-Chōḷa governing Pāḍakal-800, Naḷavāḍi-500, Āyaṁje-300 and Kanne-300.
Also it refers to a royal lady named Mahādēvi who is described as dvitiya-Lakshmī samāne (may
be the wife of mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Chiddana-Chōḷa).

It records a gift of land to Lakuḷēśvara-āchārya, the disciple of Jagadindu-paṇḍita of
Baḷagarānūru. The grant was probably made by Kumārapaya.

The record is a bit damaged and incomplete. It is dated Śaka 985 Śōbhakrit, Phālguṇa, śu.
2 Sunday. It corresponds to 1064 A.D., February 22, Sunday.

inscription.head:
No. 32.
(A.R. No. 82 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar (No. 3) dugout from the main street in the village.

States that when Chālukya Trailōkyamalladēva was ruling the empire from Kalyāṇa,
perggaḍe Kāvaṇayya made a grant of kara-maryāde to Kāḷapa-seṭṭi of Manavakkulakēri in
Koḷḷipāke on the orders of mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara Buddharasa. Also, it refers to the exemption of
some taxes like bilkoḍe etc.

It is dated Śaka 986, Krōdhi, Bhādrapada, śu 13, Thursday. It corresponds to 1064 A.D.,
August 27, f.d.t. 46 Friday.

inscription.head:
No. 33.
(A.R. No. 60 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar in the Museum within the compound of the Sōmēśvara temple [Museum Acc. No. 81].

The record engraved on two faces of the pillar registers a grant of income from the
kiṟudeṟe taxes of the dēvabhōga village Gāḍicheṟuvu situated in Ānemārga-70 for worship and
offerings in the temples of Svayambhu-Sōmēśvara and Nanni nārāyaṇa Vishṇu by mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara
Kōṭeya Bhīmarasa, who bears among others, the epithet mānyakaṭaka-puravarēśvara. The grant was
made when he (the donor) was ruling Koḷḷipāke-7000 while Chālukya Trailōkyamalla was ruling
over the empire.

The record is dated Śaka 989, Plavaṅga, Vaiśākha śu. 5 Sōmavāra. It corresponds to 1067
A.D., April 21. The week day was however, Saturday and not Monday (as stated in the record).

l.note: Stops here abruptly.

inscription.head:
No. 34.
(A.R. No. 61 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the same pillar-Museum Acc. No. 81.

The top portion on the first face (actual III face of the pillar) seems to be a continuation
of the previous record. It begins abruptly. It mentions Koḷapāka as the capital and registers a grant
made on uttarāyaṇa saṁkrānti to the god Svayaṁbhu Sōmēśvara of Koḷapāka by Śrī Trailōkyamalla
Rebbiseṭṭi and the nakara (of the place).

Though engraved separately and being a continuation part this portion of the record can
be ascribed to the date of the previous record (No. 33).

l.note: Stops here abruptly.

inscription.head:
No. 35.
(A.R. No. 93 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALAGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar standing near a stream called Peddavāgu.

Mentions the rule of Chālukya Trilōkyamalladēva who was then ruling the empire
from Samparavāḍi. mahāsāmanta Mēḷarasa was then governing Koḷḷipāke-7000 an administrative
division.

It states that earlier Śaṅkaragaṇḍa of the Rāshṭrakūṭa family had constructed basadis at
Rāvunḍi and Nērila and made grants of some lands to them in Juvvikumṭe and Niduṅgalūru by
issuing a copper-plate charter. Later on these lands were alienated from the basadis by the queen
at the request of mahāsandhivigrahi daṇḍanāyaka Kēsimayya. Subsequently, the record states, that
the land grants were restored to the same basadis by the Karaṇas headed by perggaḍe Appaṇayya
and Muṁnavapura Venkaṭai-seṭṭī on the occasion of a solar eclipse. Perhaps the Jina Bhavana of
Śānti tīrthēśvara, was completely renovated to suit the needs of it being a sacred center. Kēsirāja
is mentioned at the end portion of the record which is damaged. Probably he had some role in
the renovation of the basadi.

It is dated Śaka 989, Plavaṁga, Kārttika, śu. 13 Monday, corresponding to 1067 A.D.,
October 23, f.d.t. 20 [Tuesday].

yumarmmāḍi ·isi cakriyiṁ paḍedā tāṁbra śāsanaṁ sa-

inscription.head:
No. 36.
(A.R. No. B. 598 of 1961-62.)
MĀNVI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying near the Mosque.

This is a damaged record in characters of the 11th century A.D. It refers to the rule of
Trai(lōkyama)lla, the Chālukya king and to his subordinate mahāmaṇḍḷēśvara (name lost) who was
governing Eḍedoṟe-2000. During this time probably tantrapāla Gangara Chāvuṇḍamayya obtained
some lands on his requesting (the king) at [Kalyā]ṇa. The rest is broken and lost.

l.ka: ṇada nelavīḍinōḷ binnapaṁ geydu paḍeya svastyanēka • • • • •

inscription.head:
No. 37.
(A.R. No. B. 614 of 1961-62.)
RAICHUR, RAICHUR TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup in the temple of Rāmalinga.

This is a broken and damaged inscription in characters of about the 11th century A.D. It
contains a eulogistic account of a feudatory ruler by name Handa and his predecessors such as
Lōkabhūpa, Panda, Chanda, Aṇṇaladēva, Bhima, Handiga, Bijja etc.

The date and grant portion are lost. At the beginning the inscription speaks of the rule
of a king who is described as Chakravarti Śri-Rāya-Nārāyaṇa but his name is either not given or
lost. Though of the Kalyāṇa Chālukya period the record contains no royal epithets of that family.

Chālukya king Sōmēśvara I [1043-1068 A.D.] had the title Raja Nārāyaṇa, perhaps Rāya
Nārāyaṇa may be the same as Rāja Nārāyaṇa, on this basis the king can be identified with
Sōmēśvara I.

inscription.head:
No. 38.
(A.R. No. B. 758 of 1962-63.)
TUNGABHADRA DAM, MALLAPURAM TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Two pieces of a slab lying at the entrance of the office of the Asst. Engineer.

This highly damaged record refers to the rule of Chālukya Trailōkyamalla, to an officer
(probably [chā]kaya) administering Kisukāḍu-70 division and to mahāsāmanta, manneya Dojjarasa
[Ajjarasa]. It records gift of land, houses, oil-mill etc., to a deity and for feeding ascetics.

The record is dated 9 • • • • • Mārggaśira, śu. 5, uttarāyaṇa (saṁkrānti). The information
is insufficient for verification. The record is in the characters of the 11th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 39.
(A.R. No. B. 812 of 1962-63.)
ATNŪR, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built in by the side of the steps of the Īśvara temple.

It states that when Chālukya Trailōkyamalladēva was ruling over the empire from
the capital Kalyāṇa mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara Rēvarasa governed Eḍadore-nāḍu-2000 and mahāsāmanta
manneya Nayyaparasa governed the territory (name not mentioned) from his capital Māhishmatīpura.

At this time daṇḍanāyaka Pōtimayya made a grant of some money (amount not specified)
in the presence of Nayyaparasa and Sādhya-Śiva paṇḍitāchārya, the sthānapati of Pallēśvara of
Mudugu[ṁ]dūru. Also the record has registered grants of 2 mattar of land each to Brahmapuri
Chāmuṇḍa-mayya Shaḍangi and a Taḷāṟa.

The record is not dated. But on the basis of the rule of Trailōkyamalla mentioned here
the record can be assigned to the 11th century A.D.

l.ka: svasti samasta bhuvanāśraya śrī pṛthvīvallabha ma-
nibbaru miḻdu svasti samadhigata paṁca ma-

inscription.head:
No. 40.
(A.R. No. B. 815 of 1962-63.)
DODDALA, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying behind the village.

It refers to the reign of Chālukya Bhuvanaikamalladēva [Sōmēśvara II] and to his
feudatory Peḍakalu Jatāchōḷabhīma-mahārāja of Kali kālānvaya, who is eulogized very much with
epithets-Kāvērīvallabha, mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara, Oṟeyūr puravarēśvara etc.

On the date specified another feudatory of the king mahāsamanta Maḷeyamarasa of
Mānuve made grant of 24 mattar of fertile land, one mattar extent flower garden, one
oil-mill and 5 house sites to Girigōṭemalla-Jinalaya built by Mākiseṭṭi at Ponnapāḷu, mahāsāmanta
Maḷeyamarasa is described as māhishmatī puravarēśvara, i.e., lord of the city Māhishmatī and bears
the epithet girigōṭemalla among others.

The record is dated Śaka 991, Saumya, uttarayana sankrānti. It corresponds to 1069 A.D.,
December 24, Thursday.

inscription.head:
No. 41.
(A.R. No. B. 570 of 1961-62.)
KAPGAL, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Broken slab kept on the wall near the Joḍ-Īśvara temple [two pieces].

It records that during the reign of Bhuvanaikamalladēva a grant of land for the temple
of Bhōgēśvaradēva, at Bhattagrama Kappekallu, for the maṭha and for the well, and a oil mill for
perpetual lamp were made by the queen Paṁpādēvi, daughter of Chāmaladēvi, while camping at
Keṟeyūru. Kappekallu is stated to have been situated in Gorikallu-12, a division of Mosaḷikallu-300.

It is dated Śaka 991. Saumya, uttarāyaṇa, saṁkrānti corresponding to 1069 A.D., December
24 Thursday.

inscription.head:
No. 42.
(A.R. No. 57 of 1961-62.)
KOHIR, ZAHEERABAD TALUK, MEDAK DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built into the Vīrasangayya-kaṭṭe.

The epigraph is engraved on four faces of a slab of which the bottom portion is
broken and lost. It seems to register a gift to a basadi constructed by Paḍavaḷa Chāvuṇḍamayya, a
disciple of Śubhachandra-siddhāntadēva. Paṁpa-permānaḍi, by a subordinate of the king Chālukya
Śrī-Bhuvanaikamalla (Sōmēśvara II).

It is dated Śaka 991, Saumya, Phā(lguṇa) and the rest of the information is lost. The given
details correspond to 1070 A.D., February-March.

l.note: The bottom portion of

inscription.head:
No. 43.
(A.R. No. B. 555 of 1961-62.)
HARVI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Broken slab built into the wall of Police-Patel-chāvaḍi.

The left half of the slab is broken and lost. It states that the queen (name lost) of
Chālukya Bhuvanaikamalla made a grant of land for the worship and offerings to the god,
maintenance and repairs (of the temple) and for feeding ascetics in the temple of god
Svayaṁbhu Mūlasthānadēva. The administrative division Eḍadoṟe-nāḍu is mentioned here.

In the date portion only śaka 998 Naḷa. (A)māvāsye, Friday is available. It may
correspond with 1076-77 A.D. [In the month Āśvīja, Amāvāsye occured on a Friday corresponding to
September 30].

edition.h4: TEXT. The Left side of the slab is broken and lost.

inscription.head:
No. 44.
(A.R. No. 54 of 1961-62.)
PAṬANCHERU, SANGAREDDY TALUK, MEDAK DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A broken slab built into the wall of the southern gate of Jama masjid.

The record engraved on two faces of a broken slab contains the genealogy of the Chālukya
kings (of Kalyāṇa) of which Trailokyamalla, Somēśvara II and the latter's younger brother are
mentioned.

Much of the information is lost as the top and sides of the slab are broken and lost. It
is in characters of the 11th century A.D.

l.note: In many

inscription.head:
No. 45.
(A.R. No. 59 of 1961-62.)
KOHIR, ZAHEERABAD TALUK, MEDAK DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built into the wall of the house of Nurul Hassan in Sultānwāḍi.

Records a gift of land to god Beṭṭadadēva by paṭṭasāhaṇi Ādityarasa who is described as
kaṭṭidalagu of (Chālukya) Sōmēśvara [II]. It is in characters of the 11th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 46.
(A.R. No. 84 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar (No. 5) dugout from the main street in the village.

It states that when Chālukya Tribhuvanamalla (Vikramāditya VI) was ruling over the empire
from Kalyāṇa and mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara Śankaragaṇḍa bearing the epithet Lattalūra puravarēśvara,
was governing Koḷḷipāke-7000, the latter made a stipulation that the nagaras [nakharas] of
rājadhāni Koḷḷipāke should pay siddhāya at the rate of one poṇgadyāṇa for each shop per annum.
The capital is stated to be under the jurisdiction of perggaḍe Kāvaṇayya and other karaṇas.

It is dated Śaka 999, Pingaḷa, Bhādrapada, śu 5, Sunday corresponding to 1077 A.D., August
26, Saturday (not Sunday).

inscription.head:
No. 47.
(A.R. No. B. 578 of 1961-62.)
KURAḌI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built into the wall of a house near the temple known as Nelaguḍi.

The record is broken and badly damaged. It records a grant of land by the mahājanas
of agrahāra Rūḍi for the worship and offerings to the god Pallavēśvara. Ayyaṇavāḍi, Bēvinahāḷa
and Kisukāḍu are mentioned here.

It is dated in Tribhuvanamalladēva year 3 Āngīrasa, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 4. Monday. The
information given in the record is wrong, for the year 3 do not suit with the cyclic year Āngīrasa
while keeping in view of the rule of Tribhuvanamalladēva viz., Vīkramāditya VI.

Here either the regnal year or the cyclic year is wrong. Regnal year 3 is Śaka 1000 and
the cyclic year Kāḷāyukta. The given tithi corresponds with 1078 A.D., July 16, Monday. Probably
this may be the intended date of the record. While the tithi in the cyclic year Āngīrasa (Śaka
1014 corresponds with 1092 A.D., July 11, Sunday).

edition.h4:
TEXT. The top and bottom portions of the record are broken and lost. Hence many lines of
the end are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 48.
(A.R. No. B. 766 of 1962-63.)
GORṬA, BHALKI TALUK, BIDAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On three faces of a pillar lying in front of the Hanumān temple.

The top portion of the pillar is broken and lost. It has caused loss of information also. It
states that the king Tribhuvanamalladēva was ruling from his capital Kalyāṇapura. At the time
of the record the king was stationed at the camp Ghaṭṭada-Bōrīhaḷḷa. It was probably a subsidiary
capital for, in the record it is mentioned as bīḍu. Here the king performed several kratus [religious
functions] for his personal merit. At the end the king gave the village Hulicheṟu as dakshiṇā to
his preceptor Rudramiśra. It is stated that the village Hulicheṟu was situated in Bhaluṅke-86, a
kaṁpaṇa of Mēlaṭṭaḷi.

It is said that the preceptor Rudramiśra was the son of Gōvardhana-bhaṭṭa and his wife
Arasikabbe of Vasiśṭha-vaṁśa. The family is eulogized at the beginning of the record. The
record states that Rudramiśra made over the village through a copper-plate grant the village
Hulicheṟu so received from the king as a gift to the god Rudrēśvaradēva installed by him at
agrahāra Gōranṭe for worship, offerings and various services to the god and in the temple and
for feeding and clothing the ascetics, teachers and students there in. This indicates that the temple
was also a centre of education.

Further gifts to the same deity probably made by the authorities of Goranṭe [Gōranṭe
prabhutva] of land, income from taxes like tippesuṅka, an oil mill for the perpetual lamp etc.,
are also registered in the record. The temple servants such as, Kētabōva, the mason and Bhāśōja,
the smith received a house site each.

The date portion is a little damaged. It states • • • • • • • • • • bhi, Kārttika amāvāsye, Tuesday.
If the truncated letter • • • bhi is taken to mean the cyclic year Dundubhi then probably the
given tithi corresponds to 1082 A.D., November 22, Tuesday.

inscription.head:
No. 49.
(A.R. No. 68 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar kept in the compound of Sōmēśvara temple (Museum No. 86-87.)

It records the gift of the village Uddaṁḍige in Ānemārga-70, free from all taxes, for the
repairs to the temple of god Sōmēśvara and for feeding the ascetics therein by mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Toṇvaya Chōḍa mahārāja of Kandūru, who was administering Kollipāke-7000 division. The king
Tribhuvanamalladēva is stated to have been ruling over the empire from the capital Kalyāṇa. The
record, engraved on different pillars is now broken into pieces.

It is dated in Chālukya Vikrama year 12, Prabhava, uttarāyaṇa sankrānti. It corresponds to
1087 A.D., December 25, [Saturday]. The Śaka year was 1009.

inscription.head:
No. 50.
(A.R. No. B. 189 of 1962-63.)
MADDŪRU, ALAMPUR TALUK, MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in the ruined Fort.

The top portion of the slab is broken and lost. Writing on the right side of the slab is
also badly damaged. The record belongs to the reign period of Chālukya Tribhuvanamalladēva.

It registers a gift of income from taxes for maintaining a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Chintaka Rēchēśvara of Maddūru by Viṭṭapayya-Billamayya, the kēṇiverggaḍe of vaḍḍarāvuḷa and
biḷkoḍe (taxes), Rakkasayya Māchirāja, the perggaḍe of perjjuṁka, Chaṭṭimayya and others at the
instance of the vaḍḍarāvuḷa daṇḍanāyaka Mahēśvarayya when daṇḍanāyaka Rudra-bhaṭṭōpādhyāya
was governing over Ayje-300 division.

The record is dated in the Chālukya Vikrama year 12, Prabhava, uttarāyaṇa saṁkrānti. It
corresponds to 1087 A.D., December 25 [Saturday].

l.ka: ma varṣada 12 neya prabhava saṁvatsarada yu[tta]

inscription.head:
No. 51.
(A.R. No. B. 191 of 1962-63.)
MADDURU, ALAMPUR TALUK, MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Another slab lying in the ruined Fort.

The top portion of the slab in broken and lost. Perhaps this lost portion contained
reference to the rule of Chālukya Tribhuvanamalladēva [Vikramāditya VI].

It records the gift of some plots of land mesasured by the Satya ratnākarana kōlu and
made tax free in Maddūru and Rēkalūru to the god Chintaka-Rēchēśvara of rājadhāni Maddūru
situated on the southern banks of the Tuṅgabhadrā in Ayje-300, for worship of the god,
maintenance of the musicians and running a choultry by mahāsāmanta Pallavarasa of Puṇḍūr in
the presence of Sōvarasa, Nāgimayya-nāyaka and the pradhānas. The donor bears such epithets as
Muduguṇḍūr puravarēśvara, Alampura-bhujaṁga, etc. Also it contains another gift of land for the
repairs of the temple, annadana etc.

The date of the first grant is Chālukya Vikrama year 13, Vibhava, Āśhādha, Lunar eclipse
Thursday, corresponding to 1088 A.D., July 6, Thursday. Lunar eclipse occured on that day.

l.note: The slab is broken at the beginning and lost.
The end is broken and lost.
Originally engraved as 15 and then corrected into 20.

inscription.head:
No. 52.
(A.R. No. 94 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALAGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in front of the Hanuman temple in the village.

Mentions the rule of Chālukya Tribhuvanamalla ruling the empire from the capital
Kalyāṇa. It records the gift of the village Śandularahaḷu in Kōḍada-12 as taḷavṛitti to Ramēśvara-
Paṇḍita, the disciple of Tējōnidhi-Paṇḍita of the Kāḷāmukha sect for worship and offerings, feeding
the students and maintaining a satra [choultry] in the temple of Uttarēśvara by Toṇḍaya-Chola-
mahārāja of Kandūru who was governing Koḷḷipāke-7000. Further gifts of money made by several
officials are also recorded. Kanbeṇa of the maṭha wrote the record.

It is dated in Chālukya Vikrama year 13, Vibhava, uttarāyaṇa saṅkrānti. It corresponds to
1088 A.D., December 24, [Sunday].

inscription.head:
No. 53.
(A.R. No. 69 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On broken pieces kept in the Museum Acc. No. 86-87.

It records the gift of a village (name lost) included in Koṁḍada-12 for worship and
offerings to god Sōmēśvara by the chief Tonḍaya Chōḍa-mahārāja of Kandūru, who was ruling
over Kollipāke-7000. Chālukya Tribhuvanamalla is stated to have been ruling over the empire
from the capital Kalyāṇa. Kandeṇa was the composer of this record.

The record is dated in the Chālukya Vikrama year 16, Prajāpati, solar eclipse. The intended
tithi may correspond to 1091 A.D., May 21 [Wednesday] f.d.t. 18. The only solar eclipse of the year
occured on this day. The corresponding month and tithi were Vaiśākha, amāvāsye.

inscription.head:
No. 54.
(A.R. No. 70 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On another pillar at the same place Museum Acc. No. 88.

It states that when Chālukya Tribhuvanamalladēva was ruling over the empire from
his capital Kalyāṇa, mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kandūra Toṅḍaya-Chōḍa mahārāja made a gift of the
village Rācheṟuvu situated in Koḍada-12, for worship and offerings to god Vishṇudēva free of all
imposts. The record describes the donor with a string of birudas and states that he was
administering over Koḷḷipāka-7000.

The record is dated in Chālukya Vikrama year 16, Prajāpati, [Śaka 1013], uttarāyaṇa
saṁkrānti. It corresponds to 1091 A.D., December 25, [Thursday].

There is no difference in script except the size.

There is no difference in script except the size.

inscription.head:
No. 55.
(A.R. No. B. 530 of 1961-62.)
BĀGALWĀḌ, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Two pieces of a stone lying near the temple of Banagarda Basavaṇṇa.

It records a grant of land excluding the taxes mūligardeṟe and uṁbaḷi to the prabhus and
gāvuṇḍas of Bammaḻahāṉa by mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Ayyaṇādēva of the Haihaya lineage, a feudatory
of the king Chālukya Vīkramāditya VI. Ayyaṇadēva is described as the servant of the queen
Malayamatidēvi.

It is dated in Chālukya Vikrama year 20, Yuva, Pushya śu. 13. Thursday. It corresponds
to 1095 A.D., December 12, Wednesday, f.d.t. 05.

l.ka: svastyanēka bhaṁgura taraṁga saṁghauta vīcicchāya lavaṇābdavaḷaya vaḷayta

inscription.head:
No. 56.
(A.R. No. B. 566 of 1961-62.)
KALLUR, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built into another wall inside the Māratēśvara temple.

It states that when the king Tribhuvanamalla was in his military camp on the bank of the
river Narmada his preceptor Vikramāditya-bhattōpādhyāya Sarvakratu Sōmayājin, who is described
as a daṇḍanāyaka, received from the king the village Kallūru as dakshiṇā on performing the
Paṅchalāñgūla-kratu and he, in his turn, made over the village to the brahamaṇas in the presence
of the king.

On the same occasion mahāmaṇḍḷēśvara Chandarasa of the Haihaya family also associated
himself in the act of making gift of the village Kallūru to the brahmaṇas and made some other
gifts such as 100 mattar of land measured by the rod called the Māṇikēśvaradakōlu, house sites,
oil-mills etc, to the god Svayambhū-Maraṭēśvara of Kallūru.

Also, it records a grant of land made on the same occasion by Vāmarasa, the
brother-in-law of the royal preceptor to the same deity of Kallūru. The record has profusely
eulogized the royal preceptor and mahāmaṇḍaleśvara Chandarasa of the Haihaya family. Also the
record mentions god Gavaṟēśvara.

The record is dated in the Chālukya Vikrama year 21. Dhātu, Chaitra śu. 1, Sunday
corresponding to 1096 A.D., February 27. The weekday was Wednesday, f.d.t. 45.

l.ka: svasti samasta bhuvanāśraya śrī pṛthvīvallabha mahārā-
ḷatiḷakaṁ cāḷukyābharaṇaṁ śrī matribhuvanamalladēvara vijaya rā-
re narmmadādēviya daṇḍina naḍevīḍinalu sukhasaṁkathā vi-
yuktaṁ gurudēva bhaktaṁ kritavidyā vicāraṁ maṁgaḷācāraṁtyaga cintā-
maṇi vidvaccūḍāmaṇi ripunikara kṛtānta yaśōdhavaḷita diganta da-
ṇḍanāyakaṁ mauḷi māṇikya nīti cāṇākya dvijakuḷa kamaḷanī divā-
kara saujanya ratnākara kāvya cāmīkara nikaḷōphaḷatyūrjata nija bhu-
ja lālita lakṣmīcāpaḷa haracaraṇa sarasīruha nabhinava ka-
lva bhūruha turaṁga vidyānaḷa raṇaraṁga dāvānaḷa rūpādharīkritama
karakētanaṁ vijaya lakṣmī kētanaṁ nāmādi samasta praśasti sahitaṁ śrīma-
śrī maccāḷukya vikrama varṣada 21 neya dhātu saṁvatsarada cai-
yyalu śrī narmmadādēviyaddhōdakadalu paṁcalāṁgūla kratu dakṣiṇe
kallūraṁ dhārāpūrvvakaṁ paḍedu ·ā cakravartiya sannidhānadalu svastyanē-
ka bhaṁgura taraṁga saṁghāta vībīnicaya lavaṇābdhavaḷa yavaḷaita
vēḷāvanaṁga pāriyātra tanmādhya dēśādhipati sahasrabāhu pratāpa
bhūpa diḷīpanahihaya vaṁśōdbhavanatyugra naraśaradarphaṇa dvajavirāja vīrā
jamāna dāna kānīna mūlapaṟe ghōśaṇa nriparatnabhūṣaṇa satyavikhyāta nī-
ti māndhāta budhajana stutya nayyanayyaṇāditya nāmādi samasta praśasti sahi-
taṁ ś®(śrī) manmahāmaṇḍaḷēśvaraṁ caṁdarasaruṁ | ·āvūraṁ brāhmaṇarige sarvvanamaśya-
vāgi kuḍuttuṁ vāḷikāladalu ·āvūra śrī svayaṁbhu māraṭēśvaradēva
nisakkaṁ biṭṭa vṛttiyaydakkaṁ gobbūra dāriyiṁ mūḍalu māṇikēśvarada kōla-
lu mattaru nūṟu hiriyakeṟeya baḍagagi kōḍiya keḷage tōṁ-
ṭa mattareraḍu dēvariṁ teṁkaṇakēri ·ondu gavaṟēśvara dīśānyadalu baṇaṁjeya nivēśa-
ṇanavaydu nandādīvigege gāṇaveraḍu ·aṁgaḍiyondu || śrī madayyaṁgaḷa ma-
yduna vāmarasaṁ tannavṛttiyoḷage khaṁḍyake biṭṭa mattaraydu
kaṁbavaṟunūṟu || © sāmānyōyaṁ dharmasētu nripāṇāṁ | kālē kā-

inscription.head:
No. 57.
(A.R. No. B. 785 of 1962-63.)
HARSŪR, GULBARGA TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying on to the left side [outside] the Parvatadēva temple.

It belongs to the reign period of Tribhuvanamalladēva and the record carries his
genealogy. Somēśvara III, his son Perma-Jagadēkamalla and Taila II [brother of Somēśvara III] are
also mentioned in the record.

The inscription also has mentioned Bammaṇayya, the feudatory chief of Jagadēkamalla,
his wife Lakkādēvi and Hemmāḍidēva (their son). The record intends to highlight the works and
greatness of the family of Bhāskara-seṭṭi and Sāgara-seṭṭi. Some information regarding this cannot
be made out properly since the record is damaged. There seems to be a reference to Kēdārēśvara
and Bankēśa.

It has registered a grant of land for the worship and offerings to god Bāḷēśvara by
Goṅkarasa of Goṅka-nāḍu Nāmarasa, the prabhu of Sīmaḷa, and Kēti-seṭṭi, a jewel of the Vaiśya-
kula. The hiriya daṇḍanāyaka Baṁmaṇayya, his son by wife Lakkhā-dēvi, Hemmāḍi-arasa and the
mahājanas [of Sīmaḷa] were entrusted with the duties and responsibilities of managing this dharma.
The temple of Bāḷeśvara was constructed in the place Sāleya-Sīmaḷa. It is said that this place was
situated in Goṅka-nāḍu, a sub-division of Alande-1000 in Kuntaḷa-Janapada. The record written by
Mallōja.

This record is dated in the regnal year 21 of Chālukya Tribhuvanamalla, Īśvara, Pushya
ba. 3, Thursday, uttarāyaṇa saṅkrānti. It corresponds to 1097 A.D., December 24, Thursday, f.d.t. 33.

inscription.head:
No. 58.
(A.R. No. 63 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the same pillar (No. 25), Museum No. 82.

It states that during the reign of Chālukya Tribhuvanamalla hērisandhivigrahi daṇḍanāyaka
Chiddarasa while administering Kollipāke-7000 made a gift of a village Pākuguṇte situated in
Kōḍada-12 free of all taxes to the god Svayambhu Sōmēśvara. Also it records a gift of a house
for a perpetual lamp to the same god by a certain Sōmarasa.

This record engraved on the other two faces of the same pillar (No. 25) is dated in the
Chālukya Vikrama year 22, Īśvara, Pushya, ba. 5 Thursday, uttarāyaṇa saṁkrānti. It corresponds to
1097 A.D., December 26, Saturday (not Thursday). There was no saṁkrānti on that day.

l.note: Stops here.

edition.p:
[Note: Only the second face of this inscription has been published in the Kannada Inscription of
A.P.G. Arch. series No. 3-Ng-47, p. 62-63].

inscription.head:
No. 59.
(A.R. No. B. 781 of 1962-63.)
DASTĀPUR, GULBARGA TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup in front of the Hanuman temple.

The first portion of this record [about 17 lines] is completely damaged and even the next
portion is also damaged. But some information can be culled out from the extant portion of
engraving. It records a gift of 55 mattar land, an oil mill for burning perpetual lamp, 6 mattar
land for flower garden and an additional 2 mattar land made probably by Vaṁṇēśvarāji. Probably
the gift was made to a temple the information of which is lost.

This portion of the record is dated in Chālukya Vikrama year 24, Pramādi, Kārttika, śu.
12 Thursday. It corresponds with 1099 A.D., October 28 [Friday] f.d.t. 77.

inscription.head:
No. 60.
(A.R. No. 89 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALAGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built into the wall of the main shrine of the Viranārāyaṇasvāmi temple, proper left.

This epigraph is in Kannaḍa script and Sanskrit language mentions the rule of Chālukya
Tribhuvanamalla-vallabhēṁdra ruling the empire from Kalyāṇapura.

The record gives a lengthy description of the Arbuda mountain and an account of the
Paramāra rulers down to Jagadēva who on the orders of the king granted the villages included
in kollipāka-dēśa excluding from it, the lands which were already given to the god Auppēśvara,
for the anga-ranga-bhōgha of the god, the upkeep of the temple and the feeding of the ascetics
at Jagaddēva-nārāyaṇapura village built by Sōmala-daṇḍanāyaka. Village Piriya Pembarati situated
within Dvādaruśa village is mentioned as grant village.

It also records in addition the grant of uttamagaṇḍachinhas at the rate of five, four and
three from all the villages of Koḷḷipāke-7000, classified into three grades as uttama, madhyama
and adhama respectively and some lands in Niḍuṅgalūru and another place (name not clear) to
the west of Jagadēva-taṭāka for certain other services such as supply of garlands, fruits etc. As
boundary marks the following villages situated respectively in the east, west, south and north are
mentioned-Chiduku Cheṟuvu, Duddē-Chēṟuvu, Eṟṟiṁgunṭa and Mūrāvi. Also, there is a mention
of Rāmalacheṟuvu as Suvarṇa bhaṇḍāgāra of the goddess Śrī-Majjagadēva Nārāyaṇadēvi.

It is dated in Chālukya Vikrama year 29, Tāraṇa, Chaitra lunar eclipse, Sunday. It
corresponds to 1104 A.D., March 13, Sunday. Lunar eclipse occured on that day. The Śaka year
was 1026.

l.ka:
śrī nāthasya mahāvarāha vapuṣōlīlāgra daṁṣṭrō dhṛtā dhātrī yasya vibhāti kētaka

| bhaktyā prajvalayanti yatra satataṁ vidvēśiṇaḥ prāṇino mitrāṇyēva viśiṣṭa saṁgati matāṁ

syēva saṁgrahītaṁ virāja tē || • • || ·adhīśvarastasya viśiṣṭa nāmā munīśvarō divya
munīṁdra sēvyaḥ samasta vēdārttha vicāra dakṣaḥ sākṣācvaturvvaktra (i)va dvitīyaḥ || • • ||
dharmādharma vicāra pāraga mataryyāga prayōga prabhu rmmōkṣāpēkṣita cētasāṁ
sukṛti nāṁ nirvāṇa dīkṣāguruḥ | ·ātmātarnni ||

yamā svahasyā viṣaya vyāvṛtta paṁcēṁdriyaḥ sarvva prāṇihitaḥ samādhi vibhava jñāta
trikāla sthitiḥ || ·avighnatāṁ sādhayituṁ samarttha dharmma kriyāṇāṁ muni puṁgavānāṁ
|| dharmanelē naiṣanṛpāla mēkaṁ lōkatra brājita tūṇa yugmaṁ kōdaṁḍa vīrāja
mānaṁ pinākinaṁ burvvata śā ||

travāṇāṁ puratrayaṁ dugdumiva prabhūtaṁ || • • || savahni kuṁḍāduditō mahīpatiḥ
pravarttamānaḥ paramāraṇī para(ḥ) | rarāja labdā paramāra saujña manvartthā bhūtāṁ
munipuṁga vēbhyaḥ || • • || lakṣmīśvara sthira padādavilaṁghya simnāstasyanvayā tsakala sadguṇa
ratna rāśēḥ [|*] kṣīrārṇnavādiva sudhādyutirā virāsīt śrī harṣadēva ||

nṛpati rnṛparū caṁdraḥ || • • || namanmahīpāla kirīṭa nūtna ratna prabhālaṁkṛta pāda
pīṭhaḥ | digaṁta viśrāṁta viśuddha kīrttiḥ parākramālaṁkṛta divya mūrttiḥ ||
acchannerhōmadānairbbahuvidha vidhibhistarppayandīva viprānkōśaṁ svarṇnēna puṣṇanjagadupakṛta
yē dharmmamārggurjjitēna | ·ātmīyābhiḥ sa-

masta prakṛti bhīrucitaṁ nirvviśansarvvabhōgānēvaṁ dharmmārttha kāmairiha parasukhakaṁ
prājyā rājyaṁ cakāra || • • || tasmiṁścaturttha puruṣārttha sukhapravṛttā śrīharṣadēva nṛpatau
tanaya stadiyaḥ | muṁjō jagadvinuta sadguṇa ratna puṁjōna bhūmērbbabhūva patirarṇnava
mēkhalāyaḥ || 28* || līlāṁ rājyasya vairavyatika ||

ra rahitāṁ sādhubhiḥ sādhu dhṛtvā kṛtvādhārabhidānaṁ puramamara harasparddhi lakṣmī samṛddhyā
| nāmnasi labdhvānvitārtthē kavijana nivahādvākpatitō tpalitvējātaḥ śrī maṁjadēvaḥ
kṣitipatitilakaḥ kīrttilakṣmī nivāsaḥ || 29* || tatastasya bhrātā nijabhuja balōpārjjita(ṁ)
balō bilvādi dviṣṭānāṁ balama laghu saṁhṛtya bali ||

nāṁ cakārorvvī mājña praguṇita nijacāra nirata prajāṁ sarvvāmādyā kṣitipa caritaḥ si(ṁ)
dala nṛpaḥ || • • || sūnu stasya parākrama praṇayinō bhōjacchalēna svayaṁ jātō
madhyama pāṁḍavaḥ kimaparaṁtadbhū patērvvarṇnanaṁ | nirvvākī kṛta cakravartti vibhavaḥ sāhitya
vidyāgu ||

ruḥ satyācāra vicāra vikrama khaṇiḥ śiṣṭēṣṭa ciṁtāmaṇiḥ || • • || ·āścaryya janmaśaraṇaṁ
dharaṇīśa vaṁdyaḥ sauṁdharyya saṁpada dharīkṛta puḥ pacāpaḥ kōpāgni tāpa bhasitī kṛta
vairirājō bhōjaḥ kavīśvara manōratha kalpa bhūjaḥ || • • || svajana sujana sēvayastasya
bhavyaḥ pitṛvyaḥ parihṛta parabhauma styāga ||

bhōgābhirāmaḥ [|*] nayavinaya vilāsaḥ śauryyalakṣmī nivāsaḥ śunavibhava viśālō
gōṁḍala kṣōṇēpālaḥ || • • || tadbhūpālo dayādrērnnija rucinica yadhvasta dōṣākara
śrīrānaṁdā vāpti hētuḥ pratidina muditō mitra paṁkēru hāṇāṁ līlā padmābhirāmaḥ
paranṛpati miradhvaṁpakaḥ sarvvalōka vyāpārōtsāha saṁpatpra ||

dagati rudayāditya dhātrī talēśaḥ || • • || citramaurvvaḥ paviddāvaḥ śatru durggatrayakṣayē
| ·ēkōvya bhūtpratāpāgni rudayāditya saṁbhavaḥ || tējaḥ sīmūrttīva virājamānanasta
syātmajātaḥ pratipannakarṇnaḥ | ·anūna dānādi guṇa pravṛddhō rāja jagaddēva ·iti prasiddhaḥ ||
• • || śiṣṭēṣṭābhi matā mura sthirata ||

rāṁ kurvvanramāṁ bhārati māsyāṁbhōjaratāṁ hitāṁ sukṛtināṁ dōrvvaścavīra
śrīyaṁ | dhātrī rakṣaṇa dakṣiṇāmapi jagaddēva kṣamāpāla kaścitraṁ bhāti diśāsthi kīrtti lalanā
sārtthaikaniṣṭhā paraḥ || dānāṁcu śōbhita karōdhṛta bhadra cihnaḥ bharōdhvahana kēli samarttha
mūrttiḥ | prakhyāta vaṁśa vibhavō vibhudā ||

bhivṛddhihētu ssuradviṣāṁca pratipanna karṇnaḥ || kalyāṇa mūrttiravanīdhara mukhya bhūtaḥ
pādāśrītākhila dharō vibhudādi vāsaḥ | ·atyunnataḥ sthirataraḥ sthiti hētururvyāṁ
hēmacalēśa ·iva kuṁtala dhāriṇīśaḥ || hēḷōtkhāta niśāta khaḍgadalitānkṛtvā jagakkaṁṭakāndhṛtvā
vakṣasiniścalaṁ priyatamāṁ sā ||

mrājya rājya śrīyaṁ bhītāyātavinīta bhūtala patīndatvā bhayaṁ pālayandēvaḥ kuṁtala
vallabhō vijayatē viśrāṁta cakrēśvaraḥ || • • || saḥ śrī cāḷukya rāmaḥ sakala nṛpaśirō
maṁḍanābhyarccitāṁghriḥ snēhānānīya dhārā puri vibhu vibhavā nirvviśaṁtaṁ vasaṁtaṁ
datvārddhaṁ kuṁtalō rvyābhiḥ pitṛjana caritaiḥ pu ||

travanmānayaṁstaṁ lōkā lōkādri sīmāvanipatirabhavattatkumāra pratāpāt || • •
|| tasminsamasta bhuvanāśraya śrī pṛthvīvallabha mahārājādhi rāja paramēśvara parama bhaṭṭāraka
satyāśraya kulatilaka cālukyābharaṇa śrīmatribhuvanamalla vallabhēṁdra kalyāṇapura nivēśita
nijavijaya skaṁdhavārē sukhēnarājya ||

sukhamanubhavati || dēśēśrī pāriyātrē mahiṣapuravarē yaḥ sadādhāra bhūtaḥ khyātaḥ
sthānaṁ guṇānāṁ sa mahiṣa puravāḷānvayaḥ sīhanāmālēbhē putraṁ pavitraṁ parahita
nirataṁ sāḍigākhyaṁ samākhyaṁ tatsūnuḥ sōmalaḥkṣmā vinuta sitayaśaḥ śrī vadhū
prāṇanāthaḥ || tyāgī satkavi baṁdhupōṣaṇa ||

rataḥ satyavratālaṁkṛtirddūrī bhūta parōpa tāpinacanō vikrāṁta lakṣmīpriyaḥ |
ātmasvāmya vidhēya vairi lalanā vaidhavya dīkṣāgururbbhāsvaccaṁdra kalāvataṁsa caraṇa
dhyānāmalaḥ sōmalaḥ || • • || ·aninditācāra vicārasāraḥ parōpakārādi guṇāvatāraḥ
vidvajjanādhāra ramā sa nāthō virājatē ||

sōmala daṇḍanāthaḥ || ·avāsa saṁvāsita kāmadhēnuḥ sadmāṁgaṇā rōpita kalpav®kṣaḥ
nidhigg®hābhyaṁtara saṁgahītaḥ khyātaḥ satāṁ sōmaladaṇḍanāthaḥ || yēna mahātmanā ||
sōmēśvarērkagraha māt® kāṇāṁ viṣṇōḥ pratiṣṭhāna karēśvarasyā || buddhasya kālēśvara
saṁnidhānē k®tōttarē śāna maṭhēcavācaḥ || tīnasama ||

sta rājya bhara nirūpita mahāmātya padavi vīrājamāna mānōṁnnata prabhu maṁtrōtsāha
śaktitraya saṁpaṁnēna śrīmanmahāsaṁdhivigrahi mahāpracaṇḍa daṇḍanāyaka sōmalēna nija
pādōpa jīvināṁ nijarājyādhiṣṭhāne kollipāka nagarē nija rājyānvaya śrēyasē
(dattāṁ) nirmāpita jagaddēva nārāyaṇa pu ||

rasya khaṇḍasphuṭitānava sudhākarmma nirmāṇarttha dēvāṁgabhōga raṁgabhōgārttha munijana
svādhyāyāṁna dānārttha śrī maccālukya vikrama varṣa ·ēkōnatriṁśattamē tāraṇa saṁvatsarē
caitra pūrṇamāyā mādityavārē sōmagrahaṇa nimittaṁ paṭṭana pratibaddhadvādaruśa grāma
madhye piriya peṁbaṟiti nāmagrāmaṁ pū-||

rvvaṁ puradharmmaṁ vicita bhattabhāgē bhāgadvaya mappēśvara dēvāyadattaṁ parihṛtya
sarvvabhōgābhyaṁtara śuddhyādēvabhōgī kṛtya sarvvanamasya cālukya cakrēśvara
tribhuvanamalla narēśvarājñātaḥ jagaddēva maṇḍalēśvarō dattavān || grāmasya ghāṭakaḥ pūrva
cidukuceṟuvu kaṁṭṭekṛndi vrayadāna | paścimataḥ duddēceṟuvu | daṁkṣiṇataḥ

·eṟiṟaṁguṁṭa ·uttarataḥ kaḍita mūrāvi | kollipāka sapta sahasra madhye pratigrāma muttama
madhyamādhama kramēṇa paṁca catvāritriṇyuttamagaṇḍa ciṁnāni dēva bhōgikṛtya dattavān |
tadaiva paṭṭanē gāṇa vādili hūḍḍipu dēvamānaṁ cavuṭuṁ pratigṛhē mālākāriṣu puḥ
pasūtrayaṁ ca dattavān ||

dēvasyapuḥ papātra phalāvalyartthaṁ niḍuṁgalūrē • • • • • vāṭikā kṣētaṁ dattavān ||
ēvame tatsarvvaṁ svadharmma nirvviśēṣaṁ śēṣairnnara patibhiḥ dēśādhikāribhiśca paripālanīyaṁ
|| bahubhirvvasudhā datta rājabhiḥ sagarādibhiḥ yasyayasya yadābhūmi stasya tasya tadā
phalaṁ || svadattāṁ paradattāṁ vāyō harēti vasuṁ-||

dharāṁ ṣaṣṭiṁ varṣasahasrāṇi viṣṭāyāṁ jāyatē krimiḥ || tathā rāmacaṁdrēṇāpyuktaṁ
| sāmānyōyaṁ dharmasētu nṛpāṇāṁ kālē kālē pālaniyō bhavadbhiḥ (|)
sarvānētānbhāvinaḥ pārtthivēṁdrānbhūyō bhūyō yācate rāmacaṁdraḥ || madvaṁśajāḥ
para mahīpati vaṁśajā vāpāpāda pētta manasō bhuvi bhāvibhūpāḥ ||

yē pālayanti mama dharma miduṁ samastaṁ tēṣārmmayā vīra citāṁjali śēṣa
mūrdhni || śatruṇāpi kṛtōdharmmaḥ pālanīyaḥ prayatnataḥ śatrurēvahi śatrussyāddharmmaḥ
śatrurnna kasyacitaḥ || ·anyaśca śrī majjagadēva nārāyaṇa dēviya bhāṇḍāgāra
suvarṇnāgārāmalaceṟuvu ||

gāmē badda jagaddēva mahōdadi nāmataḍāga paścādbhāgē śālikṣētra madhyē nivartana
daśakamasya dēvasya sarvanamasyā māṁcaṁ[drā*]rkkasthāyi | ·iti śubhaṁ maṁgaḷa mahāśrī
śrī prati • • • • • natra caravaṁ bhairavīta vyajñā ||

inscription.head:
No. 61.
(A.R. No. B. 511 of 1961-62.)
BHAṄKUR, CHITAPUR TALUK, GULBARGA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in the ruined temple of Īśvara outside the village.

The record is worn out in parts. It mentions yuvaraja Mallikārjunadēva as the aṇuga
of the Paṭṭamahādēvi. It registers a gift of land and garden to the god Māṇikēśvara of
Bammukūru. Also, it mentions maṇḍalēśvara Nāgarasa. It refers to a measuring rod probably
named after the god Māṇikēśvara.

It is dated in Chālukya Vikrama year 29, Tārana, Bhādrapada śu. 13, Sunday corresponding
to 1104 A.D., September 4, Sunday f.d.t. 71.

l.ka: svasti samasta bhuvana vikhyātaṁ cāḷukyānvaya prasū • • • • • • • • • •
maraṁka bhīmaṁ nayanābhirāmaṁ ripurāya ku • • • • • je
raṁ vīra vidyādharaṁ sakaḷa kaḷādharaṁ padma • • giri vajra
paṭṭa mahādēviyaraṇugaṁ nāmādi samasta prasasti sa-

inscription.head:
No. 62.
(A.R. No. B. 548 of 1961-62.)
BALLAṬGI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the same slab [No. 134] left side.

It records a grant of land to Hollimayya and Mallayya at Baḷḷaniṭṭage by Chandaladēvi,
the queen of Vikramāditya VI. It refers to the measuring rod piriyakōlu.

It is dated in Chālukya Vikrama year 30, Vyaya Pushya ba. 13, Tuesday, uttarāyaṇa-
sankrānti corresponding to 1106 A.D., December 25, Tuesday, f.d.t. 05.

inscription.head:
No. 63.
(A.R. No. B. 745 of 1962-63.)
ANDUR, TULJAPUR TALUK, OSMANABAD DISTRICT, MAHARASHTRA.
inscription.p: Slab fixed into a dilapidated wall in the village.

It records a gift of the village Beṭṭakeṟeyur for worship, offerings and burning lamps
in the temple of Māṇikēśvara of Aṇādūr by yuvarāja Mallikārjunadēva at the instance of
mahāpradhāna maneverggaḍe daṇḍanāyaka Nāchimarasa when the emperor Tribhuvanamalladēva
was ruling the empire from the capital Kalyāṇa. The nāḍa-adhikaris [officers of the Divison] were
made responsible for protecting and maintaining this dharma. Also it records a grant of 42 mattar
land which includes 12 mattar land situated in Sāntayanāyakana kēri, to the god Māṇikēśvara by
Bāḷeya-seṭṭi.

At the instance of Anantapāḷa daṇḍanāyaka the tax chchhatra, chchhāya suṅka was gifted
(to the god) by suṅkaverggaḍe Sōmanāthayya-nāyaka and Dāsimarasa. Further at the instance of
yuvaraja vallabha Mallikārjuna income from taxes on various commodities was granted by
suṅkaverggaḍe Sōbhana-nāyaka of Āṇadūr-300 and others. The Telligas [oil-pressers] also granted
oil for burning perpetual lamp and for Chaitra[māsa] celebrations. The three Seṭṭis and the
nāḍaadhikari were made responsible for the protection and maintenance of all these grants to the
temple.

As post script another grant is also recorded which states that Māgarasa of Moraṁba made
a grant of 100 mattar land for special offerings to god in the months of Chaitra, Śrāvaṇa and
Dīpāvaḷi celebrations.

It is dated in Chālukya Vikrama year 32, (Śaka 1029) Sarvajitu, Margaśira, amāvāsye,
Sunday, Chūḍāmaṇi grahaṇa corresponding to 1107 A.D., December 16. The weekday was however,
Monday on which day there was a Solar eclipse.

inscription.head:
No. 64.
(A.R. No. 91 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALAGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab fixed into the proper right side wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the main shrine of the
Vīranārāyaṇasvāmī temple.

It states that when Chālukya Tribhuvanamalladēva was ruling the empire from the
capital Kalyāṇapura, mahāpradhāna bāṇasaverggaḍe Anantapāla-daṇḍanāyaka made a gift of
income derived from the taxes vaṭṭapārikāya and kāvalideṟe of the village Piriya-Pembaṟiti
belonging to the temple and situated in Hoḷalu-12 for worship and offerings to the god Jagaddēva-
nārāyaṇa.

The record is dated in Chālukya Vikrama year 31, Sarvajit, Pushya, śu. 9, Wednesday,
uttarāyaṇa saṁkrānti. It corresponds to 1107 A.D., December 25, Wednesday.

l.ka: yakaṁ sujanaprasannaṁ gōtra pavitraṁ parāṁganā putra tura-
dēvariṁgāḷva hoḷalu haṁneraḍaṟa baḷiya piriya poṁba
maṁ dēvara bhōgakke dhārāpūrvva māḍi koṭṭarīdha-

l.note: Figures of Sun and Moon in line 22 and Nandi in 23 are carved.

inscription.head:
No. 65.
(A.R. No. 62 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Second pillar in the Museum (Museum No. 82.)

It registers a gift of income from pārikhāya tax (?) collected from the villages belonging
to the temple of Sōmēśvara for burning a perpetual lamp there in by Gōpatayayya, the pergaḍe
of mahāpradhāna bāṇasaverggaḍe daṇḍanāyaka Anantapālayya when Chālukya Tribhuvanamalla
was ruling from Kalyāṇapura. It also registers another gift (of rice) by the Seṭṭis and the nakharas
of Koḷḷipāke.

It is dated in Chālukya Vikrama year 43 [wrong for 33], (Śaka 1030) Sarvadhāri, Āshāḍha
śu. 1, Thursday, Solar eclipse. It corresponds to 1108 A.D., June 12, f.d.t. 10 [Friday]. Solar eclipse
occured on the previous day [Thursday].

inscription.head:
No. 66.
(A.R. No. 65 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Another pillar in the Sōmēśvara temple, Museum No. 84.

It gives a genealogical account of the Chālukyas up to Tribhuvanamalla (Vikramāditya VI).
It records the gift of land including the one-third portion of the royal dues from the taḷavritti
formerly granted to the Cheniṁche (?) basadi in agrahāra Tānḍipāmu-lanka which was a
prabhutva agrahāra of Sōmanātha-bhaṭṭa and was included in Kalavachēḍu-40, to god
Sōmēśvara under the orders of the king [Tribhuvanamalla] and at the request of mahāpradhāna
antahpura vergaḍe Kāḷimayya and his younger brother Tikkapayya who was the daṇḍanāyaka of
Koḷḷipāke-7000.

Prince Sōmēśvara is stated to have been ruling over several territories including Kollipāke-
nāḍu. Also, the king Tribhuvanamalladēva is stated to have been ruling over the empire from
Mānyakeṟe camp.

It is dated in the Chālukya Vikrama year 34, Vīrodhi, Śaka 1031, vishusankrānti. It
corresponds to 1109 A.D., March 24 [Wednesday], f.d.t. 01.

inscription.head:
No. 67.
(A.R. No. 85 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar (No. 6) dugout from the main street in the village.

Mentions the rule of Chālukya Tribhuvanamalla and states that prince Sōmēśvara made a
gift of 12 mattar of wet land to Āditya-bhaṭṭa Sōmayāji on the occasion of offering mahādānas
after dēvatārchana and that under his instructions mahāpradhāna, sandhivigrahi, daṇḍanāyaka
Kāḷimarasa allotted at the donee's choice, land situated under the main canal (hiriya-kāluve) at
Iṭṭikāla in Koḷḷipāke-7000.

It is dated in Chālukya Vikrama year 35, Vikṛiti, Kārttika, Puṇṇami, Ādivāra. It
corresponds to 1110 A.D., October 29, Saturday (not Sunday). Lunar eclipse occured on that day.

l.note: For varmmadharmma mahArAja etc.
This line has been

inscription.head:
No. 68.
(A.R. No. 86 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar (No. 7) dugout from the main street in the village.

States that when Chālukya Tribhuvanamalla was ruling the empire from Jayantipura
and his son prince Somēśvara, who is eulogized with a string of birudas, ruled over several
territories including Koḷḷipāke-7000, sandhivigrahi, daṇḍanāyaka Kāḷimayya made the grant of
three pieces of land of the extent of 3 mattar each to (i) god Mallikārjunadēva installed by his
younger brother Tikkapayya, the daṇḍanāyaka of Koḷḷipāke-nāḍu, (ii) god Brahmēśvaradēva of
the same place installed by his son-in-law Pratikaṁta Bammadēva-nāyaka and (iii) Svayampāki
Mallu-bhaṭṭōpādhyāya, with the permission of the king.

A gift of one mattar of land to god Prabhudēva is mentioned in the post script. This gift
is out of six mattar (of land) which point is not clear.

The record is dated in the Chālukya Vikrama year 35, Khara, Pushya śu. 11, Thursday,
uttarāyaṇa sankrānti. It corresponds to 1111 A.D., December 12, [Tuesday]. [If the tithi was ba. 11
then it corresponds to December 28, Thursday. But there was no sankrānti on both the days].

l.note: Lettergra is written above the line.

inscription.head:
No. 69.
(A.R. No. 87 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALAGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar (No. 8) dugout from the main street in the village.

Mentions that Chālukya Tribhuvanamalla was then ruling the empire from the
capital Kalyāṇapura. It records gift of ten nivarttanas of land each to the brāhmaṇas Kēśava-
bhaṭṭōpādhyāya, son of Mailara-nāyaka of Śānḍilya-gōtra and Vāmana-bhaṭṭa, son of Penna-bhaṭṭa
of Bhāradvāja-gōtra under the main canal of the Brihat-taṭāka in the village Nellūṭu, and to
Golaya-bhaṭṭōpādhyāya, son of Ellaṇa-bhaṭṭōpādhyāya of Harita-gōtra under the main canal of the
village Vaḍḍagicheruvu, both situated in Kaluvachēḍu-40, a kaṁpaṇa of Koḷḷipāka-dēśa, by prince
Sōmēśvara. Besides 40 nivarttanas of wet land and two house-sites were granted to each of the
above mentioned donees in their respective villages.

The record is dated in the Chālukya Vikrama year 37, [Śaka 1034] Nandana, dakshiṇāyaṇa-
saṁkrānti. It corresponds to 1112 A.D., June 26, [Wednesday].

inscription.head:
No. 70.
(A.R. No. B. 790 of 1962-63.)
HOLKUNDA, GULBARGA TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in a locality called Khaṇḍāri.

This, a little damaged record, speaks of the rule of Chālukya Tribhuvanamalla. It records
the grant of 35 mattar of land to the west of the village by the mahājanas and 40 mattar of
land to the east (of the village) by the queen Chandaladēvi, to the god Kēśavadēva of agrahāra
Poḷagunḍe which is stated to be a bhattagrāma of the queen.

The record is dated in the Chālukya Vikrama year 37, Śaka 1035, Vijaya, Bhādrapada, śu.
lunar eclipse. It corresponds to 1113 A.D., August. 28 [Thursday]. Lunar eclipse occured on that day.

inscription.head:
No. 71.
(A.R. No. B. 772 of 1962-63.)
RAÑJŌLE-KHĒṆI, BIDAR TALUK, BIDAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab kept behind the Hanumān temple.

The slab is broken into three pieces and badly worn out. It belongs to the reign period
of Chālukya Tribhuvanamalladēva [Vikramāditya VI]. Then the record gives a long genealogical
account of the Sindha family to which mahāmaṇḍlēśvara Bhairavadēva belonged. Bhairavadēva
made a gift of lands and money to gods Hemmēśvara and Mailāḷēśvara of Ṟenjeṟu in the name
of his parents (tammayyana tammabbēya pesaralu). The gifts were made over to after leaving the
feet of ārādhya Davarēśvaradēva [perhaps he was the sthānapati of both the temples].

Also the record has registered gifts of land and money made by the mahājanas and
other individuals to the god Hemmēśvara and to the Brahmaṇas of the Brahmapuri of that god
and also to Bhāskara who wrote the record. Further, the record states that Jagatsēvya, one of the
four sons of Niḍudōḷ-Sindha, the progenitor of the Sindha family, governed Ṟenjeṟu, situated in
Aṭṭaḷe-nāḍu. The other three sons viz., Satyasēvya, Prabhusēvya and Budhasēvya administered over
Paḷiyaṁda-maṇḍala, Bāgaḍge-dēśa and Baḷḷare-nāḍu respectively. Davarēśa, the receipient of the gifts
is stated to be an expert in Vēdic tarka [logic] and vyākaraṇa [grammar] and a muni of Lākuḷa
Samaya, with exceedingly high tapaśakti.

The record is dated in Chālukya Vikrama year 40, Manmatha, uttarāyaṇa. Though the
information is insufficient the cyclic year can be equated with 1115 A.D. [Śaka 1037] which is the
40th year of the Chālukya Vikrama year and the tithi corresponds to December 25, [Saturday].

inscription.head:
No. 72.
(A.R. No. B. 543 of 1961-62.)
BALLAṬGI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the Desai tōṭa.

It states that during the reign of Chālukya Tribhuvanamalla, his queen Chandaladēvi made
several grants viz., a land of 12 mattar extent, a flower garden of 1(0) mattar extent, a house site
and an oil mill to the god Kalidēva for the worship, for the priest and for burning of perpetual
lamp. It mentions Mānuve and god Dhōrapēśvara also.

It is dated in Chāḷukya Vikrama year 40. Manmatha, Pushya, śu. 1 Vaḍḍavara, uttarāyaṇa
sankrānti. It corresponds to 1115 A.D., December 18. But, there was no sankrānti on that day.
uttarāyaṇa sankrānti occured on December 25, Saturday and the tithi was śu. 7 [f.d.t. 43].
Probably this is the actual date of the record.

mēśvara parama bhaṭṭārakaṁ satyāśraya kuḷatiḷakaṁ cāḷu-
bhivṛddhi pravardhamāna mācandrārkka tāraṁ saluttamire śrī svastyānavarata parama ka-
kaḷa haṁsayāne śrī ś®tā[gā]ra vidyādhara sakaḷakaḷādhari kastūrikāmōde [dāna vi]-
nōde śrīmatribhuvanamalla viśāḷa vakṣasthaḷa nivāsiniyarappa śrī[mat]
caṁdaladēviyaru śrīmaccāḷukya vikrama varṣada 40 neya manmatha saṁvatsa(rada)
kkaṁ mānuveya baṭṭeyiṁ baḍaga deseya dhōrapēśvara dēvara • • •
• • ḍaga sarvvabādhā parihāravāgi biṭṭa kariya nela mmattar 12 hūvina tōṁṭaṁ

inscription.head:
No. 73.
(A.R. No. B. 517 of 1961-62.)
PĒṬHŚIRŪR, CHITAPUR TALUK, GULBARGA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in the ruined Mallikārjuna temple outside the village.

The record mentions the rule of king Chālukya Tribhuvanamalla from the capital
Jayantīpura. The record is badly damaged. It states that Rebbanayya-daṇḍanāyaka of Śrīpura built
the temple of Rebbēśvara and endowed lands, a flower garden, three oil mills etc., for worship,
repairs, burning a perpetual lamp in the temple and for feeding students studying in the maṭha.
The endowments were made over to Sadyōjāta Panditadēva, the āchārya of Nūrēśvara (temple)
of Bamukūru. The endowments were made in the presence of Nāyaka Kāḷidasa, Manneya gouḍa,
Ambirāyarasa • • • • • sāhaṇi, Jingaḷada-arasa, his son Permādiyarasa, other officers and samasta prajes.

It is dated Chālukya Vikrama year 41, Hēviḷambi, Vaiśākha, Amāvāsya, Sunday, Solar
eclipse. It probably corresponds to 1117 A.D., May 3 [Thursday]. There was no eclipse on that day.
If the cyclic year is a mistake for Vilambi then the given tithi regularly corresponds to 1118 A.D.,
May 22, Wednesday [not Sunday]. Solar eclipse occured on that day. Perhaps this may be the
intended date.

inscription.head:
No. 74.
(A.R. No. B. 783 of 1962-63.)
GULBARGA, GULBARGA TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup in the Municipal garden.

It belongs to the reign period of Chālukya Tribhuvanamalladēva ruling from Jayantīpura.
It records that mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Hemmādi[yarasa] made a grant of 40 mattar land along with four
others [named] of Kadagaṅchi to the god Mallikārjuna, installed by Govindajīya at Kadaganchi.
The grant was made for the worship and offerings to the god. The record is incomplete since the
bottom portion of the slab is broken and lost.

It is dated in the Chalukya Vikrama year 42, Hēmaḷambi, Pushya, śu. 8, Monday,
uttarāyaṇa saṁkrānti. It corresponds to 1117 A.D., December 3, Monday. There was no saṁkrānti
on that day.

māhēśvaraṁ nijakīrtti mandākini pavitrīkṛtadi gaṁganāyakhaṁ catu-
rṣa sujanajana cittāpahāra [na]vadharimada nivāraṇa • kana gandhavāraṇa

padmarasanuṁ sāvaṇadeyana magaṁ biṁbarasanuṁ minti nālvaruṁ (bi)ṭṭa

l.note: The slab is broken and lost.

inscription.head:
No. 75.
(A.R. No. B. 576 of 1961-62.)
KURAḌI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab near the temple known as Nelaguḍi.

It states that when Chālukya Tribhuvanamalla was ruling from the capital Jayantīpura, the
mahājanas of agrahāra Rūḍhi made a grant of land for the benefit of the temple of Vāsudēva
built by Madhuvapayya alias Madhusūdhana-daṇḍanāyaka whose genealogy is given. Of the lands
granted, one was purchased by them from Chandikabbe, the wife of Chandraśēkhara-bhaṭṭa.

It also records grants of income from levies on articles such as pepper, ginger,
suger, Jaggery etc., to the same deity by the nānādēśi and other bodies of agrahāra Rūḍi.
The administrative division Karaḍikal-300 is mentioned here. The record is damaged. The top
portion is broken, hence, a few lines in the beginning are lost.

It is dated in Chālukya Vikrama year 43 • • • • • • • • • • Phālguṇa, Pūrṇima, Thursday.
It probably corresponds to 1119 A.D., February 27, Thursday, [Śaka 1040, Vilambi].

inscription.head:
No. 76.
(A.R. No. B. 512 of 1961-62.)
GŌṬŪR, CHITAPUR TALUK, GULBARGA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in the compound of the Goṭēśvara temple.

This damaged record contains a genealogical account of the Chālukya family and refers
to the reign of Chālukya Tribhuvanamalla who is stated to have been ruling from the capital
Jayantipura. It registers a grant of land and a garden for the worship of god Iśvara by manneya
Bibbarasa and the prajes. Sōmēśvarajīya, the priest of the temple was also given 20 mattar land
by the donors.

The record is dated in Chālukya Vikrama year 46, Plava, Phālguṇa, amāvāsya, Friday,
solar eclipse. It corresponds to 1122 A.D., March 10, Friday. Solar eclipse occured on that day.

inscription.head:
No. 77.
(A.R. No. B. 526 of 1961-62.)
SYED-CHINCHŌḶI, GULBARGA TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup infront of the Hanumān Temple.

This highly damaged inscription contains two records belonging to two different periods
and dynasties viz., 1122 A.D. of Chālukya and 1173 A.D. of Kalachūri dynasties respectively.

The first one refers to the reign of Chālukya Tribhuvanamalla from the capital Kalyāṇa.
The beginning portion seems to contain the genealogical account of the Chālukya family and
that of mahāmaṇḍḷēśvara Goṁkarasa. It then refers to the construction of a trikūṭa temple
Barma-dēhara, consecration of the god (name lost) there in and to very rich land grants, oil
mills and income from certain taxes for the worship and offerings to gods, burning of lamps
and for maintenance and repairs of the (newly constructed) temple and that of Kēśavadēva by
mahamaṇḍalēśvara Goṁkarasa, daṇḍnāyaka Nāra(sim)hayya and others. God Bhōgēśvara finds
mention in the grant portion. There is a reference to Goṅkarasara nūrippat-taruvattara baḷi. It is
the same as Goṅka 120+60[180] administrative division.

The record is dated in the Chālukya Vikrams year 46, Śubhakṛit, Chaitra, amāvāsya,
Monday. It corresponds to 1122 A.D., April 8, (Saturday). [Śaka year was 1044].

inscription.head:
No. 78.
(A.R. No. B. 572 of 1961-62.)
KURAḌI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar in the Īśvara temple.

It records a grant of land at agrahāra Rūḍi for worship and offerings to god
Niṁbaṇēśvara by daṇḍanāyaka Nimbaṇayya, after purchasing the land from Chandraśēkhara-bhaṭṭa,
to whom it was originally sold by the donor.

It is dated is Chālukya Vikrama year 47, Śubhakrit, Pushya, ba. 10. Monday, uttarāyaṇa-
saṁkrānti. It corresponds to 1122 A.D., December 25, Monday.

l.ka: dadalu dhārā pūrvvakaṁ māḍi biṭṭar ·ī dharmavaṁpra-
l.note: Letter lu is written below the line.

inscription.head:
No. 79.
(A.R. No. B. 573 of 1961-62.)
KURAḌI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab kept in the Īśvara temple [No. 1].

It refers to the reign of Chālukya Tribhuvanamalladēva ruling from the capital Jayantīpura.
The purport of the record is the same as in No. 78 above. Further it adds that the temple of
Nimbaṇēśvara was constructed by Nimbaṇayya. It mentions mahāmaṇḍḷēśvara kumāra Tailapa and
records further grants by the donor for a dharmēta (Charitable picotta).
The date is same as in No. 78.

edition.p: [The slab is broken on top and top left side].

l.note: Letter va is written below the line.

Sketch of a dagger pointing downwards has been engraved after engraving the inscription from top to bottom.

inscription.head:
No. 80.
(A.R. No. B. 579 of 1961-62.)
KURAḌI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built into the wall inside the temple called Nelaguḍi.

This slab is broken and very much damaged. Hence the information obtained from this
record is dis-jointed. Probably it recorded a land grant for the [worship and] offerings to the god
[name lost] and an oil-mill perhaps for the burning of Perpetual lamp. Probably a wet land of the
extent of kamma 200 measured in a particular rod of measurement [name lost] was also granted.
Names of the donor and donee are lost. There is a mention of mahājanas.

In the date portion it mentions 47th year (probably Chalukya Vikrama year). In the cyclic
year only letter to is clear. Probably the intended year was Śubhakritu. The 47th year of Chālukya
Vikrama era holds good with the cyclic year Śubhakritu. The name of the month is lost but the
tithi seems to be ba. 5. Though the exact date of the record cannot be made out, 1122-23 A.D.
may be stated as its year. On this basis it can be said that the record belongs to the reign period
of Chālukya Vikramāditya VI. [Refer No. 78 above].

inscription.head:
No. 81.
(A.R. No. 92 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALAGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On three faces of a huge pillar in the tank behind the Sōmēśvara temple.

This very lengthy record engraved on three faces of a pillar refers to the rule of the
king Tribhuvanamalladēva and is dated in Chālukya Vikrama year 50, Viśvavāsu, Chaitra, ba. 3,
uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti, Monday. It corresponds to 1125 A.D., March 24 f.d.t. 06 [Tuesday]. uttarāyaṇa
saṁkrānti is a mistake for mēsha saṁkrānti.

It records the gift of the village Pānupuṟa, excluding the dēvabhōga land formerly granted
to Akkabasadi near Poḷalu to the goddess Ambikadēvi of Ambara-tilaka by prince Sōmēśvara
at the request of daṇḍanāyaka, mahāpradhāna, maneverggaḍe Sāyimayya, the daṇḍanāyaka of
Koḷḷipāke-7000. Kēsirāja, the perggaḍe of the goddess (dēvīya-perggaḍe), was made in charge of
the gift.

On three faces of the pillar the record contains praśastis of prince Somēśvara and
Svāmi-daṇḍādhipa.

l.ka: ya kāraṇa kōpa pāvakaṁ sāśvata kīrtti
