inscription.head:
No. 82.
(A.R. No. B. 796 of 1962-63.)
JĪVAṆAGI, GULBARGA TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar setup in the Hanumān temple.

It states that when Tribhuvanamalladēva was ruling the empire from the capital Jayantīpura,
a grant was made for the worship, offerings, etc., to the god Nakharēśvara of Jīvanagi by the
āchārya (of the temple), Śrī Lākuḷēśvara-paṇḍita along with the perggaḍes and the nakharas of
the place. The details of grant are lost as the latter half of the record is heavily damaged.

It is dated in the Chālukya Vikrama year 50, Viśvā[vasu], Vaiśākha, śu, 13 Sunday. It
corresponds to 1125 A.D., April 17, Friday.

l.ka: tra • • • • • ga bhōga kkaṁ biṭṭa datti

inscription.head:
No. 83.
(A.R. No. B. 115 of 1961-62.)
BŌDHAN, BODHAN TALUK, NIZAMABAD DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab on top of the fort facing the newly discovered Ēkachakrēśvara temple.

This badly damaged record seems to register some gifts to a Jaina establishment the nature
of which is not clear. It refers to a family of Śreshṭins of which a lady (called Nālikāmbika) is
mentioned.

The record is not dated but refers to the rule of Chāḷukya Tribhuvanamalladēva. It is in
characters of the 11th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 84.
(A.R. No. B. 190 of 1962-63.)
MADDŪRU, ALAMPUR TALUK, MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the slab in the ruined fort.

The top portion of the slab is broken and lost. It refers to Tribhuvanamalladēva, his
subordinate Ayya and mahāpradhāna daṇḍanāyaka Śrī Rudra-bhattōpādhyāya who was govern-
ing Ayije 300 division along with other territories. Perhaps the record registered a grant. It is in
characters of 11th century A.D.

l.note: Letter ra is written below the line.
Stops here abruptly.

inscription.head:
No. 85.
(A.R. No. B. 233 of 1962-63.)
DŪḌKI, BANASWADA TALUK, NIZAMABAD DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the four faces of a broken slab lying in the Asharf-Khānā.

This fragmentary and badly damaged record refers to Chālukya Tribhuvanamalladēva
ruling from the capital Kalyāṇa and to his mahāsāmatādhipati, mahāprachaṇḍa, daṇḍanāyaka
Padmanābhayya. The latter was conferred with an administrative division (name lost) as tribhōgā-
bhyantarasiddhi by the king. It refers to the gift of an oil-mill and income from tolls. Details of
the gifts including the donor and donee including the date are lost. It is in characters of about
the 11th century A.D.

edition.p: Rest is completely worn out [about 12 lines].

l.note: The record is incomplete.

inscription.head:
No. 86.
(A.R. No. B. 759 of 1962-63.)
GORṬA, BHALKI TALUK, BIDAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in the field of Saṅkarappa Biradar.

The record contains genealogical accounts of Chālukya Tribhuvanamalla and his feudatory
chief.

It registers gifts of lands, house-sites, money income etc for the worship, offerings, ranga-
bhōga and sahasrōpachāra to the god Rudrēśvara in agrahāra Gōranṭe and also for maintaining
the students and teachers of an educational institution attached to the temple by Rudra-chamūpa
and the mahājanās of Piriya-Gōranṭe. The record contains details of grants and the beneficiary
sections. Among these Kannaḍāksharada khaṇḍike and vyākhyāna chhātraru attracts our attention.

The record was composed by [Parmmāka] Mallapayya. The record is badly damaged and
worn out. Hence, much of the information including the date is lost. It is in the 11th century A.D.
characters.

inscription.head:
No. 87.
(A.R. No. B. 784 of 1962-63.)
HARSŪR, GULBARGA TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Three fragments lying near the Parvatadēva temple.

[Though the Report for the year 1962-63 has noticed three stone fragments, its published
note [p. 138] is only on one piece. Since each piece seem to be different they have been identified
here separately though the A.R. No. being the same].

This fragment-1 record in the 11th century A.D. characters is incomplete and contains only
the genealogical account of Kāḷiseṭṭi partially and it seems, one of his ancestors made a gift, the
account of which is not clear. This seems to be a Jaina record.

inscription.head:
No. 88.
(A.R. No. B. 833 of 1962-63.)
TUPDŪR, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup on the hude.

The slab is broken into two pieces from top to bottom and the left portion piece is
lost. On the basis of the information obtained from the right portion piece it can be said that
it is a Kalyāṇi Chālukya record belonging to about the 11th century A.D. The king was ruling
from [Kalyā]ṇada nelevīḍu. It mentions Tuppadūra prabhu [probably a subordinate ruler] and the
installation of a god [name lost] at the place [a]grahāra [name lost] and records the grant of 400
kamma land-garden for the worship and offerings to the god.

There is a mention of god Māṇikēśvara. Perhaps it was this god installed [in the newly
built temple] and the grant of land belong to this temple.

In the date portion only [Chālukya] Vikrama year, śu. 3, Thursday can be made out which
is insufficient for verification. [Yet, it can be suggested here that the record belongs to the reign
period of Chālukya Vikramāditya VI].

edition.p: Right part of the broken slab.

l.note: Rest is broken and lost.

inscription.head:
No. 89.
(A.R. No. B. 784 of 1962-63.)
HARSŪR, GULBARGA TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Three fragments lying near the Parvatadēva temple.

The record is partially preserved as the left portion of it [from top to bottom] is cut and
lost. Even the bottom portion of it is also cut and lost. Hence the record in characters of about
the 11th century A.D., is incomplete.

The record belongs to the reign period of Chālukya Vikramāditya VI who is mentioned
as permāḍi chakrēśvaram. The record at the beginning, contains the genealogy of the king. The
record provides a beautiful description of Goṅkana-nāḍu, a subdivision of Alande-1000.

Also, the record contains a genealogical account of Sāgara-Seṭṭi and Bhāskara-seṭṭi
(probably the brothers). They are stated to have constructed two Śiva temples namely Kēdārēśvara
and Baḷēśvara respectively and endowed them for the worship and offerings to the gods, (regular)
maintenance and repairs of the temples and for feeding the ascetics in the temples. There is a
mention of a Goṅka maṁneya about whom the information is incomplete.

The record tells us that besides Śiva the images of gods like Vināyaka, Ravi (Sun),
Janārdana and Vāsudēva and goddess Durgā also installed in these temples and the endowment
included the worship and offerings to these gods and goddess also.

edition.p: [The left portion of the slab from top to bottom is cut and lost].

l.ka: • • • • • ra cāravē traiḷōkya nagarāraṁbha mūḷa sthabhāya śaṁbhavē || śrīgaṇapatē namaḥ—

inscription.head:
No. 90.
(A.R. No. B. 582 of 1961-62.)
KURAḌI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Broken slab near the temple of Rāmalinga.

This damaged and very much effaced inscriptions is in 11th-12th century A.D. characters.
It refers to the rule of king Tribhuvanamalladēva and a land grant for the worship and offerings
of the god (name lost) by Rebbaladēvi, the wife probably of mahādaṇḍanāyaka Raviyana bhaṭṭa.
Further, it registers land grants for burning of perpetual lamp in the temple and for offerings to
the god Rebbalēśvara by the daṇḍanāyaka himself. Also, it refers to Ayyaṇavāḍi and sarvanamasya
(agrahāra) Rūḍi.

The lands so granted are measured in two types of rods of measurement called (1)
Kēśvadēva(ra) kōlu and (2) Māṇikēśvarada kōlu. The samashta prajes of the place also joined
hands with the daṇḍanāyaka in making grants.

In the date portion only śu. 3 Vaḍḍavāra [aksha]ya tṛitīya in available and the rest of the
information is lost. Akshaya tṛitīya occurs in the month of Vaiśakha.

edition.p: [Few lines in the beginning are lost].

l.ka:

inscription.head:
No. 91.
(A.R. No. B. 153 of 1962-63.)
GAḌIGARĒVULA, NANDYAL TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in front of the main shrine of the Bhōgēśvarasvāmin temple.

This damaged and broken record belongs to the reign period of Chālukya Tribhuvanamalladēva.
The king is stated to have been ruling from the capital Kalyāṇa.

It seems to register some grants for worship, offerings and perpetual lamp for a
deity [name lost] probably by mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Gōvaṇachōḷa-mahārāja who was governing
Heḍakal-800 [same as Pedekal-800] division. The chief bears the usual praśasti of Telugu-Chōḍas
and is described as the lord of Noṟeyūrpura.

The slab containing details of grant and date is broken and lost. The record is in characters
of 11th-12th century A.D.

l.note: The rest

inscription.head:
No. 92.
(A.R. No. 73 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the same Museum No. 90.

This record in Kannaḍa script and Sanskrit language is very much damaged. It gives a
genealogical account of the dynasty of Chālukyas of Kalyāṇa upto the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva.
It registers the grant of a village and mentions some āchāryas of some maṭhas as the recipients
of the gifts by Svāmi-daṇḍādhipa. The gift was made at the behest of the Prince Sōmēśvara.

The date portion is very much damaged and only Chālukya Vikrama year • • • • • •
uttarāyaṇa sankrānti can be made out. The information is insufficient for verification. It is in 12th
century A.D. characters.

inscription.head:
No. 93.
(A.R. No. 73 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the same pillar in the same Museum No. 90.

This record, engraved on the fourth face of the same pillar [Museum No. 90], seems to
be a different record but damaged very much.

Dējarasa, Tribhuvanamalla and Sōmēśvara described as narēśvara, are mentioned in the
record. Besides, there is a mention of Ganga, Kaḷinga and Siṁhaḷa kings. Since, the beginning is
broken and lost and the end is completely damaged the purport of the record is not clear. It is
in characters of the 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 94.
(A.R. No. B. 550 of 1961-62.)
BĒVINŪR, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in front of the ruined temple outside the village.

This highly damaged record states that when Chālukya Tribhuvanamalladēva was
ruling over the empire, Aṇnaladēva [a subordinate of the king] made a grant of land probably
at Bhattagrama Bēvīnahā[ḷa]. In the date portion only Pushya, amāvāsya is available. It is in
characters of the 12th century A.D.

l.ka: svasti samasta bhuvanāśrayaṁ śrīpṛthvī vallabha mahārājādhirājaṁ paramē-

l.note: Right side of the slab is broken from line 5 to the end.

inscription.head:
No. 95.
(A.R. No. B. 809 of 1962-63.)
BASTIPURA, KOLLEGAL TALUK, MYSORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built into a platform just outside the village.
It mentions Śrī-man-mahāmaṅḍaḷēśvara Tribhuvanamalla only.

inscription.head:
No. 96.
(A.R. No. B. 761 of 1962-63.)
GORṬA, BHALKI TALUK, BIDAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built into the courtyard of the house of Prayāgabāyi Nāgappa Hāḷuṁbare.

The top portion of this slab is broken and lost. As a result information regarding the king
and the family to which he belonged, are lost. However, the date of the record indicates that it
belonged to the reign period of Sōmēśvara III.

It registers gifts of lands for worship and offerings to god and burning of a perpetual
lamp in the temple of god Mallēśvara situated near the temple of Nagarēśvara in Goranṭe, a
sarvanamasyadagrahāra, by the mahājanas of that place. The record has eulogized the qualities
of mahājanas. It also registers a gift of a garden to the same deity by Pullavaḍugara Māyipayya-
nāyaka and [Ra]yiṇa-nāyaka probably on the same date.

The record is dated in Chālukya Vikrama year [5]3, Kīlaka, Pushya śu. 1, Monday,
uttarāyaṇa saṁkrānti. It corresponds to 1128 A.D., December 24, Monday.

inscription.head:
No. 97.
(A.R. No. B. 575 of 1961-62.)
KURAḌI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built in to the floor of the Īśvara temple.

This damaged record registers a grant of money by [Nārā]yaṇa bhaṭṭōpādhyāya, son of
Sōma-bhaṭṭōpādhyāya to the mahājanas of agrahāra Rūḍi when Chālukya Bhūlokamalla [Sōmēśvara
III] was ruling over the empire.

It is dated in the king's regnal year 9, Ānanda, Pushya • • • • • Tuesday, uttarayaṇa
saṁkrānti. It corresponds to 1134 A.D., December 25, Tuesday. The tithi was śu. 8.

l.note: First three lines are engraved on top of the slab.

l.ka: ra makkaḷu • • yaṇa bhaṭṭōpādhyāya śrī matsarvva namasya

l.note: Contains the sketch of a dagger pointing down to the length of the slab.

inscription.head:
No. 98.
(A.R. No. B. 565 of 1961-62.)
KALLŪR, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built into the wall of the Māraṭēśvara temple.

It states that when Bhūlōkamalla was ruling the empire from the capital Kalyāṇa.
mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara Būchidēvarasa of the Haihaya family made a land grant to the god Māraṭēśvara
of agrahāra Kallūru in the presence of all the mahājanas of the village. The donor was governing
Mudugundūru-300 from his headquarters at Mudugundūru fort. It has also recorded a land grant
made by hegaḍe Chavutaṇa to the same god.

The record is dated in the regnal year 9 of Bhūlōkamalla [Somēśvara III], Ānanda, Pushya,
śu. 11 Friday, uttarāyaṇa saṁkramaṇa. It corresponds to 1134 A.D., December 29, Saturday, [f.d.t. 06].
There was no saṁkramaṇa on that day.

inscription.head:
No. 99.
(A.R. No. B. 207 of 1962-63.)
RĀJŌLI, ALAMPUR TALUK, MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
A broken slab in front of Rāmēśvarasvāmi temple on the banks of Tuṅgabhadrā (about half-a-
mile from the village).

It states that when Chālukya emperor Bhūlōkamalla [Sōmēśvara III] was ruling over
the empire perggaḍe Sūrapayya, the adhikāri [officer-in-charge] of Yaḍanāḍu, made a grant
of income from various taxes to the god Sahasralingadēva of Poḷasanūru for offerings to and
worship of the god and the repairs of the temple at the instance of mahāpradhāna, maddverggeḍe,
bāṇasuverggaḍe, daṇḍanāyaka Śrivatsadēvarasa, the mahāsāmanta of the emperor.

The record after referring to the rule of the emperor begins with an eulogy of this
mahāsāmantadhipati. Poḷasanūru is stated to be a datti of Vinaya-Satyāśrayadēva. The 300 Viḷāsinis
of Poḷasanūru, situated within Ayije-nādu division are also mentioned. The record also has regis-
tered another grant of income from shops, sale of betel leaves and areca, oil-mill etc., of Rājavoḷalu
to the god. Rājavoḷalu seems to be the ancient name of the present village Rājōli.

The record is dated in Bhūlōkamalla year II, Naḷa, Chaitra, ba. Monday, Vyatipāta, Vishu-
saṁkramaṇa corresponding to 1136 A.D., March 23, Monday. [the tithi was probably ba. 4].

inscription.head:
No. 100.
(A.R. No. B. 746 of 1962-63.)
ANDUR, TULJAPUR TALUK, OSMANABAD DISTRICT, MAHARASHTRA.
inscription.p: On a slab fixed in to a dilapidated wall in the village.
This record is engraved on the same slab above No. 63.

This damaged record has registered a grant of 5 gadyāṇas per year for maintaining a
perpetual lamp in the temple of Māṇikēśvara by the queen Mājiladēvi probably in the presence
of heggaḍe Mattiyaṇa-nāyaka of Hannipaḷḷi and karaṇās.

Nāḍa adhikāri was made responsible for maintaining this dharma. It is dated in Chālukya
Bhūlōkamalla year 11, Naḷa, Kārttika, śu. 1, Monday. It corresponds to 1136 A.D., September 28,
Monday.

l.ka:

inscription.head:
No. 101.
(A.R. No. B. 236 of 1962-63.)
DŪḌKI, BANASWADA TALUK, NIZAMABAD DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On four faces of a pillar setup near the dhavajasthaṁbha in the Sōmēśvara temple beyond the
Pirula-Cheruvu.

It refers to the rule of Chālukya Bhūlōkamalladēva who was ruling the empire from
the capital Kalyāṇa and to his mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara Sōma-permmāḍi who is described as Paṁpana
gandhavāraṇa. The latter made a grant of the mēlāḷike including taxes like sēse, kiṟukuḷa, kāṇike
etc., of Bāḍa-Bāṁsavāde, situated in Bolle-70 division, to Bālachandradēva for worship, and offerings
and for feeding the guests and ascetics.

The record is dated Piṅgaḷa, saṁkramaṇa, sūrya grahamṇa, Monday. Probably it corresponds
to 1137 A.D., November 15, Monday. Solar eclipse occured on that day.

l.note: About four lines of
Letter ga is engraved below the line.

inscription.head:
No. 102.
(A.R. No. B. 574 of 1961-62.)
KURAḌI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab kept in the Īśvara temple. [No. 2].

It states that mahāpradhāna daṇḍanāyaka Niṁbaṇayya made a grant of land at agrahāra
Ṟaṟāvi to Nāgaladēvi, wife of Śankarārya, as a present (baḷuvaḷi). Half of that land was made over
by Nāgaladēvi to the temples of Sankarēśvara and Nāgēśvara at agrahāra Rūḍi probably built by
her. Mention is made of mahāmaṇḍḷēśvara kumāra Tailapadeva. At the beginning the record has
an eulogy of Nāgaladēvi.

It is dated in the regnal year 12 (of Bhūlōkamalla-Somēśvara III), Pingaḷa, [Śaka 1059]
Pushya, śu. 11. Friday, uttarāyaṇa saṁkrānti corresponding to 1137 A.D., December 24, Friday.

l.ka: © svasti śrīmatsaneṁba riṣiya lasadvaṁśadoḷogedu gōtra maṁḍana [nese]-

inscription.head:
No. 103.
(A.R. No. B. 760 of 1962-63.)
GORṬA, BHALKI TALUK, BIDAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab lying in the field of Shankarappa.

This short and incomplete record engraved at the end of Inscription No. 86 above registers
gifts of lands [probably to the god Rudrēśvara of agrahāra Gōraṇṭe] on the occasion of solar
eclipse by Bhāskara bhattōpādhyāya, the preceptor of emperor Bhūlōkamalladēva. The gifted lands
were measured in the measuring rod named Samkēśvaradēvara kaiyyakōlu. Kōḷanūru (village) is
mentioned at the end. It is in characters of the 12th century A.D.

l.ka:
yyāṁgaḷu bhāskara bhaṭṭōpādhyāyaru sūryyagrahaṇa nimittavāgi tamma • • • • • doḷage •
• • • • • daṁ paḍuvalu maṁbava(ku) baḍa baṭṭiyiṁ baḍagalu saṁkēśvaradēvara kaiyyaṁ
kolu biṭṭa mattaru • • • • •

inscription.head:
No. 104.
(A.R. No. B. 769 of 1962-63.)
AṆADUR, BIDAR TALUK, BIDAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Two faces of a slab lying in the grave yard behind the Hire-maṭha.
The record belongs to the first regnal year of the Chālukya king Jagadēkamalla II.

It states that a gift of lands, flower gardens, house-sites, income from taxes and oil mills
for worship and offerings to god Nāraṇēśvaradēva of Aṇaṁdūr and for feeding Brāhmaṇas and
ascetics in the choultry (sattra), by maṇḍalēśvara Baddarasa, the manneya, and mahāpradhāna,
maneverggaḍe Bammaṇayya-daṇḍanāyaka, the sāmantikey-adhikāri of Ṟaṭṭale [Aṭṭale]-nāḍu under
the orders of the king when, he visited the temple of Mahābhairava Maiḷāradēva in the first year
of his rule and had the foot prints of the god Śrī Pādagaḷam installed in the temple. The record
contains a long genealogical account of Baddarasa and mahāpradhāna Gopadēva-daṇḍanāyaka.

The record is dated Śaka 1059, Kāḷāyukti, Regnal year 1, Pushy śu. 15, uttarāyaṇa
saṁkrānti. The cyclic year Kāḷāyukti occurred in Śaka 1060 and the given tithi corresponds to
1138 A.D., December 18, Sunday.

inscription.head:
No. 105.
(A.R. No. B. 564 of 1961-62.)
KALLŪR, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab near the Salt-water well.

It states that when Chālukya Jagadēkamalla was ruling over the empire from the
capital Jayantīpura, Sātikavve made a grant of land in the presence of the mahājanas of agrahāra
Kallūru to the temple of Kēśavadēva constructed by her. Mention is made of Būchidēvarasa and
Kāvarājayya. The latter was the daṇḍanāyaka of Eḍedoṟe-2000 and the heggaḍe of paṭṭasāhaṇi
hiriya daṇḍanāyaka Bīmaṇayya.

The record is dated in the regnal year 5, Dundubhi, Pushya, śu. 7, Friday, uttarāyaṇa
saṅkramaṇa. It corresponds with 1142 A.D., December 25, Friday. However, the tithi was śu. 6.

l.ka: © svasti samasta bhuvanāśraya śrī pṛthvīvallabha mahārājādhirāja
paramēśvara parama bhaṭṭāraka satyāśraya kuḷatiḷakaṁ cāḷukyābha-
kaṁ bīmaṇayyaṁgaḷa heggaḍe yeḍedoṟeyicchāsirada daṁḍanāyakaṁ kā-
varājayyaṁgaḷu samasta praśasti sahitaṁ śrīmanmāhāmaṁḍaḷēśvaraṁ bhū-
(dā) 5 neya duṁdubhi saṁvatsarada puṣya śuddha 7 śukravāra duttarāyaṇa
saṁkramaṇa nimittavāgi sātikavvegaḷu tāvmāḍisida śrīkēśavadēvaraṁga bhōga
naivēdya naṁdādīvigegaṁ caitrapavitrakkaveṁdu ballakabbegalu vīsakadalli baḍagaṇa ka-
ḍeyaloṁdu vṛttiya kaḷedalli • teṁkaṇa tammadoṁdu vrittiya keyyaṁ

pāḷisidavargge śrī vāraṇāśiyalu sāvira kavilegaḷa koḍuṁ
koḷagu maṁgalavankadalu kaṭṭisi caturvvēda pāragarappa brāhmaṇargge dānaṁ
gaḷmanā brāhmaṇarumaṁ koṁda dōṣavakku | sāmanyōyaṁ dharmma sētuṁ nripā-
ṇaṁ kālē kālē pālaniyō bhavadbhiḥ sarbvānētānbāginaḥ pārtthi vēṁdrān bhūyō
bhūyō yācate rāma bhadrāṁ | svadattāṁ paradattā vāyō (ha)
rēti vasuṁdharāṁ ṣaṣṭivvarṣa sahasrāṇi viṣṭhāyāṁ jāyatē krimi(ḥ) [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 106.
(A.R. No. B. 577 of 1961-62.)
KURAḌI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab near the temple known as Nelaguḍi.

The record is damaged and incomplete. It is engraved at the bottom of the slab contain-
ing record No. 75 above. The record says that the mahājanas of (agrahāra Rūḍi) made a grant
of land of 4 mattar extent measured in the rod Beṁkoḷvana kōlu to the temple of Vāsudēva at
the place during the reign of Chālukya Jagadēkamalla II. It is said that Mācharasa had made a
request to the mahājanas for a little more land grant earlier at the time of the installation of
the deity in the temple of god Vāsudēva built by his father Madhuvapayya-nāyaka.

The record is dated in the regnal year 10 [of the king] Pra[bhava]. Māgha, ba. 2, Sunday
corresponding to 1148 A.D., February 8, Sunday.

l.ka: svasti śrī maccāḷukya pratāpa cakravartti jagadēkamalladēva varṣada 10 neya pra • • • • •
ṣṭheyaṁdu ·avara magaṁ mācarasanu taṁma dēvarige kiṟidu bhūmiyaṁ kuḍalu • • • • •

inscription.head:
No. 107.
(A.R. No. B. 797 of 1962-63.)
JĪVAṆAGI, GULBARGA TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup into the prākāra of the Bimbanātha temple, in front of the central shrine.

It states that when Jagadēkamalladēva was ruling over the empire from the capital Kaly-
anapura, Kriyāśakti-paṇḍita, the ācharya of the sthāna of god Bibbēśvara of Jīvanige, along with
the prabhus and the samastanagaras made some grants of land to the god Pārśvanātha of the
village, attached to the Śāntinātha [basadi] of Alande. The grant was made at the instance of
Bhānusēnayya.

The grant was made for raṅgabhōga and offerings to Pārśvanāthadēva and for feeding the
ascetics living there. It included one garden of two matter extent. The record has a description of
the ascetic qualities of Kriyāśakti-paṇḍita.

It is dated in the Jagadēkamalla year 2, Vibhava, Mārggaśira, ba. 14 Monday. It corresponds
to 1148 A.D., December 11, Saturday (not Monday).

l.ka: syaśāsanaṁ jinaśāsanaṁ || svasti samasta bhuvanāśrayaṁ śrīpri-
thvīvallabhaṁ mahārājādhirājaṁ paramēśvaraṁ parama bhaṭṭārakaṁ satyāśraya kuḷa-

baṭṭeya baḍaga deseyaloṁdu tōṭaṁgaḷidalli kālaḍiyimmattaru tōṁ-

inscription.head:
No. 108.
(A.R. No. B. 567 of 1961-62.)
KALLŪR, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the compound of the Māraṭēśvara temple. [First Face].

It records a grant of income occurring from various taxes such as hejjuṅka, bilkoḍe,
baṇnaya and vaḍḍarāvuḷa for the worship, offerings and perpetual lamp to the god Māraṭēśvara.
The grant was made by heggaḍe Mallidēvarasa and Kopparasa on behalf of daṇḍanāyaka pasāyita
surigi Vēmṇnamarasa, the heggaḍe of Ayyaṇavāḍi and mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Būchidēvarasa.

The record speaks of the rule of Chālukya chakravarti, Rakkasagaṅga [Tailapa III] and
is dated in the king's regnal year 1, Pramōdūta, Phālguṇa, amāvāsya, Monday, solar eclipse
corresponding to 1151 A.D., March 19, Monday. There was no eclipse on that day. Solar eclipse
occured on February 18, Sunday. But the tithi was Magha, Amāvāsye.

l.ka: © svasti śrī namastuṁga śiraścuṁbi caṁdracāmara cāravē
va variṣada 1 neya pramōdūtha saṁvatsarada phālgu-
kaṁ pasāyitaṁ surige veṁṇnamarasaruṁ svasti śrīmanmahā-
gabōgakkaṁ naṁdādīvigegaṁ biṭṭaru yī dharṁmmavaṁ prati-

l.note: Letter va is written below the line.

inscription.head:
No. 109.
(A.R. No. B. 544 of 1961-62.)
BALLAṬGI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup in the Desai tōṭa.

It records a grant of land for the worship and offerings to the god and repairs of the
temple of Kalideva by mahāmaṇḍḷēśvara Ghaṭṭidēvarasa of the Haihaya family during the rule of
Chālukya Vikrama chakravarti Rakkasagaṁga. It also registers a grant of land to the god Āditya.

It is dated in the regnal year 2, Prajāpati Āshāḍha. śu. 11, Tuesday, dakasiṇāyaṇa, saṁkrānti.
It corresponds to 1151 A.D., June 26, Tuesday.
This record is engraved on the same slab [No. 19 above].

l.ka: ra taraṁga saṁhata vīcīnicaya niritalavaṇābdha hōvasa vaḷaya suvaḷayi-
ta gaṁgā pāriyātra tanmadhya dēśādhiśvaraṁ māhiṣmatī puravarēśvaraṁ sahasra bā-
hu pratāpaṁ bhūpadiḷāpana tyugra naraśika darppaṇa dhraja virājamānaṁ
dānaśānī naṁ mūlapaṟē ghōṣaṇaṁ nṛparatna bhūṣaṇanahihaya vaṁśō
dbhava kanyāṁganā tanūbhavaṁ śrī mallikārjjuna dēvalabdha vara prasādaṁ vairi
maṇḍaḷika mastaka divya pra(pā)daṁ nāmādi samasta praśasti sahitaṁ śrī-
manmahā maṇḍaḷēśvaraṁ ghaṭṭi dēvarasaru śrī maccāḷukya vikrama cakra-
vartti rakkasagaṁga dēva varṣada 2 neya pra[jāpa]ti[saṁva]tsara dāṣā-
[ḍha] śuddha 11 maṁgaḷavāradaṁdu dakṣiṇāyana saṁkrānti parvva nimitta
• • kaṁ kētēśvara dēvara kayya mūḍa sarvva[bādhā pari] hāravāgi
koṭṭageya nela mattarppanneraḍu || ślōkaṁ || svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vāyō harēti
vasuṁdharāṁ ṣaṣṭirvvarṣa sahasrāṇi viṣṭāyāṁ jāyatē krimi [||]
mūlasthāna dēvara muṁdaṇāditya dēvarggā dēvara keyya mūḍalu biṭṭa kari-

inscription.head:
No. 110.
(A.R. No. B. 568 of 1961-62.)
KALLŪR, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup in the compound of the Māraṭēśvara temple [Second Face].

This incomplete record states that at the instance of mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Būchidēvarasa, his
feudatories, such as heggaḍe Chāmaḍāṇa and another person (name lost) who was a mahāpradhāna
and taṁtrapāla, made a grant of income from certain taxes for the haḍapa of god Mārateśvara.
The guild of Hamnavaṇa-1000 made a grant of betel leaves for the same purpose. The record
mentions the 500 svāmis of Mahānāḍu (the assembly) which was probably in-charge of the entire
administration.

It is dated Chālukya Vikrama year 4 (the record year - 4 of Tailapa III) Śrīmukha, Pushya.
śu. 8, Friday. uttarāyaṇa saṁkramaṇa corresponding to 1153 A.D., December 25, Friday.

inscription.head:
No. 111.
(A.R. No. B. 535 of 1961-62.)
BALLAṬGI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup behind the Hanumān temple.

This fragment belonging to the reign period of Chālukya nūrmaḍi Traiḷōkyamalladēva
refers to the prabhu of Baḷḷaniṭṭage.

It is dated in the 4th regnal year (of the king) Bhava, Pushya, śu. 10. Thursday, uttarāyaṇa
saṁkrānti. It corresponds to 1154 A.D., December 16. But there was no saṁkrānti on that day.

inscription.head:
No. 112.
(A.R. No. B. 516 of 1961-62.)
MUPTI, CHITAPUR TALUK, GULBARGA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in the Īśvara temple.

The record is partially damaged. This record registers grants of land and flower gardens for
the worship of and offerings to the god Kalidēva, Saṁkaradēva and Kēśavadēva of Bāḍa Muppaṭi,
a village in Goṁka-nūrippat-aruvattu [120+60=180] in Alaṁde-1000 when mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Goṁ
karasa was governing the division from the capital Kopaṇapura during the reign of Chālukya
Trailōkyamalla (III) ruling from the capital Kalyāṇa.

It is dated in the regnal year 8, Dhātu, Pushya ba. 10, Thursday, uttarāyaṇa saṁkrānti.
It corresponds to 1157 A.D., January 8, Tuesday. But uttarāyaṇa saṁkrānti occured on śu. 11 of
Pushya month and it corresponds with 1156 A.D., December 24 Monday. Probably this may be
the intended date of the record and ba. 10 is a mistake for Śukla 11. Regnal year 8 seems to be
mistake for 6.

inscription.head:
No. 113.
(A.R. No. B. 775 of 1962-63.)
DHARWAR, DHARWAR TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Impression received from Dr. G.S. Dixit, Karnataka University, Dharwar
[Findspot-Kolhapur].

h4.b:
Nūrmaḍi Chakravartti Trailōkyamalla [Sōmēśvara IV]
12th century A.D.

inscription.p:
The record is broken and damaged. It belongs to the reign period of Chālukya Nūrmaḍi
Trailōkyamalla. It seems to register a gift of land to the god Sankēśvaradēva. It refers to Bijjala
and mahājanās of an agrahāra [name lost]. The record gives the genealogy of the king and the
general Kāḷidāsa.

Regarding the date only Śivarātri 14 Monday is available. It is in the 12th century A.D.
characters.

śaṁbhavē [|*] • jaya

ḷidāsa • tanādipaniṁ kiṟiyaṁ samasta sajjananuta kāmadhēnu [kali kāla] • • • • •
tritaḷaṁ permmevetudu saṁkēśvara dēvatthā niḷaya • mu • • • • • • • • • •

inscription.head:
No. 114.
(A.R. No. B. 481 of 1961-62.)
JĀGANŪR, CHIKKODI TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built into the backside wall of the shrine of the Māruti temple.

The right side of the slab is broken causing a loss of 8-10 letters in each line. It records a
gift of land to the god Svayambhu Siddhēśvara of Bayināḷa situated in a division (name lost) con-
sisting of 120 villages by several persons of whom Vaijarasayya, Viṭṭharasayya and Guḍḍagāmuṇḍa
are mentioned. Padmarāsi-paṇḍita, the āchārya of the temple received the gift.

The grant (of land) was made specifically for the worship and offerings to the god and
maintenance of the temple.

The record states that mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Tribhuvanamalla Bijjaṇadēva was in charge
of the mēlāḷke of a division (name lost), while daṇḍanāyaka sēnādhipati Dhannugidēvayya and
mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kattamadēvarasa were together administering the manneya of Kūnḍi-3000.

The record is dated Śaka 108(9), Pushya, Śu. 7, Sunday, uttarāyaṇa saṁkramaṇa probably
corresponding to 1167 A.D., December 20, Wednesday. There was no saṁkrānti on that day.

l.ka: caṁdra cāmara cāravē traiḷōkya nagarāraṁbha mūlastaṁbhāya • • • • •
masta bhuvanāśraya śrī pṛthvīvallabha mahārājādhi rāja para(mē) • • • • •
masta saraṇāgata vajrapaṁjaraṁ pratā[pa*] laṁkēśvara vairibhakaṇṭhīrava • • • • •
giridurgamalla nissaṁkamalla nāmādi samasta praśasti sahi • • • • •
tpāda padmōpajīvigaḷappa svasti samasta praśasti sahitaṁ śrī • • • • •
raṁ suvarṇa garuḍadvajaṁ trivaḷi paṟeghōśaṇaṁ ṟaṭa • • • • •
dhūra lāṁcchanaṁ sāhitya vidyāviriṁcanaṁ sityarādhēyaṁ saucāṁ • • • • •
taḷaprahāri nāmādi samasta praśasti sahitaṁ śrīmanmahāmaṇḍaḷēśvaraṁ • • • • •
vardamāna mācaṁdrārkka tāraṁbaraṁ saluttamire kūṁḍi mūṟu sāsirada manne • • • • •
le nūṟippattaṟabaḷiya bāḍa śrīmatu bayināḷiya mūli • • • • •
va hoḍeya śrīmatu [sva]yaṁbhu siddēśvaradēvargge khaṁḍasphuṭita ji • • • • •

inscription.head:
No. 115.
(A.R. No. B. 560 of 1961-62.)
HIREKOṬNAKALLU, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup on the Banni-kaṭṭi.

It states that during the reign of Chālukya Tribhuvanamalla Sōmēśvara and when
mahamaṇḍaḷēśvara Maiḷugidēvarasa of Mānuve was administering, Brahmadēvabhaṭṭa, the grandson
of Tippaṇa bhaṭṭōpādhyāya made a grant of land in the presence of the mahājanas of agrahāra
Koṭṭukuṇuhe for a feeding house (choultry). The gifted land was earlier purchased by the donor
from Iśvaraṇṇama of Bāguḷahāra.

The record is dated śaka 1113, Virōdhikṟit, Chaitra, śu. 13, Sunday corresponding to 1191
A.D., March 10. Sunday.

l.ka: śrī ·ōṁnamaḥ śivāyaḥ || namastuṁga siraścuṁbi caṁdracāmara cā-
ravē trailōkya nagarāraṁbha mūlastaṁbhāya śaṁbhavē || svasti sama-
sta bhuvanāśraya śrīpṛthvīvallabhaṁ mahārājādhirāja paramēśvaraṁ pa-
rama bhaṭṭārakaṁ satyāśrayaṁ kuḻatiḷakaṁ cāḷukyābharaṇa śrīmatribhuvanamalla sōmēśvara
raṁbaraṁ saluttamire || tatpāda padmōpajīvigaḷappa svastyānēka bhaṁgura taraṁga saṁ-
ghātavīcī[vi]jaya lavaṇābdhivaḷaya vaḷayīta vēḷāvana gaṁgāyātra tanmadhye
dēśādhīśvara māhiṣmatī puravarādhīśvaraṁ madhyadēśādhipati sahasrabāhu pratāpabhū-

inscription.head:
No. 116.
(A.R. No. B. 485 of 1961-62.)
KERŪR, CHIKKODI TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built upon the platform in front of the Māruti temple.

The record is damaged and the top portion of which is broken and lost. It refers to the
rule of a king (name lost) and to his subordinate (name lost) who is stated to be the lord of
Kāḷanjara. It registers a grant of land, a house, a garden etc., to the god Mūlasthāna Koppēśvara of
agrahāra Keṟevūr in Koravaḷḷi-150. The grant meant for the worship and offerings to the god and
for the maintenance of the temple was made over to Vāmarāsi-paṇḍita, the āchārya of the temple.
The grant was made by mahāpradhāna sēnādhipati daṇḍanāyaka Jayadēvayya, mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Jayasiṁhadēva who was governing the manneya of Kūnḍi-3000, mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara Kattamadēva,
mahāmaṇḍaḷika Hemmāḍidēvarasa who was administering Koravaḷḷi division from Gōkāge and
other mahājanas when mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kaḷachurya Bijjaladēva was in charge of the mēlāḷke
of Kūnḍi-3000.

The record is dated Śaka 1071, Vibhava, Pushya, śu. [1]4 Thursday. It corresponds to 1148
A.D., December 26, Sunday. [The Śaka year was 1070].

inscription.head:
No. 117.
(A.R. No. B. 774 of 1962-63.)
RAÑJŌLE-KHĒṆI, BIDAR TALUK & DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built into the right wall (outside) of the Hanumān temple.

This very much damaged record belongs to the reign period of Rāyamurāri Sovīdēva of
Kalachūri family which ruled from the capital Kalyāṇa.

The record registers a gift of land, gardens and income from taxes for worship and
offerings to the god Brahmēśvara, for the repairs of the temple and for feeding ascetics by
śrīkaraṇa, and antahpura verggade, mahapasāyita Brahmadēva-daṇḍanāyaka after laving the feet
of Chandrābharana-munīndra. The temple of Brahmēśvara seems to have been established at
Ṟenjēṟu by Bāchidēvarasa, the mahāmaṇḍalēśvara. The record contains genealogical accounts of
both Brahmadēva and Bāchidēvarasa and also of the preceptor Chandrābharaṇa-munīndra, the
āchāry of Ṟenjēṟu Hejjēśvara temple. There is a reference to the construction of the temple of
Hejjēśvara with pond and a shikhara over the temple by Hejjabhūpa, the Sindha chief.

The record is dated in the 2nd regnal year of the king Śaka 1091, Sarbbadhāri, uttarāyaṇa
saṁkramaṇa. The cyclic year Sarbbadhāri occurred in Śaka 1090 corresponding to 1168 A.D., which
happens to be the 2nd regnal year of king Sovidēva. uttarāyaṇa saṁkramaṇa occured on 25th
December, Wednesday.

inscription.head:
No. 118.
(A.R. No. B. 787 of 1962-63.)
HARSŪR, GULBARGA TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup against the wall inside the Boslingēśvara temple.

It carries a very long genealogical account of the family of Kalachūris and states that the
king Rāyamurāri Sōvidēva was ruling over the empire from the capital Mādiganūru.

It records grant of the village Ankulage for the worship of the god and repairs of the
temple of Bhōgēśvaradēva by mahāpradhāna, sēnādhipati, bāhattara niyōgādhipati, hiriya daṇḍanāyaka
Mādhavayya-nāyaka along with mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara Vīra kāmadēvarasa and Vīra Goṅkarasa. Also
it registers further grant of an oilmill, income from the sale of arecca-nut, pepper etc and land.

The village Ankulage is the same as Ankulage of Bāḍa situated with in the administrative
division Goṅka-nūrippat-taṟuvattu [120+60=180 viz, Goṅka-180]. River Beṇṇeyatoṟe is mentioned as
one of the boundary lines of the granted village. The grant was received by Hariharajīya, the
āchārya of the temple.

The record is dated in the cyclic year Khara, Māgha, śu. Huṇṇime, Thursday
corresponding to 1172 A.D., January 13, Thursday. Lunar eclipse occured on the previous day
January 12, Wednesday.

inscription.head:
No. 119.
(A.R. No. B. 527 of 1961-62.)
SYED-CHINCHŌḶI GULBARGA TALUK, GULBARGA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup infront of the Hanumān temple.

This record is engraved in continuation of No. 77 above. It belongs to the reign period
of Kalachūri Rayamurāri Bhujabalamalladēva. It states that mahāpradhāna Mādhavaiah after
appealing to the emperor, made a gift of land in Bāḍa-uppaḷa-vāvi situated in Goṁka-nūrippa-
ttaru-vattu [120+60=180], which was under the jurīsdiction of his administration, to the worship
and offerings and for various services and maintenance of the temple of the god Nāraṇēśvara of
Cheṁchavaḷḷi and also for the feeding of the ascetics and for maintaining a choultry there in.

Chaṅḍimarasa, who was them administering Cheṁchavaḷḷi made over the gift to Vāraṇāsi
Sṛishṭikāchārya Tējōnīdhi paṅḍitadeva. Details of land so gifted is mentioned in the record. The
responsibility of maintaining and protecting the gift was made to vest on the prabhus and
gāvuṇḍas (of the place).

The record is dated in the 7th regnal year (of the king), Vijaya [Śaka year not mentioned],
Jyēshṭha, amāvāsya, Monday, solar eclipse, Pushya nakshatra and Vishu saṁkramaṇa. It probably
corresponds to 1173 A.D., June 12 (Tuesday). There was no saṁkramaṇa on that day but the solar
eclipse occured. The eclipse occurred on Tuesday. But the nakshatra was Ardrā.

inscription.head:
No. 120.
(A.R. No. B. 817 of 1962-63.)
GŌGĒBĀḶ, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying by the side of the path leading to the field of Kawtal Sivappa.

This highly damaged record belongs to reign period of [Kalachūri] Rāyamurāri Sōvidēva
described as Kāḷanjara puravarādhīśvara (lord of the city of Kāḷanjara). It mentions vīrabaṇanju-
dharma, nānādēsis, seṭṭiguttu, mummuri-daṇḍas, nakhara, Aruvattokkalu [a group of 60 landed prop-
erty] etc. Perhaps all these together made some grant of income from tax to a god [name lost]
and also to the sthānika Chavuḍa of the place. Also it seems to record an additional gift of 31
haṇa per month [probably arising out of the tax collected] to the same god.

It is dated Śaka 1097, Jaya, Mārggaśira, huṇṇime. It corresponds to 1174 A.D., November
10, Sunday. Lunar eclipse occured on that day.

inscription.head:
No. 121.
(A.R. No. B. 160 of 1962-63.)
ĀLAMPUR, ALAMPUR TALUK, MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the pedestal of an image in the open air museum, Alampur.

This very much damaged and incomplete record belongs to the reign period of
Kalachūri Bhujabalamalla. [Bhujabalamalla was one of the titles possessed by the Kalachēri king
Bijjaḷa]. The purport of the record is not clear. Probably it registers a grant (of land), the details
of which are lost and to the son (name lost) of Māramayya.

inscription.head:
No. 122.
(A.R. No. B. 192 of 1962-63.)
MADDŪRU, ALAMPUR TALUK, MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Third face of the same slab [No. 52] in the ruined fort.

It states that when mahāmaṇḍḷēśvara Kāvaṇa Chōḷa mahārāja was ruling the kingdom from
the capital Mosaḷemaḍuvu, made a grant of 20 mattar land measured in the scale Śatyaratnākarana
kōlu in Rēkalūru for worship, offerings, etc., of the god Chinchaka Rēchēśvara whose temple was
situated on the banks of the river Tungabhadrā at the capital Maddūru located in Aje [Ayije]
300 division.

The record is dated in Chālukya Vikrama year 16, [Śaka 1013] Prajāpati, Mārgaśira, śu, 12,
Tuesday corresponding to 1091 A.D., November 25, Tuesday.

l.note: Line 18 onwards has been engraved on the second face of the slab below No.

inscription.head:
No. 123.
(A.R. No. B. 747 of 1962-63.)
ANDUR, TULJAPUR TALUK, OSMANABAD DISTRICT, MAHARASHTRA.
inscription.p: On a slab fixed into a diapidated wall in the village.

This damaged and incomplete record refers to the rule of Yādava king Bhillamadēva. It
mentions mahāpradhāna Gaṇḍarayya and Vachōrāśī.

It is dated in Bhillamadēva year 6, [Śaka 1106] Krōdhi, Ā(śvīna), Thursday, vishu
saṁkramaṇa. Probably it corresponds to 1184 A.D., September 27, Thursday [tithi ba. 6]. There was
tulāsaṁkramaṇa on that day.

l.ka: • • • • • ma(ru) yādava kuḷatiḷakaṁ pratāpa cakravarti śrī

inscription.head:
No. 124.
(A.R. No. B. 482 of 1961-62.)
JĀGANŪR, CHIKKODI TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab in the ruined Gavi-Siddēśvara temple on the bank of the Hirehaḷḷa about a mile to the
south of the village.

This very badly damaged record seems to have registered a grant mentions to a basadi
of which details are lost. It refers to Kūṇḍi-3000 and to piriya agrahāra Kabbūr, Kallarasa, an
officer probably in charge of a division is also mentioned.

It is dated in the (regnal) year 7, Prabha(va), Bhādrapada, śu. 3 Monday. The date is wrong.
Regnal year 7 falls in the cyclic year Viśvāvasu. The cyclic year Prabhava mentioned here do
not tally. It may be a mistake for Parābhava which occured in Śaka 1108. Then the given tithi
can be equated with 1186 A.D., August 19, Tuesday. In the cyclic year Viśvāvasu the given tithi
occured on 1185 A.D., July 31, Wednesday.

l.ka:
hādēvarāya [vija]ya rājyōdayada [yē]ḷaneya prabha • saṁvatsarada bhādrapada suddha 3
[sō]-
vāyō harēta vasuṁdharāṁ saṣṭi rvvaruṣa sahasrāṇi viṣṭāyāṁ jāyatē kri-
miḥ maṁgaḷa mahā śrī śrī śrī śrī śrī [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 125.
(A.R. No. B. 773 of 1962-63.)
RAÑJŌLE-KHĒṆI, BIDAR TALUK & DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab kept behind the Hanumān temple.

This damaged record is engraved as post script to the record No. 71, A.R. No. 772 above
but 94 years after that of the Chālukya record dated 1115 A.D. It belongs to the reign period of
Yādava Śinghaṇa, the king of the Yādavas of Dēvagiri family. The record is dated in his 10th
regnal year, [Śaka 1130] Vibhava, Māgha, śu. Puṇṇame, lunar eclipse. Thursday, Pushya nakshatra
corresponding to 1209 A.D., January 22. The nakshatra was however Āślēsha and not Pushya. The
week day was Thursday and the lunar eclipse occured on that day.

It mentions Ayaryaṇa bhūpa of Aṭṭaḷe-nāḍu and refers to the great qualities of ārādhya
Ballahadēva.

The record states that mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara Ādyarasa had the god Hajjēśvaradēva
reinstalled besides installing god Ādyēśvaradēva and made a gift of 300 mattar of his own land
at Hajuvalige for the repairs of the temples after laving the feet of ārādhya Ballahadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 126.
(A.R. No. B. 508 of 1961-62.)
BEṆṆUR, CHITAPUR TALUK, GULBARGA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in the ruined temple.

It refers to Rāmachandra ruling from the capital Dēvagiri and to his subordinate
mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara Goṁka-bhūpāla administering from his head quarters Kōpaṇa [described as
Kōpaṇa puravarādhiśvara].

It states that manneyas of the 18 santatis of Goṁka-nāḍu including mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Nāgadēva, Nāchideva, Mailugidēva and Satyadēva made a grant of the village of Beṅdanūr,
situated in Saravaḍige kaṁpana, a sub-division in Goṁkana-180 of Alande-vishaya to mahāpradhāna
Mallināthadēva-daṇḍādhiśa who was ruling over Tardavāḍi, Banavasi and Halasige situated with in
Eḷambi-600 division.

The record is dated Śaka 1210, Sarvadhāri, Kārttika, śu. 5, Thursday corresponding to 1288
A.D., October 31, Sunday [not Thursday].

inscription.head:
No. 127.
(A.R. No. B. 832 of 1962-63.)
TAḌKAL, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup near the house of the Patel.

This very much damaged record mentions the rule of Yādava Rāmachandra. Here only
the date and the village Bāguḷahāḻu can be made out. It seems to record the death of a hero.

It is dated in the cyclic year Nandana, Āsvija, ba. 13, Monday. It corresponds to 1292 A.D.,
October 9, Thursday [The Śaka year was 1214].

l.note: Stops here abruptly.

inscription.head:
No. 128.
(A.R. No. B. 499 of 1961-62.)
ŚRINGĒRI, SRINGERI TALUK, CHICKMAGALUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab near the western side of the Ānjanēya temple (behind the Vidyāśankara temple.)

This is a very important inscription from the point of both the history of Vijayanagara
empire and the maṭha of Śringeri.

It records a grant of nine villages (named) of Kela-nāḍu situated with in Sāntaḷige-nādu
after deducting 150 gadyāṇa towards taxes payable by the cultivators within, by the king Śrī
Vīra-Hariyapoḍeya and his brothers Kampaṇṇoḍeya, Bukkaṇṇoḍeya, Mārapoḍeya, Muddapoḍeya,
son-in-law Ballappa-daṁṇāyaka and kumara Sōvaṇṇoḍeya to Śrī Bharatitīrtha Śripāda, his disciples
and the forty Brahmin attendants.

It also registers a grant of Kela-nāḍu and Kaimaleya Hosaūru and Kanyana situated
with in Sāntaḷige-nāḍu to the attendants of Śrī-Bharatitīrtha Śrīpāda by Vira Kikkayi-tāyi who is
described as Pānḍya chakravarti. The record begins with salutations to the Vidyātīrtha. Lingōja,
son of Chiṁna-Mallōja wrote this record.

The record is dated Śaka 1268, Pārthiva, Phālguṇa, ba. 1, Thursday corresponding to 1346
A.D., March 9, Thursday.

inscription.head:
No. 129.
(A.R. No. B. 621 of 1961-62.)
KERGĀL, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in the Durgaparamēśvari temple.

It registers a land grant for the offerings of god Trimūrti in order to expiate the sin
of killing a Brahmana. The grant was made probably by Huṇisemane Govinda and Kuyalamane
Kṛishṇa when mahāpradhāna Maleya daṁṇāyaka was administering the region from Bārahkanyāpura.

Since the record is damaged not much information is available regarding the murder of a
Brahmana and the responsibility of the donors in this regard. At the end the record informs that
the donors were granted with 2 gadyāṇā cash and probably with some tax relief.

It is dated in the cyclic year Sarvajitu, Vaishākha, ba. 5 [Vrishabha māsa 4] Monday. The
given tithi corresponds with [Śaka 1269] 1347 A.D., April 30.

l.ka: • • nū barsi koṭṭaru ·ā 2 manege ve • biḍusa-

inscription.head:
No. 130.
(A.R. No. B. 620 of 1961-62.)
KAÑCHIGĀN (BIJUR), COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in a field called Nils-Kal-hal-hāne.

It registers a tax free gift for offerings to maṭhada Śālagrāmadēva probably by mahāpradhāna
Maleya daṁṇāyaka and Chika Maleya daṁṇāyaka during the rule of Bukkaṁṇa-voḍeya. It
mentions Amaḷa-Śrī-nandi-Śripāda.

It is dated Śaka 1271, Virōdhi, Kārttika, śu. 2, Thursday corresponding to 1349 A.D., October
14, Wednesday. The record is a little damaged.

l.ka: gi sarba māṁnyavāgi baṁdaṁthā pa(ṇa) • silā
kālē kālē pālaniyō bhavadbhiḥ sarvānēvaṁ bā-
vinaḥ pārthivēṁdrān bhūyō bhūyō yācatē rāmacaṁdraḥ ||

inscription.head:
No. 131.
(A.R. No. B. 500 of 1961-62.)
ŚRINGĒRI, SRINGERI TALUK, CHICKMAGALUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab kept near the eastern side of the Aṅjanēya temple.

This inscription has recorded grant of lands in some of the villages (named) situated in
Sātaḷige-nāḍu and Kikunda-nāḍu with an annual yield of 300 gadyāṇa and confirming the old
grants of the yield of 60 gadyāṇa (total of 360 gadyāṇa) for the maintenance of the attendants of
the maṭha and for continuous feeding of ascetics in the maṭha by the king Śri-Vira-Bukkaṇṇoḍeya
when the latter paid a visit to Śringeri and had the darshan of Śri-Vidyatīrtha-śripāda. The record
begins with a salutation to Śri Vidyatīrtha yati. It has no imprecatory section at the end.

The record is dated Śaka 1277, Manmatha, Phālguṇa, Śu. 1, Tuesday corresponding to 1356
A.D., February 2, Tuesday.

daya śakavaruṣa 1277 maṁnmatha saṁvatsarada phālguṇa śu 1 maṁgaḷa vāra

inscription.head:
No. 132.
(A.R. No. B. 625 of 1961-62.)
KOḌĒRI [hamlet of] KIRIMANJĒŚVARA, COONDAPUR TALUK,
SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup in front of Hakre-maṭha.

It states that when mahāpradhāna Maleya-daṁṇāyaka was governing the Bārakūru-rajya,
Vissaṇṇa, son of Vīraṇṇa made a grant of land called Āneya-bāḷu after purchasing it by pay-
ing the prevailing rates, to the god Kiru-manḍilēśvara for feeding 14 brahmanas and burning a
perpetual lamp in the temple. The record gives details of boundaries of lands and also details of
daily expenditure. It is said that the grant was made on the day of solar eclipse [the previous day
after record]. Also the record speaks of a grant made for burning a perpetual lamp (in front of)
Kōṭīśvaradēva.

Also the record says that Vissaṇṇa got renovated the Hosakēṟi maṭha at Bayidūru which
had been heavily damaged by the floods and made some grants to it. Earlier Kōṭi-seṭṭi of
Mangalūru had made some grants to this maṭha. Since the record is very much damaged details
of grants are lost.

It is dated Śaka 1280, Viḷambi, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 1, Vaḍḍavāra. It corresponds with 1358 A.D.,
July 7, Saturday, f.d.t. 01.

l.ka: śrī gaṇādhipatayē nama[ḥ*] | śrī sarasvatyeyēṁ namaḥ [|*]
sti śrī jayābhyudaya śakavaruśa 1280 neya viḷaṁ-

lēśvara dēvaralli māḍi • • • • • barasida śilā śāsanada

da[lū] voppi yi • • gaḍiya kaṭṭiṁda voḷa guḷa ba-
yalu tōṭa makki mara nokki nidilu ·allivuḷaṁ-

rige naṁdā veḷaku 1kkaṁ ga(4) hoṁnu maṁgalūra
lli māḍida dharmmakke [yi kēri] ya bāḷinali (naḍḍūra)
li voḷaguḷḷa • • gadde hoḷe [kaḷi] yāgi hō-
gōpināthana kēśavaṁge siṁnegēri sthaḷa kariya gōppaṇa

sanakke maṁgaḷa mahā śrī śrī śrī śrī śrī [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 133.
(A.R. No. B. 497 of 1961-62.)
ŚRINGĒRI, SRINGERI TALUK, CHICKMAGALUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Broken slab in front of the Ādi-Śankara temple.

In this fragmentary inscription Bukkarāya is mentioned and all other details are lost. It is
in 14th century A.D. characters. Probably Bukkarāya made some grants, the details of which are
lost.

inscription.head:
No. 134.
(A.R. No. B. 626 of 1961-62.)
NANDANAVANA(Uppuṇḍa Po), COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in the prākāra of the Mahābalēśvara temple.

It states that mahāpradhana Mallapa-Voḍeya, while governing the Bārakūru-rajya under the
orders of the king Harihara II, made a tax free gift of land to Vidyāpuriya Śrīpāda. The gifted
land was within the Mahābalēśvarada-sthāna. The details of purpose and grant are lost since the
record is damaged.

It is dated Śaka 1312 Śukla, [Jyēshṭha] śu. 5, Sunday corresponding to 1389 A.D., May 30.
The month was nija Jyēshṭha.

l.ka: namastuṁga śiraścuṁbi caṁdra cāmara cāravē trailō-
kya nagarāraṁba mūlastaṁbhāya śaṁbhavē | svasti jayābhyu-
daya śālivāhana śakavaruṣa 1312 śukla saṁvatsarada [jēṣṭha] śudha 5 [ādivāra]
svasti śrī madrārājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara ·arirāya vibhāḍa bhāśe(ṣe)-
ge tappuva rāyaragaṁḍa pūrvadakṣiṇa paścima ·uttara samudrā-
dhipati śrī vīra pratāpa harihara mahārāyara nirūpadi-
gi biṭṭa śrī mahābalēśvarada sthānada • • • • • • • • • • kaṭi(ṁ)

inscription.head:
No. 135.
(A.R. No. B. 638 of 1961-62.)
CHANDKŪR, PUTTUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup in the Durgaparamēśvari temple.

It states that when mahāpradhāna Kēsappa-Oḍeya was governing the Mangaḷūru-rājya
during the reign of Devarāya-Voḍeya, Bīraṁṇa of Anegallu made a land grant yielding 600 muḍe
paddy for feeding 12 brāhmaṇas daily in the choultry attached to the Durgāparamēśvari temple at
Chandakūru. The record gives details of boundaries and income from each piece of land. Sēnabōva
Dēvarasa wrote this record.

It is dated Śaka 1339, Dundubhi, Pushya ba. amāvāse, Sunday, Śrāvaṇa nakshtra, Brihaspati
in Kuṁbha. The cyclic year Dundubhi is a mistake for Durmukhi. The given details of tithi
correspond to 1417 A.D., January 18 when Dēvarāya I was ruling over the empire of Vijayanagara.
The tithi amāvāse occured on both 17th and 18th [Sunday and Monday] of the month January. But
the nakshatra Śrāvaṇa was on Monday the 18th.

l.ka: svasti śrī gaṇādhipatayē namaḥ || śrī durgāyai namaḥ namastu-
ṁga śiraścuṁbi caṁdra cāmara cāravē trailōkya nagarāraṁbha mū-
lastaṁbhāya śaṁbhave || śubhamastu || śakavaruṣa sāvirada mūnūṟa mūvattavoṁbha-

lla bīraṁṇaru caṁdanūra durgāparamēśvariya sthānadalū māḍida dharma
vajana ·aḍigeyātanoba sahita ja 12 yi haṁneraḍu ma-
ṁdi brāhmarige bhōjanakke naḍava braṁhasva bhūmi ·uḷuvayi mūnūrā
vāsudēvana bāḷige || ·ā ·uḷuvayya bhūmige gaḍi mūḍalu tāhina beṭṭa-
dagaḍiyiṁdaṁ paḍuvalu | teṁkalu nelliṁjada mattitimara gaḍiyiṁ-
daṁ baḍagalu | paḍuvalu bareyada heggaḍeya magaḷiya gaḍiyagaḍi-
yiṁdaṁ mūḍalu | baḍagalu yeṟagida beṭṭada gaḍiyiṁdaṁ teṁkalu
yiṁti catusīmeyiṁdoḷage yeṇalu suggiya bittuva yeraḍu
beḷeya bhūmi prati mū 1 kkaṁ hāṁ 30 lekkadalu mūla ·ā gaddeya ga •
ddeyiṁ caṁdakūra chatrada maṭhake naḍava bhatta pramū 1 kkaṁ hā 30
lekkadalu 450 mūḍe bhatta mattaṁ ·uḷuvayoḷage jitamūṟara kayyalu
bīraṁṇaru koṁḍa gadde mū 12 yi gaddeyiṁda maṭhakke naḍava bhattada
mū 50 ·ubhayaṁ ·uḷumeya bayalu mū 100 kkaṁ chatrakke naḍa-
va bhatta mū 500 mattaṁ puruśōttama yirāyanamaga subraṁhma-
ṇyana bāḷikeya hosamaṭada bayaliṁge gaḍi mūḍalu • • • • • • • • • •
ḍagalu koḷaceya gōviṁdana gaddegaḍi | yiṁti catusimeyiṁ-
doḷage bayalu mū 10 majalu mū 10 ·ubhayaṁ 20 mūḍegadde
• gaddeyiṁdaṁ caṁdakūra catrada maṭhake kālaṁ pratiyalā caṁdrārkka
vāgi naḍasuva bhatta prati mū 1 kkaṁ hā • ṁ lekkadalū mū 50 ·akṣa-
radalū ·ayivattu mūḍe batta | mattaṁ yi ·ayila hiriya gaddeya keḷa
gaṇa hosa māṭada gaddege gaḍi mūḍalu hoḷegaḍi | teṁkalu mūḍappa-
viya vrittigaḍi | paḍuvalu beṭṭa gaḍi baḍugalu hiriya gaddegaḍi | yiṁti
nālku gaḍiyiṁdoḷage yeraḍa beḷe bittuva bayalu mū 10 yi ha-
ttu mūḍe gaddeyiṁdalu chatra maṭhake naḍeva bhatta pramū 1 kkaṁ hā 30 le-
bhatta ·akṣaradalu ·āṟunūṟu mūḍe bhatta caṁdakūra durggāparamēśvariya sa-
ṁnidhiyalu brāhmaṇa bhōjanakke ·ācaṁdrārkka sthāyiyyāgi naḍeva dharma yidharmma-
vanu baṁgarū naḍasina jananeva bāḷuva bīraṁṇarū nālkūra sāvirada nūṟava-
rū grāmau pālisi baharu yiṁtappudakke sākṣigaḷu ·āditya caṁ-
rātriśca ·ubhayeca saṁdhyē dharṁmmaṁna jānāti narasyavittaṁ | dānapā-
taṁ padaṁ [|*] sudattāṁ paradattāṁ vāyōharēta vasuṁdhara ṣaṣṭivaruṣa saha-
srāṇi vriṣṭāyāṁ jāyate krimi | yidharmmavanū ·aḷupi ·āvanobanu keḍi-
sidātaṁge vāraṇāsiyalu nāvirada nūṟu kavileya koṁda dōṣa ku-
rukṣētradalū taṁma tāyitaṁdeyanū vadhisida dōṣa | gōkaranada śrīma-
hābala dēvara liṁgava kittadōṣa | śrī durgāparamēśvariya mūrttiyanū ki-
tta dōṣa || yidharmavanū yi sthānada jananigaḷu pālisi baharu | yiṁtivaru
bhayāṁmatadiṁ bīraṁṇara sthaḷada sēnabhōvaru dēvarasara baraha | śubhamastu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 136.
(A.R. No. B. 637 of 1961-62.)
BEḶAMANJE, PUTTUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in a field called Kaḷḷi-maru.

This badly damaged record states that haḍapada Nagaṁṇa-Oḍeya was governing the
Mangalūru-rājya under the orders of Virapratāpa Dēvarāya-mahārāya and refers to Pānḍyapparasa,
the younger brother of the Banga Chief (name lost).

It is dated Śaka 1340, Bhādrapada, ba. 30, Monday. The cyclic year is not mentioned.
Probably the given tithi corresponds to 1418 A.D., August 31. [In Śaka 1340 the cyclic year was
Viḷambi].

l.ka: ruṣa sāvirada mūnūṟa nālvattaneya bhādrapada ba 30 sō • •

inscription.head:
No. 137.
(A.R. No. B. 622 of 1961-62.)
KERGĀL, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In a field called Maṭṭan-kaṭṭe.

This is a badly worn out record. It states that Panḍaridēva voḍeya was governing the
Bārakūru-rājya under the orders of Timmaṇa-daṇāyaka when Mallikārjuna was ruling over the
empire from the capital Vijayanagara. It seems to record some grants which were entrusted to
the care of Gaṇapu-setti. The details of which are lost.

It is dated Śaka 1377, Yuva, Jyēshṭha, ba. 1 Sunday corresponding to 1455 A.D., June 1,
Sunday.

l.ka: • • ṇādhipatayē namaḥ śrī gurubhyō namaḥ śrī sarasvatyē namaḥ
• • • ṁga śiraścuṁbi caṁdra cāmara cāravē trailōkya nagarāraṁbha mū-
la staṁbhāya saṁbhavē || svasti śrī jayādbhudaya śakavaruṣa [1377 neya]
yuva saṁvatsarada jēṣṭa ba 1 ·ā śrī manmahārājādhirāja rāja-

inscription.head:
No. 138.
(A.R. No. B. 623 of 1961-62.)
KHAMBADA KŌṆE COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup in a field called Kallu-toḍame.

It states that Vijayappa voḍeya son of Ratnappa voḍeya, while governing Bārakūru-rājya
under the orders of Kṛishṇarāya mahārāya who was ruling over the empire from the capital
Vijayanagara, made a grant of 20 ghaṭi-varaha-gadyāṇa equivalent of 50 Kāṭi-gadyāṇa, for the
amritapaḍi offerings to Chandranātha and for burning a perpetual lamp there (in the basadi). The
grant of money was made from out of the income from siddhāya tax of Kambada-kōṇe, a place
of Bhanḍāra-sthaḷa of the palace situated within the village Kerugāla. The grant was made free
of all taxes for the merit of the king. Mallarasa, the karaṇika of Bārakūru wrote this record.

It is dated Śaka 1438, [passed nine] Dhātu, Mārgaśira, śu. 15 corresponding to 1516 A.D.,
December 9, [Tuesday].

l.ka: ṁ svasti namastuṁga śiraścuṁbi caṁdra cāmara cārave trailōkya nagarāraṁbha mū-
ṁ la staṁbhāya śaṁbhavē svasti śrī jayāda(ṁdaya) śālivāhana śakavaruśa

inscription.head:
No. 139.
(A.R. No. B. 624 of 1961-62.)
KHAMBADA KŌṆE COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Another slab in the same field called Kallu-toḍame.

This seems to be a replica of the previous [No. 138] record. The king, the governor, the
grant, the donor, the purpose of the grant and the date-all being the same as in the previ-
ous record. The only difference we find here is the recipient and the amount of the grant. The
amount was 60 dod[ḍ]a varaha gadyāṇas equivalent of 150 Kāṭi-gadyāṇa and it was given to
Vīrasēna-dēvācharya of Penugoṁḍe. Bārakūru karaṇika Mallarasa wrote this record.
The date of this record is as in No. 138 i.e., 1516 A.D., December 9, [Tuesday].

la staṁbhāya śaṁbhave | svasti jayābhyudaya śālivāhana śakavaruśa 1[43]8

kha saṁkathā vinōdadiṁda duṣṭanigraha śiṣṭapratipālaneyāgi saka-

inscription.head:
No. 140.
(A.R. No. B. 552 of 1961-62.)
CHIKALPARAVI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Four slabs setup at the entrance of the village.

The record is highly damaged. It states that Krishnadēvaraya, after his conquest of Raichū-
ru, Alaṁpūru, Mānve, Hānumagalu and Māgaḍe forts Visited Kāḍalūru at the confluence of
the Kṛishṇa and Bhimārathi and made a grant of the village Chikalparvi in Mānuve-sīme to
god Tiruveṅgaḷanātha while performing ratna-dhēnu, gōsahasra and Kanakāśva mahādānas in
association with his queens Chinnādēvi and Tirumaladēvi. Gods Virūpāksha-dēva, Kālahāstikēśvara
and Sangamēśa are mentioned in the record.

It is dated Śaka 1443 Vṛisha, Śrāvaṇa śu 12. Monday corresponding to 1521 A.D., July 16,
Tuesday.

l.ka: patiṁ bā • • ḷāta dha • ne • • gaṁgāḷi (karaṁ)
tā śāsana || 12 || nānādāna kuḷārjita • sada jayaḥ śrī
paraṇa kāvana • kālaṁ kā • ma māja • na

ke cetaisi svasti śrī jayābhyudaya śāli-

inscription.head:
No. 141.
(A.R. No. B. 183 of 1962-63.)
CHENNIPĀḌU, ALAMPUR TALUK, MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab kept in front of Kēśavaśvāmi temple.

The record belonging to the reign period of emperor Krishnadēvamahārāya registers a
gift of the village Gōkulapāḍu alias Bhandarada Pēdāpura to god Chennakēśva of Chennipāḍu by
Peddināyaka son of Bhandarada Siddhināyaka for the merit of the king.

The gift village was situated in Alampura-Sīme which was given to the donor by the
king as Amaranāyakatana.

The record is dated Śaka 1449, Sarvajitu, Chaitra, Śu. 1. It corresponds to 1527 A.D., March
3, [Sunday].

This is the date of issuing the record. But the grant was actually made some time back,
the date of which is available in the record. The grant was made on Pushya, ba. 10 of the cyclic
year Vyaya. It corresponds to 1526 A.D., December 29, [Saturday].

edition.h4: TEXT. Published in Telangāṇā Śāsanamulu, Vol. II, p. 148, No. 5.

l.ka: ṁ śubhamastu || namastuṁga śiraśuṁ(ścuṁ)bi caṁdra cāmara cāra[vē] [|*]

yābhyudaya śālivāhana śakavaruṣaṁgaḷu 1449 neya sarvajitu saṁ-
vatsarada caitra śu 1 lu ceṁnipāḍa śrī ceṁnnakēśvara dēvarige | śrī manmahā
rājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrīvīra pratāpa śrī vīra kṛṣṇadēva mahā-

rada sidhināyakara makaḷu peddināyakaru sāṣṭāṁga namaskāra māḍi samarppisikoṭṭa

ra nāyakatanakke pālisida ·alaṁpūra sīmeya voḷagaṇa gōkulapāḍa grā-
makke pratināmavāda bhaṁḍārada pedāpurada grāmavannu ceṁnnakēśvara dēvara
·amṛta paḍi ·aṁgaraṁga vaibhavakke veyasaṁvatsarada puṣya ba 10 saṁkrāṁ-

rada grāmakke saluva ·aṣṭa bōga tēja sāṁmya sakaḷa suvarnādāya sa(ṁ)-
kaḷa dhāṁnya ·ādāya ·a grāmakke saluva catusīmevoḷagāda | nidhinikṣē-

·āgi ceṁnipāḍa ceṁnnakēśvara dēvarige gōkulapāḍa grāmakke pratinā-
mavāda bhaṁḍārada pedāpurada grāmavanu dhāreyaneṟadu ko-

inscription.head:
No. 142.
(A.R. No. B. 184 of 1962-63.)
CHENNIPĀḌU, ALAMPUR TALUK, MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (2) kept in front of Kēśavasvāmi temple.

The record belongs to the reign period of the Vijayanagara emperor Krishnadēvarāya.
The inscription records a gift of the village Pūsalapāḍu alias Krishnarāyapura together with the
income from various taxes by Dīkshitayya, the younger brother of Chandrasēkarayya and the son
of Aṇṇāji-ayya to the god Chennakēśava of Chennipāḍu for the merit of the king and also of
Dēmarasayya. Nothing is known about Dēmarasayya. Based on the suffix, it may be suggested
that he might be a respectful elderly member of the family of Dīkshitayya. The gift village was
situated in Alampura-sīme which was given to the donor as amaranāyakatana by the king.

The record is dated Śaka 1450 [wrong for 1451], Virōdhi, Vaiśākha Śu. 15 (on the occasion
of lunar eclipse). It corresponds to 1529 A.D., April 23, [Friday]. The Lunar eclipse occured on that
day.
The last two lines contain imprecatory portion in Telugu.

edition.h4: TEXT. Published in Telangāṇa Śāsanamulu, Vol. II, Part-2, p. 149, No. 6.

lō caṁpina pāpānaṁ bōduru maṁgaḷa mahā śrī śrī śrī śrī śrī [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 143.
(A.R. No. B. 601 of 1961-62.)
MĀNVI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a boulder known as Jīnagāra-baṇḍi.

It states that during the reign of Sadāśivarāya that mahāmaṇḍlēśvara aḷiya Rāmarāja, on
the request of Timmoja, Bhadrōja and Koṇḍōja, exempted the barbers of the Rāyachūr area, from
the payment of certain taxes with a view to wishing the welfare of the king.

The record is dated Śaka 1475, Pramādi, Chaitra, śu. 15. It corresponds to 1553 A.D., March
29, [Wednesday].

l.ka: ṁ || śubhamastu || gaṇādhipatayē[nu] nama(ḥ) | svasti jayābhya-
ma saṁvatsarada | cayitra śu 15lu śrīmanumahārājādhirāja rāja
ha ·arasugaḷu | tiṁmōja koṁḍōja bhadrōjanavara biṁnahavanu kēḷi | sadā-

braṁhmākaḷuvārana ·uyaridaḷi sahā(rada vi) varāhanu sārāvā • • (ka bhu) mane

inscription.head:
No. 144.
(A.R. No. B. 743 of 1962-63.)
PRINCE OF WALES MUSEUM, BOMBAY.
inscription.p: Third slab kept in the Museum.

It records the grant of land in Kanyana (the village) situated in Yijapu by Pāṇḍyachakravarti
Baṁma-dēvāḷpendra-dēva to Gagana-Śivēśvarāchārya of the Dūrvāsa munīndra lineage. This grant
was in turn made over for the offerings [nivēdya] to the god Vighnēśvara of Nagareśvara by the
muni.

In the Annual Report for the year 1962-63 the king's name has been wrongly read as
Pāṇḍyachakravarti Dattāḷpēndradēva. The engraving on the stone slab is very clear and the king's
name Baṁma-dēvāḷpēndradēva can be read without any ambiguity. In characters of the 13th century
A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 145.
(A.R. No. B. 618 of 1961-62.)
HOSAKŌṬE, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in a field called Vishṇumūrti oḷa-māḍu.

It seems to register a grant (details are lost) made by the king Vīra Sōyidēvāḷpēndradēva
while giving audience at his court in the palace at (the capital) Barahkanyāpura (Bārakūru) and
in the presence of mahāpradāna Sōvaṇṇa sēnabōva, Siṅgaṇa sāhaṇi, Eraḍu-Kōlabaḷiyavaru, the
bāhattaraniyōgi, Viṭhaṇa and others. The place Rājāḍi is mentioned.

It is dated Śaka 1247, Raktākshi, Āshāḍha Śu. 7, Thursday. It corresponds to 1324 A.D.,
June 29, Friday.

inscription.head:
No. 146.
(A.R. No. B. 629 of 1961-62.)
KERAVASE, KARKALA TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup in the prākāra of Hirebasti of Vardhamanasvami.

It records a gift of land and 5 Bārakūru gadyāṇa by the king of Keravase-Karkala,
Vira-Pānḍyadēvarasa to Sanjāra seṭṭi, the nephew of Sanjaraḍahā of the Puḷi-seṭṭi baḷi (lineage)
with the stipulation that the donee should maintain the burning of a perpetual lamp in front of
Vardhamānaswamy of Keravase. The record gives details of land so granted and made free of all
taxes. Panḍari sēnabova son of Dēvappa sēnabova wrote this record.

It it dated Śaka 1371, Śukla, Kartika, śu. 1, Sunday. It corresponds to 1449 A.D., October 17,
Friday [not Sunday].

l.ka: trailōkyanāthasyaśāsanaṁ jinaśāsanaṁ || svasti śrī jayābhyudaya śaka-
varuṣa 1371 neya śukla saṁvatsarada kārttika śu 1 ·ādivāradaṁdu śrī matpa-

na ·ōju paḍuvalu ·ā baḷiya dattu kāḍana ·ōju baḍagalu ko-

ṁtāna paraṁpareyāgi ·ā caṁdrārkka stāyigaḷāgi bāḷi bahareṁ-

(vapa*) śīkaraṇa sthaḷada dēvappasēna bōvara maga paṁḍarisēnabō-

inscription.head:
No. 147.
(A.R. No. B. 627 of 1961-62.)
KERAVASE, KARKALA TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab [1] setup in the mūḍa-basti of Vardhamāna Svāmi.

It records a political agreement entered into by Pāñḍya-dēvarasa of Keravase and
Kāmirāyarasa Banga of Bangavāḍi. Both the rulers agreed to suspend thence forward all acts of
hostility against each other, that when one was to be attacked by an outsider, the other will rush
to the help of the defender and none of the two shall unilaterally enter into any pact with the
Chauṭas.

It is dated Śaka 1083, Vishu, Āśvīja, Śu. 1, Thursday. It corresponds with 1161 A.D.,
September 21. But the Śaka year given is too early and wrong for the record is in the
characters of the 15th century A.D. If it is taken to mean a mistake for Śaka 1383 then the given
tithi corresponds to 1461 A.D., September 5, Saturday [not Sunday]. This date falls with in the
reign period of both the kings mentioned in the record.

l.ka:

kramaveṁteṁdare nā-

·ū nīu kaṁḍu mātanāḍi baṁgavāḍi keravase ·uḷalli paryyaṁtara ·ācaṁdrārkkasthāyiyāgi
yeraḍu śā-

sanada vakhaṇebhiṁnavāgadahāṁge yeraḍu kōlabaḷi sāviradavaru | cikarasaru hari·adhikāri
dēvarsaru | gōpaṇa sēna bōvaru kūḍi

inscription.head:
No. 148.
(A.R. No. B. 630 of 1961-62.)
KERAVASE, KARKALA TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup in the Mahālingēśvara temple.

It records the grant of several lands for offerings during Ushā-pūje to the god and various
services in the temple of Mahādēva by Timmaya sēnabōva and his sons when Vira Pānḍyappa-
oḍeya was ruling the kingdom from the capital Keravase.

The record is dated Śaka 1468, Parābhava, Mēśha 15, Sunday corresponding to 1546 A.D.,
April 11, Sunday.

inscription.head:
No. 149.
(A.R. No. B. 503 of 1961-62.)
SRINGERI, SRINGERI TALUK, CHICKMAGALUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab on the eastern side inside the Mallikārjuna temple.

The record begins with salutations to god Śiva and Vidyā-tīrtha-Vidyā-tīrtha-mahēśvara,
and Vidyāraṇya, the pontiffs of Śrigeri maṭha. The inscription records that Venkatappa-nāyaka,
Grandson of Sadāśiva-nāyaka of Keḷadi re-established Śṛingeri where Abhinava-Narasiṁha-
bhāratisvāmi was ruling the Yōga-paṭṭa, and that the latter's disciple Puṭṭappaiyya, son of
Gangarasaiyya of Bilge, belonging to viśvamitra-gōtra, bōdhāyan-sūtra and yājus-śākha, renovated
the Mallikārjuna temple and made a gift of lands yielding 45 gadyāṇa (annually), after purchase
for 300 gadyāṇa for worship and offerings at the temple. The gift was made over to the maṭha
for proper management. The record gives details of the types of worship and offerings and the
related expenditure.

It is dated Śaka 1543, Durmati, Vaiśākha Śu. 14 [Tuesday] corresponding to 1621 A.D., April
24.

inscription.head:
No. 150.
(A.R. No. B. 502 of 1961-62.)
SRINGERI, SRINGERI TALUK, CHICKMAGALUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the base of a pillar in the enclosure of the Mallikārjuna temple.

It speaks of a worship rendered by Siddammāji daughter of Keḷadi Śivappa-nāyaka and
his wife Lingammāji, to the god Kaṁbada-Vinayakadēva of Śrīṅgapura (Śringēri).

It is dated Śaka 1606, Raktakshi, Māgha ba. 12, Friday corresponding to 1685 A.D.,
February, 20.

l.ka: raktākṣi saṁvatsarada māgha ba 12 yu śu-

inscription.head:
No. 151.
(A.R. No. B. 170 of 1962-63.)
ALAMPUR, ALAMPUR TALUK, MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar inside the Kumara-Brahma temple.
Refers to a gift, probably of the pillar, by the mother of Dāri Vallabha.

inscription.head:
No. 152.
(A.R. No. B. 630 of 1961-62.)
KERAVASE, KARKALA TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same slab [as in No. 147] in the Mahalingeśvara temple.

This stone slab with an inscription of 1546 A.D., [see No. 147 above] seems to have
contained an inscription of about the 8th century A.D. Very faint image of a few letters of
this period can be made out on the back side of this record. Probably due to lapse of time and
negligence the first engraving must have been damaged beyond recovery. Hence the slab has been
used for engraving the present record. [A few cases of this type have come to light in some parts
of Karnataka].

inscription.head:
No. 153.
(A.R. No. 7 of 1961-62.)
KANYATĪRTHAM (a hamlet of Pedda Doṇḍluru), JAMMALAMADAGU TALUK,
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Two sides of a fragment of a pillar in the Sundarēśvara Swamy temple.
Fragmentary and all details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 154.
(A.R. No. B. 8 of 1961-62.)
KANYATĪRTHAM (hamlet of Pedda Doṇḍluru), JAMMALMADUGU TALUK,
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the left side of the base of the image of Tripura-sundari in Sundarēśvara
swami temple.
Fragmentary and damaged. Seems to mention a person called Rēvaś(r)ma.

inscription.head:
No. 155.
(A.R. No. B. 547 of 1961-62.)
BALLATGI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup near the Mosque (right side.)

This highly damaged fragment mentions a person called Kalivarma and the purport
is not clear.
It is in characters of the 8th-9th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 156.
(A.R. No. B. 583 of 1961-62.)
KURAḌI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone near the maṭha.

This is a broken piece of a hero-stone. The purport of this inscription is not clear. It is
dated Śaka 784, 862 A.D. There is a mention of Ballaha (Ballāḷa (?)) and a Garuḍi (a woman
officer ?).

inscription.head:
No. 157.
(A.R. No. B. 537 of 1961-62.)
BALLATAGI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Broken slab lying in the field of Hanuma-seṭṭi.

It mentions a gāvuṇḍa [village officer], Pāpēśvara [a god ?] and Aivara [an assembly of
five ?] The record is incomplete. It is in characters of the 9th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 158.
(A.R. No. B. 159 of 1962-63.)
GAḌIGARĒVULA, NANDYAL TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar No. 3 in the main shrine of Bhōgēśvarasvāmin temple.
This damaged record refers to a preceptor (name lost) and his disciple Amaḷa Chandri.

inscription.head:
No. 159.
(A.R. No. B. 612 of 1961-62.)
SIRVĀR, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab at the foot of the hill.

It states that Ayyapa son of Kaṇṇabbe made a grant of 225 house sites each measuring
9 hands in with and 50 hands in length to the city named Ayyaṇa-Śrīpura. It is in characters
of 9th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 160.
(A.R. No. B. 177 of 1962-63.)
ALAMPUR, ALAMPUR TALUK, MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Fourth pillar in the same place, Bāla Brahmēśvara temple.

States that this is the image of Dantīśa-Nichchhakandarpa. It is in characters of the 9th
century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 161.
(A.R. No. B. 216 of 1962-63.)
TĀṆḌRAPĀḌU, GADWAL TALUK, MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in the temple of Satyamamba.

It refers to daṇḍayara (daṇḍanāyaka) Mābale(ya) and to Taṁḍūru. Rest of the information
is lost.

This badly damaged fragment is in characters of about 9th century A.D. In its date portion
only Āśhāḍamāsa, Sukla and Brihaspativāra (Thursday) can be read.

inscription.head:
No. 162.
(A.R. No. B. 533 of 1961-62.)
BĀGALAWĀḌ, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in a field out side the village.

It records the death of Bhima-tuṟuvaḷḷa, son of Sīya tuṟuvaḷḷa in a cattle raid. It is in
characters of the 9th-10th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 163.
(A.R. No. B. 816 of 1962-63.)
GŌGĒBĀḶ, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup near the temple of Basavaṇṇa.

It probably registered a gift of 50 mattar land and money [a portion of income from
siddhāya tax]. The beginning part of the slab are cut and lost. It is in characters of the 9th-10th
century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 164.
(A.R. No. B. 532 of 1961-62.)
BĀGALAWĀḌ, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup outside the village.

It records the death of a hero probably Kāmarayya in a fight. This damaged inscription
dated Śaka 826, Raktākshi 904-905 A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 165.
(A.R. No. B. 802 of 1962-63.)
KERUR, GULBARGA TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built into the wall of village ghūḍe.

The record is damaged and incomplete. It registers a grant of 96 mattar of land and six
gāṇa (oil-mills) to the god Nāgēśvara of Keṟeyūru probably by Nāga gāvuṇḍa.

It is dated Śaka 849 Sarvvadhāri, Phālguṇa Su. 7, Sunday, Rōhini-nakstra. It corresponds
with 929 A.D., February 19. But the week day was Thursday. Nakshatra Rōhini was current.

inscription.head:
No. 166.
(A.R. No. B. 589 of 1961-62.)
MALLIGIMAḌUVU, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup near the Iśvara temple.

It refers to the grants of land, 9 house sites and a garden to the main deity of the temple
by Puḷiyamma and the 32 (mahājanas) of Malligemaḍuvu. Probably the grant was made over to
Padumaṇabhaṭarāya. It mentions brati paṭṭa-Maṇisiva-Gorava. It also refers to a grant of 2 mattar
land to Nāgiyabbe.

In the date portion the cyclic year Rudhirōdagāri, the month Śrāvaṇa and Saturday are
mentioned. The details are insufficient for verification. It is in characters of the 10th century A.D.
The cyclic year Rudhirōdgari occurred twice in that century viz., Śaka 825 903 A.D. and Śaka 885
963 A.D., July and August.

l.ka:
• • • • • paḍuvatōṇṭa padumaṇabhaṭa rāyarige piriya • • • • • brati paṭṭa maṇisiva goravara
tāḻu[va] • • • • •

inscription.head:
No. 167.
(A.R. No. B. 174 of 1962-63.)
ALAMPUR, ALAMPUR TALUK, MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar near the navagraha in the Bāla-Brahmēśvara temple.

Badly damaged and purport not clear. It is dated Śaka [8]96 Bhāva, Bhādrapada Śu; rest
of the information is damaged. It may be equated with 974 A.D., August-September.

inscription.head:
No. 168.
(A.R. No. B. 590 of 1961-62.)
MALLIGIMAḌUVU, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying outside the village.

It seems to refer to [Ma]lligema[ḍuvu]. All other details are lost. It is in characters of the
10th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 169.
(A.R. No. B. 600 of 1961-62.)
MĀNVI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Fragment lying near the house of Tirakaṇṇa Akkasāli.

It seems to refer to a person by name Guṇḍa. Details of this fragmentary record are lost.
In the date portion only Śaka [9 • •] • • • • ba. 6 Monday can be made out. It is in characters
of the 10th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 170.
(A.R. No. B. 154 of 1962-63.)
GAḌIGARĒVULA, NANDYAL TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: First face of a pillar (No. 1) in the main Shrine of Bhōgēśvarasvāmin temple.
It reads Sarasa-Paramātma. It is in characters of the 10th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 171.
(A.R. No. B. 158 of 1962-63.)
GAḌIGARĒVULA, NANDYAL TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar No. 2 in the main shrine of Bhōgēśvarasvāmi temple.

It refers to Śrī Vyālasiṁha guru, disciple of Śrī Sakalasiṁha bhaṭṭāraka. It is in characters
of the 10th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 172.
(A.R. No. B. 171 of 1962-63.)
ALAMPUR, ALAMPUR TALUK, MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar outside the Kumāra-Brahma temple.
It reads Śrī-Kārava prachchandan. It is in characters of the 10th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 173.
(A.R. No. B. 172 of 1962-63.)
ALAMPUR, ALAMPUR TALUK, MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the rim of the pedestal of Śivalinga, Pātālēśvara shrine in the Bāla-Brahmēśvara temple.
It reads Om-Śrī-Śa[n*]kara-dhvajasya. It is in characters of the 10th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 174.
(A.R. No. B. 175 of 1962-63.)
ALAMPUR, ALAMPUR TALUK, MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar near the navagraha in the Bāla Brahmēśvara temple.
It refers to the daughter of Ēkānta-dēva. It is in characters of the 10th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 175.
(A.R. No. B. 776 of 1962-63.)
DHARWAR, DHARWAR TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Impression received from Dr. G.S. Dixit, Karnataka University, Dharwar-(2).

The purport of this badly damaged record is not clear. It is in the characters of the 10th
century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 176.
(A.R. No. B. 805 of 1962-63.)
SARAḌIGI, GULBARGA TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in front of the Hanuman temple.

It refers to the rule of a feudatory of Sāntaḷige-nāḍu a subdivision of Banavāsi-nāḍu and
records a grant of 50 mattar of land in Saravaḍigi, income from Sunday [Tax] collection and 5
house sites by the palace servant Neṟayaṇṇa of Sāntalige-nāḍu. Bankāpura is mentioned in this
record.
It is in characters of the 10th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 177.
(A.R. No. B. 807 of 1962-63.)
BASTIPURA, KOLLEGAL TALUK, MYSORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a rock on the hill outside the village.

It records the commemoration of the death of great ascetic Pushpaṇandi. It is in characters
of the 10th century A.D.

l.ka:
śiṣyē yōgya tamē nidhāya mahatīṁ vūḍhā[ṁ] svayaṁ śrīyasīṁ | cāritrasya dhuraṁ

malaṁ tapāṁsyācarēt ||

inscription.head:
No. 178.
(A.R. No. B. 808 of 1962-63.)
BASTIPURA, KOLLEGAL TALUK, MYSORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a rock on the hill outside the village.

It refers to the nishidhika of the jaina ascetic Pushpaṇandi and records the death [by
Sallekhana] of the muni Purimaṇḍala, a disciple of Pushapaṇandi muni. Two small lines on the
right side are indistinct.
It is in characters of the 10th century A.D.

l.ka: śrī ·atiśaya puṣṭaṇandimuni muktiyaneydidoḍaṁ niśidhikā pati[baḷame] citra-
