kīrtti kāśakaḷa

inscription.head:
No. 179.
(A.R. No. B. 827 of 1962-63.)
SIRWĀR, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a boulder at the foot of the hill.

It refers to an arrangement samaya-sthiti made by the two-hundred mahājana of Śrīvūra
for the regular payment of taxes such as vīsaka, dasaka and pañchaka. It stipulates that though
vīsaka and dasaka can be divided in terms of five, pañchaka should not be divided. If done one
has to pay three times the value [of paṇa] assessed as tax. It states that if the system is damaged
it is dangerous to the [good of the] village.
This record in characters of about the 10th century A.D.

l.ka: svasti śrī śrī vuradiṟnūrbba[r] māḍida samaya sthiti
daśaka vaneyduvudu daśakanteṟa tolage vīsakava neydugu pañcaka voḍeya salla
·ūraḻiya tetta kūḻu paṇavuntri guṇa ·āpāda samayadoḷtri guṇavanikki puguvo
·ī sthitiya naḻidāta svānagāḷdābha caṇḍāḷaṁ pemi meḻtu peṇḍi[rddā] gapilla [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 180.
(A.R. No. B. 828/830 (?) of 1962-63.)This is a misplaced estampage of 1962-63 collection kept in
neither 830 nor 828 and not noticed in the Annual Report for 1962-63.
SUNKĒŚVAR, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in the field known as Mullāna-hola.

It probably registered a grant of one mattar land, the details of which are lost. This broken
and very much damaged record is in about the 10th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 181.
(A.R. No. 78 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone, Museum No. 20.
Fragmentary and damaged. Purport not clear.

inscription.head:
No. 182.
(A.R. No. B. 824 of 1962-63.)
NUGḌŌṆI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup in front of the Basavēśvara temple.

It states that danḍanāyaka Chākimayya made a grant of 6 mattar of land to the god
Mahādēva of Biyāḷkaṟike.

It is dated Śaka 956 Bhāva, Śrā[vaṇa]. It may correspond with 1034 A.D., July 19 to August
16.

inscription.head:
No. 183.
(A.R. No. B. 209 of 1962-63.)
ŚANGAMĒŚVARAM [NEAR KŪDAVALLI VILLAGE], ALAMPUR TALUK,
MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Basement slab to the right of the Nandi maṇṭapa of Sangamēśvara temple.

It registers a grant of one baḷḷa (measurement) oil each for burning four perpetual lamps
before the god Sankēśvara by Rāja-Gurudēva heggaḍe Jōgabhaṭṭa who was governing Kūḍala-12
division. Ballavarasa is mentioned at the end.

It is dated Śaka 967, Tāraṇa. the cyclic year Tāraṇa occurred in Śaka 966 corresponding
to 1044 A.D.

edition.h4: TEXT. Published in Telañgāṇa Śāsanamulu Vol. II, p. 170, No. 13.

l.ka: sakavariṣa 967 tāra[ṇa*] saṁvatsarada śrāhiyoḷ śrīmat rāja-
saṁkēśvara dēvarige nāluku naṁdādīvigege diḷpaloṁdu baḷḷa

inscription.head:
No. 184.
(A.R. No. B. 113 of 1961-62.)
BŌDHAN, BODHAN TALUK, NIZAMABAD DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar [No. 2] on top of the Fort to the north of the old village.

The record is engraved on four sides of the pillar. This nishidhige inscription has the
praśasti of several Jaina munis. It states that Gaṇḍavīmukta bhaṭṭāraka, disciple of Nāganandi
bhaṭṭāraka who was himself the disciple of Nandi-siddhāntadēva attained samādhi by sanyasana
vidhi at Bahudhānya-nagara.

The date is mentioned in chronogram on the third face and letters on the fourth face of
the pillar with one difference.
(1) Pāvaka-rasa-nidhi Samkhya (Śaka 963) Vṟisha, Māgha Śu. 10.
(2) Śaka 963 Vṟisha, Pushya, Śu. 10.
The difference of one month may be explained as under.

The Jaina muni Gaṇḍavimukta bhaṭṭāraka took the owe of sanyasanavidhi on Śukla
dashami [Śu. 10] of the month Pushya and attained samādhi on Māgha, Śu. 10 of Śaka 963,
V®sha. The last ten lines of the text provide proper information to this effect. As a result it is
clear that the muni performed the vrata for one full month to attain samadhi.

The given two tithis correspond to:
(1) 1042 A.D., January, 4 [Monday].
(2) 1041 A.D., December 5, [Saturday].

l.note: The letterga is written in small size.

inscription.head:
No. 185.
(A.R. No. B. 603 of 1961-62.)
MARCHĒḌA, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in the doḍḍi.

This damaged record probably refers to a grant and mentions a mahāsāmanta (name lost),
Nanni-Chaḷuki and Bīvayya.

It is dated Śaka 96[7] Vaḍḍavāra. The available information is insufficient for verification.
However, the Śaka year corresponds to 1045 A.D. The cyclic year was Pārthiva.

inscription.head:
No. 186.
(A.R. No. B. 116 of 1961-62.)
BŌDHAN, BODHAN TALUK, NIZAMABAD DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Four sides of a pillar lying in the Inspection Bungalow.

The fourth face of the pillar (No. 27) seems to contain an independent inscription. It is
very much damaged, hence much of the information is lost. It begins with imprecatory verses and
seems to have recorded some grants, the details of which are lost.

It contains no date but palaeographically assignable to the period of the previous record
[No. 27] 1056 A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 187.
(A.R. No. B. 182 of 1962-63.)
CHENNIPĀḌU, ALAMPUR TALUK, MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Lintel of the garbhagriha of the Kēśavasvāmi temple.

It registers a grant of land and a garden for the worship and offerings to the God
Śrī Vishṇudēva the image of which got prepared and installed by perggaḍe Kōsanamayya.

It is dated Śaka 988, Parābhava, Phālguṇa, Śu. 13, Thursday corresponding to 1067 A.D.,
March 1.

l.ka: svasti sakavariṣa 988 neya parābhava saṁvatsarada phālguṇa sudda trayōdasi bṛ-
hasvativāradaṁdu śrī perggaḍe kōsanamayyaṁ māḍisi pratiṣṭemāḍi-
da śrī viṣṇudēvara pūje nivēdyakke biṭṭa nelaṁ matta 12 yoṁdu tōṁṭa [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 188.
(A.R. No. B. 114 of 1961-62.)
BŌDHAN, BODHAN TALUK, NIZAMABAD DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar [No. 3] on the top of the Fort to the north of the old village.

This nishidhige inscription carries an eulogy of the jaina muni Chandraprabha-ācharya and
records his death by samādhividhi.

It is dated in chronogram-Śara-randhra-anka Śaka 995, Paridhāvi, Bhādrapada, Krishṇāshṭami,
Sūrivāsara. It corresponds to 1072 A.D., September 8, (Saturday).

inscription.head:
No. 189.
(A.R. No. 83 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar (No. 4) dug out from the main street in the village.

It records the gift of one mattar of land to the brāhmaṇa Śrīrayya by mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Sangamarasa who bears among others the epithet lattalūrapuravarēśvara.

It is dated Śaka 996, Ānanda, Uttarāyana Sankrānti. This may correspond to 1074 A.D.,
December 23, [Tuesday].

inscription.head:
No. 190.
(A.R. No. B. 611 of 1961-62.)
SIRVĀR, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a boulder near the doṇi on the hill.

This very much damaged record, registers a grant of land by an individual whose name
is lost. It mentions perggaḍe Māramayya and the administrative division Mosaḷikal-300.

There is a mention of piriya-kōlu the measuring rod. It is dated Śaka 998, Naḷa, Pushya,
Śu. 3 corresponding to 1076 A.D., December 1, Thursday.

l.ka:
• • • • • • • • • • sakavarṣa 998 neya naḷa saṁvatsarada puṣya suddha 3 • • • • •
• • • • •

• • • • • • • • • • (vo)ladalli taḷavṛttiyāgi sarvanamasyamāgi piriya kōloḷkoṭṭa
kariya nela

inscription.head:
No. 191.
(A.R. No. 61 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a pillar in the museum with in the compound of the Sōmēśvara temple. First pillar
(Museum No. 81).

It registers a gift of forty goats for a perpetual lamp to the god Sōmēśvaradēva of
Koḷḷipāke by mahāmandaḷēśvara Eṟuve Toṇḍarasa, Dēmarasa and Kāmaṇa.

This record has been engraved as a continuation of No. 34 and is dated Śaka 999, Pingala,
Jyeshtha, śu. 13, Monday. It corresponds to 1077 A.D., May 8., Jyeshṭha month was adhika.

l.note: Stops here.

inscription.head:
No. 192.
(A.R. No. B. 186 of 1962-63.)
GUNDIMALLA, ALAMPUR TALUK, MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In front of Śiva temple.

The top portion of the slab is broken and lost. As a result much of the information at
the beginning is lost. The record registers the gift of Saṟiyūr together with the income from the
taxes like manevaṇa and poledeṟe for the merit of Govindaradēva and Kētabbarasi for offerings,
repairs, feeding of the ascetics and the students in the temple (name lost). The gift was made over
to Kāḷēśvara paṇḍita of lākuḷa sect.

The record is dated in [Chalukya Vi]krama year 7, Dundubhi. It corresponds to [Śaka
1004] 1082 A.D.

l.ka: dhana vidyārththigaḷa • • • • • • • • • • yūraṁ gōvindara dēvagaṁ kētabbara-
nīramāramaṇar lākuḷa siddhānta sarōvara jaḷakēḷi vinōdānanda bṛṁdārakarāja-
haṁsar gaurīpati padāravinda makaranda parimaḷa svādānanda madhukarappa śrī vādī-
bhava vajrāṁkuśa kaḷēśvara paṇḍita dēvargge dhārapūrvvakadiṁ sarvvanamasya māḍi koṭṭa dha-
rmmamentendare [|*] rājarasa debbarasa rācaladēvi candiyabbarasiyara manevaṇa pōle-
deṟe sādavāpu kāṇikeyoḷagāgi samastavāda teṟeyellamaṁ sarvvanamasya-
māgi saṟiyūraṁ biṭṭu | ·ācandrārkkaṁbaraṁ koṭṭa sāsana | ·idharmmamaṁ pūṇḍūra-
luṁ vāraṇāsiyaluṁ śrīparbatadaluṁ sāsira kavileyumaṁ sāsirvvaru

inscription.head:
No. 193.
(A.R. No. B. 217 of 1962-63.)
CHINTALAGHĀṬ, ZAHEERABAD TALUK, MEDAK DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone kept on the road side from Chitalaghāṭ to Chilamāmiḍi about a mile to the east.

This damaged record registers a grant to the Kotta jinālaya (of Chiṁguniti) by mahāsāmanta
Kaddarasa after laving the feet of Mādhava Chandra Śiddhāntadēva.

The record is dated in Chalukya Vikrama Varsha [6], Dundubhi Pushya, śu. 14 Thursday
corresponding to 1083 A.D., January, 5, Thursday.

inscription.head:
No. 194.
(A.R. No. 1 of 1961-62.)
ANANTAPUR, ANANTAPUR TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab fixed into the front wall of the Museum (Library).

Fragmentary mentions the name of a person called Chōrayya and refers to a grant
probably made by Chōrayya.
It is in characters of the 11th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 195.
(A.R. No. 25 of 1961-62.)
VĒMULAVĀḌA, SIRSILLA TALUK, KARIMNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the compound of the Rājēśvara temple opposite to Rāmā's shrine.

This is a damaged and incomplete record. The purport of the epitaph is not clear and the
names (Sā)simayya and Śāntībhatāra can be made out.

In the date potion only the first two figures-98 can be made out. It belongs to the 11th
century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 196.
(A.R. No. 51 of 1961-62.)
ĀRŪR, SANGAREDDI TALUK, MEDAK DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Fragment of a slab in the field of Santayyagāri Sangayya.

It seems to refer to a gift (details lost) made to Ṇarāsījiyya, probably by Biṭṭayarasa and
seems to refer to the latter's camp at Sindinīra.
This borken and incomplete record is in characters of the 11th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 197.
(A.R. No. 53 of 1961-62.)
PAṬAÑCHERU, SANGAREDDI TALUK, MEDAK DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built into the southern gate of jamā masjid.

Top portion of the slab is broken and lost. It refers to a tax free gift (of land) situated
in Poṭṭaḷakeṟe to the god (name lost) for dēvabōga.
It is in characters of the 11th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 198.
(A.R. No. 56 of 1961-62.)
VELTŪRU, SANGAREDDI TALUK, MEDAK DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built into the wall of Āñjanēya temple.

The top and bottom portions of this record are broken and lost. It mentions mahāpradhāna
Pampa-permānaḍi, his wife Jakkaladēvi and Poṭṭalakere. All other information are lost. Jakkaladēvi
is described as dānachintāmaṇi.
It is in characters of the 11th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 199.
(A.R. No. 61(?) of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a pillar in the museum with in the compound of the Sōmēśvara temple. First pillar
Museum No. 81.

This record though engraved on the other (fourth) face of No. 191 has not been noticed
in the Annual Report for the year 1961-62.

The top and bottom portion of the record are damaged and the information there in
are lost. The extant portion carries a sting of epithets of mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara Kāṁbaya. Mentions
(Cheru) Bemarasa of Koḷḷipāka.
On paleographical grounds the record can be ascribed to the same period as in No. 191.

inscription.head:
No. 200.
(A.R. No. 75 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the dhvaja staṁbha in front of the Sōmēśvara temple.

Records the renovation of the temples in Koḷḷipāka and the setting up of the makara
tōraṇa by Gosaraṇārya who claims to have obtained the fiefdom of Koḷḷipāke and the office of
the daṇḍādhīśvara from Simhachakrēśvara. Sēnabōva Chāvuṇḍamayya wrote the record.
The record is not dated and is in characters of about the 11th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 201.
(A.R. No. B. 112 of 1961-62.)
BŌDHAN, BODHAN TALUK, NIZAMABAD DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar [No. 1] on top of the Fort to the north of the old village.

Records the death by samādhividhi of Śubhanandi, disciple of Dēvēndra Siddhānta-
munīśvara. It is not dated but palaeographically assignable to about the 11th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 202.
(A.R. No. B. 460 of 1961-62.)
DHARMAPURI, MOMINABAD TALUK, BHIR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Impression received from Dy. Dir. Arch., Aurangabad, Maharashtra.

This fragmentary inscription probably recorded a grant, the details of which is lost.
It contains only imprecatory section. It mentions yāpanīya. The order of a yati (probably
belonging to yāpanīya Sangha) mentioned as Lakshmivaranidhi guṇa vīra vraṭīṁdra caused the
inscription (of a grant) to be engraved (on stone).

The record was composed by Īśvara Bhaṭṭa. The record is in characters of the 11th century
A.D.

l.ka: • • • • • ttaṁ vāyō harēti vasuṁdharāṁ | ṣaṣṭi[r]vvaruṣa saha • • •
• rādibhiḥ yasya yasya yadā bhūmi stasyatasya • • • • •
svaṁ dātu sameha(tu)kyaṁ dukha manyasya pāḷanaṁ | dānapā • • •
gaḷaṁ bādhaṁ tāvene dharmmamaṁ kiḍisuva vyādhaṁge kēḷsaṁtati (ya)
ra pāruvara piriyara kālgarcci biṭṭa dēvasva manōrvvaregōḷva

inscription.head:
No. 203.
(A.R. No. B. 461 of 1961-62.)
DHARMAPURI, MOMINABAD TALUK, BHIR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Impression received from Dy. Dir. Arch., Aurangabad, Maharashtra.

At the beginning the various merchant groups belonging to the guild the Five hundred
Svāmis of Baṇajigas have been mentioned and are eulogized. The way in which Indrasēna,
Rudrasēna and Bhadrasēna are euologised in the record is interesting. They are stated to be
dharmapālādhipati (protectors of dharma) and probably belonging to Haihaya family. It records
a grant of income from various taxes (specified) for the worship of the god and feeding of
ascetics by the paṁcha-paṭṭaṇa of Pottalakere, the Kaṁchagaras and Teluṅga-nagaras. The grant
was entrusted to Mahāvīra-paṇḍita of Yāpanīya-Saṁgha and Vamdiyūrgaṇa, the āchārya of
Be[ma]ki seṭṭiya basadi of Dharmapura. The place names Kōgaḷi, Banavase, Baḷḷigave and Veḷugave
(?) are mentioned here. At the beginning a few lines are lost and the record stops abruptly.
The record is in characters of the 11th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 204.
(A.R. No. B. 462 of 1961-62.)
DHARMAPURI, MOMINABAD TALUK, BHIR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Impression received from the Dy. Dir. Arch., Maharashtra, Aurangabad.

It recorded probably a land grant (to a temple ?) with a house and a well. The names
of the donor and donee are lost. The five hundred svāmis of a guild probably from Dēvakadere
can be made out. This highly damaged record is in characters of 11th century A.D.

l.ka: lū • • sēna bōva • • • • • • • • • • bāva • • • ma • • di

inscription.head:
No. 205.
(A.R. No. B. 463 of 1961-62.)
DHARMAPURI, MOMINABAD TALUK, BHIR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Panel on the outer wall of the Hēmādpanti temple.
This fragmentary record contains the name of a person (probably) Vikramānka.
It is in characters of the 11th century A.D.

l.ka: svasti śrī guru(bhyō)namaḥ nārāyaṇa • (yamanu) • riha diṁdēḻita dvi[tta] • •
ra sānu prāṁta śāsvallaḷita padakadoḷvikramāṁkaṁ kalyāṇa tara • •
māgiḻdaṁka muḷāvaḷiyane palavuṁ [ca] (pa?) riyiṁ kīrtti suttaṁ • śidaha • •

inscription.head:
No. 206.
(A.R. No. B. 464 of 1961-62.)
PRINCE OF WALES MUSEUM, BOMBAY TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the pedestal of the sculpture of Umā-Mahēśvara.
[Find spot: Gadag, Gadag Taluk and District].
[Impression from the Director of the Museum].

It states that the sculpture of Uma-Mahēśvara was got prepared by Rēvakabbarasi, the
wife of Vāvaṇarasa who is described as prachaṇḍa daṇḍanāyaka.
It is in characters of the 11th century A.D.

l.ka: svasti śrī matpracaṇḍa daṇḍanāyakaṁ vāvaṇarasara manneyā

inscription.head:
No. 207.
(A.R. No. B. 525 of 1961-62.)
NĀGANAHAḶḶI, GULBARGA TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in a field called sērīhola.
This fragmentary record reads tōṁṭada matta 6 [six mattar extent of garden].
It is in characters of the 11th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 208.
(A.R. No. B. 607 of 1961-62.)
RĀJOḶḶI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built into the wall of agasi.
This broken piece is totally damaged and only Puligeṟe can be made out of it.
It is in characters of the 11th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 209.
(A.R. No. B. 617 of 1961-62.)
BIJŪR, COONDAPURA TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Two sides of a slab in the Hādi-Paramēśvari temple.

This damaged record seems to register a grant of land to a person probably in
connection with water management. It mentions God Kumara Swamy, the place Bijavūra, two
samantas and a nāḍa-dhore. In the imprecatory section we find the mention of a hundred-agnihōtri-
nūrbar-agnihōtrigaḷ.

inscription.head:
No. 210.
(A.R. No. B. 741 of 1962-63.)
PRINCE OF WALES MUSEUM, BOMBAY TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A broken slab.

Rim of a bowl made of greyschist. Impression from the Director.
Findspot: Gandhāra West Pakistan.

This fragmentary and damaged record refers to a grant for worship and offerings to the
god Mallikārjuna. There is a mention of the place Bāḍa.
It is in characters of the 11th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 211.
(A.R. No. B. 770 of 1962-63.)
BIDAR, BIDAR TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Fragment of a slab kept in the office of the Conservation Assistant in the Fort.

It contains a few words of the imprecatory portion of the totally lost record. It is in
characters of about the 11th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 212.
(A.R. No. B. 771 of 1962-63.)
BIDAR, BIDAR TALUK, BIDAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Three faces of a broken slab kept in the same place [No. 211/A.R. No. 770].

This fragmentary record commences with the imprecatory verses and seems to refer to a
gift of land the details of which are lost. It is in the characters of the 11th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 213.
(A.R. No. B. 784 of 1962-63.)
HARSŪR, GULBARGA TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Three fragments lying near the Parvatadēva temple (3).

This third piece is also broken and is incomplete. It refers to Bhāskara seṭṭi and his
construction [of a temple] and installation of the images of gods there in.
It is in characters of the 11th century A.D.

edition.h4: TEXT. The beginning, end and the two sides of the slab are broken and lost. The record is

inscription.head:
No. 214.
(A.R. No. B. 477 of 1961-62.)
BASTIHAḶḶI (Near Haḷebīd), BELUR TALUK, HASSAN DISTRICT.
inscription.p: [Photograph from Conservation Asst., Bangalore].

This fragmentary epitaph records the construction of a jinālaya (basadi) by Dāsi-seṭṭi
disciple of Maladhāri-dēva.
It is in characters of the 11th-12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 215.
(A.R. No. 40 of 1961-62.)
GAṄGĀPURAM, MAHABUBNAGAR TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup in front of the Chennakēśva temple.

Top portion of the slab is broken and lost. This incomplete record contains eulogies
of Govinda daṇḍādhipa and Dēva daṇḍadhipa. Seems to refer to a gift made to Paḍale and
Bammayya by Dēvapaya daṇḍanāyaka and the seṭṭikāṟas.
It is in characters of the 12th century A.D.

l.ka: vaṇnipudoḍamartthiya • • • jaga • • ḷaṁ parasai • • • • • dbha yaśōbhirāmana ||
radiṁ gōviṁda daṁḍādhipa natuḷabaḷaṁ daṁḍādhipaṁ digvaramaṁ parvva[lva] •
cārada mahime bhayabhrāṁntiyaṁ vairigaḷu gāvagaviyalu ta[kkai]

l.note: The record seems to have ended here abruptly.

inscription.head:
No. 216.
(A.R. No. B. 509 of 1961-62.)
BHAṄKŪR, CHITAPUR TALUK, GULBARGA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Fragment lying in the Liṅgāyat hostel.

This fragment contains only the date portion referring to Chālukya-Vikrama year 30, • • •
• • • Vaiśākha, su. 11, Thursday corresponding to 1106 A.D., April 15, [Sunday]. [The Śaka and cyclic
years were 1028 and Vyaya respectively].

inscription.head:
No. 217.
(A.R. No. 66 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the Sōmēśvara temple. Fourth pillar Museum No. 84.

This record is engraved at the end of No. 66 above. It states that Malli-seṭṭi son of
Balli-seṭṭi made a gift of eight gadyaṇās for a perpetual lamp to the god Svayambhu Sōmanātha
of Kollipāka for the merit of Māyikabbe.

This undated record may be ascribed to the same date of the previous record [No. 66]
1109 A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 218.
(A.R. No. 95 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALAGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a broken pillar lying in the main street.

This fragmentary record seems to have contained a genealogical account of the Chālukya
family. It refers to mahādānās such as guḍaparvata, śarkaraparvata, lavaṇaparvata and gōsahasra.
Also, it refers to an officer Kānadēva who bears the epithets antahpura verggaḍe, mahāpradhāna,
kannada-sandhivigrahi and daṇḍanāyaka. In the post script Appēśvara-dēva is mentioned.

It is dated in the Chalukya Vikrama year 3(1), Virōdhi, Akshaya (tritīya) which is irregu-
lar. If the given Chalukya Vikrama varsha 3(1) is a mistake for 34 then the cyclic year Virōdhi
holds good [with Śaka 1031] and the given tithi corresponds with 1109 A.D., April 5, Monday.

l.ka: maṁgaḷa mahā śrī śrī śrī śrī [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 219.
(A.R. No. B. 764 of 1962-63.)
GORṬA, BHALKI TALUK, BIDAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built into the platform of Harijana-Chāvaḍi.

It registers a gift of one load of millets daily for offerings to the god Kuṁbhēra and oil
for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple by sēnabōva Gangarasa and sāvāsi Māṇikabhaṭṭa, the
perggaḍe of Bhaṭṭavēnṭheya dandanāyaka sāvāsi Pommaṇayya.

The record is dated in Chālukya Vikrama year 37, Nandana Pushya, Śu. 4, Monday,
Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkramaṇa. It corresponds to 1112 A.D., December 24, Tuesday. The Sankramana was
on 23, Monday but the tithi was śu. 3.

inscription.head:
No. 220.
(A.R. No. B. 765 of 1962-63.)
GORṬA, BHALKI TALUK, BIDAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in front of the Hanuman temple.

It registers a gift of two drammas (money) per month for the gandha (sandal paste) to
the god Rudrēśvara.

It stipulates that this dharma should be maintained by any person who becomes the of-
ficer of the temple from time to time. The slab is broken on the left side.
The date of the record and the doners are being the same as in No. 217 [A.R. 764].

inscription.head:
No. 221.
(A.R. No. B. 468 of 1961-62.)
HOṬṬAL, DEGLUR TALUK, NANDED DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Interior parapet below the kakshāsana on the right side of the mukha-maṇḍapa of the Nandi
temple within the Mahādēva temple.
Impression from the Superintendent, South Western Circle, Aurangabad.

Records a gift to the god Svayambhu Rebbēśvara by Bīrarasa and Aṁkapayya of Poṭṭaḷa-
Madanūru at the instance of Kāḷaya-nāyaka.

It is dated in Chalukya Vikrama era 45, Śārvvari, Āśvīja, Amāvāsye, Solar eclipse
corresponding to 1120 A.D., October 24, [Sunday]. There was Solar eclipse on that day.

l.ka: svasti śrīmaccāḷukya vikrama varṣada 45 neya sārvva-
dārāpūrvvaka māḍi biṭṭaru || maṁgaḷa mahā śrī śrī

inscription.head:
No. 222.
(A.R. No. 92 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALAGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a four faces of a pillar in the tank behind the Sōmēśvara temple [No. 81].

This is another record engraved on another face of the same pillar (as above). Though
seems to be a continuation of the (No. 81) above record this has been engraved as an independent
inscription.

The record is undated but palaeographically and on the basis of mention of Kēśirāja who
is also mentioned in the previous (No. 81) record it can be assigned to the same date as in that
record viz., 1125 A.D., March 24.

This is a jaina record and contains prasastis of several jaina munis such as Mēghachandra-
maḷadhāri, Padmanandi, Padmaprabha, Mēghachandra and Mādhavēndu. These munis belonged to
Krāṇūrggaṇa and Mēshapāshāṇa-Gachcha.

Kēśirāja who is described as the lord of this jinaśāsana protected by Yakshi got erected
a mānastambha and constructed a makara-tōraṇa.

inscription.head:
No. 223.
(A.R. No. B. 767 of 1962-63.)
GORṬA, BHALKI TALUK, BIDAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar setup in the field of Mahadevappa Kaṇakaṭe.

It records the death of the jaina muni Tribhuvana-sēna-Siddhāntadēva who is highly praised.
The famous jaina centre Kopaṇagiri is mentioned in the record. [No. 236/A.R. 762 below].

The record is dated in Bhūlōkavarsha 5 Sādhāraṇa, Śravaṇa, ba. 7, Monday corresponding
to 1130 A.D., July 28, Monday [Śaka 1052].

inscription.head:
No. 224.
(A.R. No. B. 488 of 1961-62.)
KHADAKLĀṬ, CHIKKODI TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built into the front wall of the Jaina Basadi.

It begins with a very long eulogy of Śrī-madaśēsha-mahājanas of śrī-madagrahāram Laṭṭiya
and followed by another eulogy of various merchant guilds (named). It registers a gift of income
from levies on several goods and pulses and even of sale of sesam, salt, cotton and horse for the
repairs and maintenance of the fort and the ditch of agrahāra Laṭṭi and a gift of 20 buffaloes
to the god Haṁchikēśvara of the same place by the Five-hundred svamis of Ayyāvaḷe, the 1700
Gavares, the Mummuridaṇḍas, Ubhaya-nānādēsis, the sixteen of 8 nādus, the people of Nālku [Four]
paṭṭaṇa etc., who had all assembled as mahānādu [the assembly probably of agrahāra Laṭṭi].

It is dated in the cyclic year Jaya, Mārgaśira Śu. 5, Friday. Probably it corresponds to 1174
A.D., November 1, Friday [Śaka 1096].

l.ka: yājanādhyayanadhyāpana dāna pratigrahaṣaṭkarmma niya(ra)taruṁ sapta sōmā-
janaṁgaḷu samastabhuvana vikhyāta paṁcaśatavīraśāsana labdhanēka guṇagaṇāḷaṁkṛta satya-

aṣṭā-

bhiyyasya yasya tadāsiddhi stasya tasya tadā phaḷaṁ || śrī śrī śrī

inscription.head:
No. 225.
(A.R. No. 58 of 1961-62.)
KŌHIR, ZAHEERABAD TALUK, MEDAK DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built as a step to the shop of Irasangappa in the bazaar.

The record engraved on two faces of the slab is worn out. The purport of the extant
portion is not clear.
It is in characters of the 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 226.
(A.R. No. 77 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the pedestal of a sculpture of a hero riding on an elephant back-Museum No. 8.

This fragmentary inscription seems to record the death of the mahout (māvanta) Aṇṇayya
and probably others in a fight.
It is in characters of the 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 227.
(A.R. No. 88 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALAGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar [No. 9] dugout from the main street in the village.

This fragmentary and damaged record seems to refer to a gift (details lost) for offerings
probably to a temple.
It is in characters of the 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 228.
(A.R. No. 100 of 1961-62.)
KOLANUPĀKA, BHUVANAGIRI TALUK, NALAGONDA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the compound of the Jaina Basadi.
The record reads Śrī Pushpasēnadēva. It is in the characters of the 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 229.
(A.R. No. B. 117 of 1961-62.)
TEKKALI, TEKKALI TALUK, SRIKAKULAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Fragment kept in the compound of the Rājāsāhib's palace.

Fragment, purport not clear, mentions probably a person by name Malla. It is in characters
of the 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 230.
(A.R. No. B. 480 of 1961-62.)
DŌṆAWĀD, CHIKKODI TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone by the side of the Kalamēśvara temple.

This badly wornout and fragmentary record intended to register a grant to a temple by
the village head gāmuṇḍa. It is in characters of the 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 231.
(A.R. No. B. 486 of 1961-62.)
KERŪR, CHIKKODI TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Fragment built into the wall of the Bharamappa temple.

The slab, of which both the top and bottom portions are broken and lost. It contains the
eulogy of a person (name lost) who is described as highly learned. There is a reference to Kau-
mudī mahōtsava.
It is in characters of the 12th century A.D.

• janita puḷakita gātra sakaḷakaḷāprāgalbhya sukavijana cakōrabriṁdapari • •

tamṛdu • yaninādā karṇnano • rṇna rāgō dayābhirāma

inscription.head:
No. 232.
(A.R. No. B. 519 of 1961-62.)
SAṄGAI, CHITAPUR TALUK, GULBARGA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Three fragments in Harijana-vāḍa.

The purport of the three fragments is not clear. Only the below words can be made out
from them.
Fragment-1-Vasishṭa, Rāmadēva.
-2-Paṁḍitā, Nāyaka.
-3-Krōda, Vi(dya).
It is in characters of the 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 233.
(A.R. No. B. 571 of 1961-62.)
KAPGAL, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Fragment near the Jod-Īśvara temple.

The slab is broken on all sides. It seems to record a gift of land probably to a temple.
It is in characters of about the 12th century A.D.

l.note: Letter Li is written below the line.

inscription.head:
No. 234.
(A.R. No. B. 580 of 1961-62.)
KURAḌI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On four stone beams in the ceiling of the temple known as Nelaguḍi.

This damaged writing with no beginning and end on four beams seems to contain an
eulogy of a person called Madhusūdhan of the Kasyapa-gotra and Karnāṭā-vamśa.
It is in characters of the 12th century A.D.

l.ka:
• • • • • • • • • • jjana saṁstuta • daya manuttama pa • darpabīraṁ madhuvarājana
[na]ltadoḍuttarōttaraṁ śamedame rūpu • • • • • • • • • •

• • • • • • • • • • śītakaranaṁtu mṛdaṁge kumārageṁtu śakrarige jayantaneṁtu raghuvige raghūdbhava
nōtu (parimaḷa) • • • • • • • • • •

• • • • • • • • • • (sa) nārjjisidante vāsudēva(ne)ge tanūjanāgi madhusūdhana nārjjisidanegaḷte
yaṁ • • • • • • • • • •

·anavadyākṛtiyiṁ prabhutva guṇadiṁ satyōptayiṁ • • • • • ganamaśuddhā caritradiṁ vibhuvasu •
• • • • •
• • sakaḷa nētrādhāranāgiṟda mā • • • diṁ dhātrige vāsudēvanesedaṁ karṇnāṭa vaṁśōdbhavaṁ ||

vipuḷa śrīstanakuṁkumāṁkita viśēśā • kanmāḷaṁ bhāratīlapanāḷōkana darppaṇaṁ nija-
yaśōvalli vṛtāśāmadadvipa saṁdōhanudātta
(pa)ntāḻdaṁ mikka kāśyapa gōtrammadhusūdhanaṁ parahita vyāpāra pārāyaṇaṁ [||]

inscription.head:
No. 235.
(A.R. No. B. 581 of 1961-62.)
KURAḌI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying near the temple of Basavaṇṇa.

This highly damaged and effaced record mentions one Ravideva who, it seems, was a lāḻa
sandhivīgrahi. There is a long description of his family. The beginning and end portions of the
record are broken and lost. The purport of it is not clear.
It is in characters of the 12th century A.D.

edition.p: [The sketch of a dagger is there pointing down; the top of it is cut].

l.ka:
vida nō(dadeyalu) • • mādanutīta • • • • • • • • • • pa vismaya • • tadvidagra • • •
• • • • • • • • • •

vidita sakaḷāgamārttha padamaḻatara kīrtti • • • • • • • • • • taḥ || • • • • • targhāvadā
• • • • • • • • • •

inscription.head:
No. 236.
(A.R. No. B. 762 of 1962-63.)
GORṬA, BHALKI TALUK, BIDAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Right niche in the maṇḍapa of the Mahādēva temple.

This damaged fragment record states that Chinni seṭṭi and Bāchaṇa, sons of Hammikabbe
and disciple of Tribhuvanasēna Siddhā[nta]dēva got the image of the goddess (name lost) made.
[No. 223/A.R. No. 767 above].
It is in characters of the 12th century A.D.

l.ka: © śrīmatribhuvanasēna si(ddhā)ṁtadēva • • • • • tu || vinaya nidhi vardhamāna

inscription.head:
No. 237.
(A.R. No. B. 763 of 1962-63.)
GORṬA, BHALKI TALUK, BIDAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Left niche in the maṇḍapa of the Mahādēva temple.

The record states that Rēvaseṭṭi and Jinnaṇa, sons of Basaviseṭṭi and his wife Lokkaṇabbe
and disciple of Muni-Suvrata-Siddhāntadēva got the image of goddess Padmāvati made.
It is in characters of the 12th century A.D.

l.ka: śrī muni suvratasiddhāṁtadēvara guḍḍaru • • • ya māgire padmāvatiyaṁ
māḍisidariṁtu basavi seṭṭiyanegaḷda sati lokkaṇabbe yātmaja (rati)

inscription.head:
No. 238.
(A.R. No. B. 768 of 1962-63.)
GORṬA, BHALKI TALUK, BIDAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar setup in the field of Mahadēvappa Kaṇakaṭe.

This damaged fragmentary record probably intends to state the death of Singiseṭṭi
through the Jaina Vow (sallēkhana) under the guidance of a jaina muni [Bhadrabāhu ?]. Probably
Vardhamāna composed this record.

The record is dated Vikramāva, [Vi]ḷambi, Chaitra • dige• It is not certain that Vikrama
refers to Chālukya-Vikrama Varsha. The cyclic year Viḷambi occurred twice 1) 1118 A.D., & 2)
1178 during the 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 239.
(A.R. No. B. 777 of 1962-63.)
DHARWAR, DHARWAR TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Impression received from Dr. G.S. Dixit, Karnataka University, Dharwar-(3)
Findspot: Bid (?) Kolhapur.

This fragmentary record refers to Bijja-bhūbuja and mentiones the death of Bonnama-
nāyaka in a battle.
It is in the characters of 12th century A.D.

l.ka:
© nija pata nō(ṁ)ḍāṁ maṇḍaḷika gaṇḍaradēva naḍurttha bijjabhūbhuja gaja marōṟutta
ṟipusiṁha parākrama • • ṁdāḷu vijaya va • •

priyaṁ negaḻda bhaṇḍa • bonnama nāyaka ripurvvaja nibhayaṁkaraṁ śubhadiśā bharaṇa [yu]
danindraśēkaraṁ svaga ṟe • śā • • [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 240.
(A.R. No. B. 788 of 1962-63.)
HARSŪR, GULBARGA TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup against the wall inside the Jaina temple.

This Chalukya record is plastered with cement. Hence nothing can be made out of it.
There is a reference to a shaṭ-karma-nirata ascetic [probably Bāḷachaṁdra] and to a ruler described
as Kalyaṇa-pura-varādīśvara, lord of the city of Kalyāṇa. The record seems to have registered
some kind of grants probably to the ascetic made by the king or his feudatory officer. A few
letters on the right side of the slab free from cement plaster, which can be made out, are noticed
here.
It is in characters of the 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 241.
(A.R. No. B. 789 of 1962-63.)
HARSŪR, GULBARGA TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone in front of the Karibasappa Temple.

This hero-stone has a sculptured panel depicting a hero fighting against a hero sitting on
an elephant back. Probably the hero succeeded in killing both the elephant and the enemy on
it. But at the end he succumbed to the wounds in the battle field. On top and bottom of the
sculptured panel there are two panels containing writing, but damaged very much. It states that
the hero probably Haṁniya died after piercing [the enemy and the elephant] to death.
It is in characters of 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 242.
(A.R. No. B. 795 of 1962-63.)
JĪVAṆAGI, GULBARGA TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab fixed into the floor of the Hanuman temple.
This fragmentary record contains only the imprecatory portion.
It is in characters of the 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 243.
(A.R. No. B. 810 of 1962-63.)
AMARĀVATI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup near the Īśvara temple.

This record seems to mention some jaina teachers but completely worn out and only svasti
can be made out.
It is in characters of the 12th century.

inscription.head:
No. 244.
(A.R. No. B. 483 of 1961-62.)
KABBŪR, CHIKKODI TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar in the Kalamēśvara temple.

The record mentions mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara Kartīvīrya-devarasa ruling from the capital
Veḷugrama. It registers a gift of money derived from the sale of horses to the god svayambhu-
dēvachakravarti Kalidēvasvāmi of piriya agrahāra Kabbūr by the Hēḷāvida (?) headed by Tik-
kayya, Anatuma-nāyaka and Hollaya-nāyaka and others.

The record is dated saka 1126, Dundubhi. The Śaka year 1126 donot correspond with the
cyclic year Dundubhi. The cyclic year Raktākshi occurred in Śaka 1126 and that of Dundubhi in
Śaka 1124.

l.ka: svayaṁbhu dēva cakravarti kalidēvasvāmi dēvargge śrī
ṣṭa hēḷāvidaru māṟida kudurevoṁdake biṭṭāya paṇa

inscription.head:
No. 245.
(A.R. No. B. 606 of 1961-62.)
RĀJOḶḶI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in the field of Gaṅgappa.
It records a gift of land to the god Gargēśvara by Kasa-gavuḍa.

It is dated in the cyclic year Vishu, Māgha ba. 11, Friday. The given tithi may correspond
to 1282 A.D., February 6.

inscription.head:
No. 246.
(A.R. No. 52 of 1961-62.)
PAṬAÑCHERU, SANGAREDDI TALUK, MEDAK DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Fragment of a slab built into Shamsuddin's well behind the Ashruf Khāna.

The record, a broken piece, refers to the God Rāmanāthadēva, the city Poṭṭala cherla, and
the two places Paṟagimaṭṭu and Naṟṟakallu.
It is in characters of the 13th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 247.
(A.R. No. B. 465 of 1961-62.)
PRINCE OF WALES MUSEUM, BOMBAY TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the base of the pedestal of the image of Ambika, photograph from the Museum through
G.S. Gai.
Reads: Śrī Gōsabi-pratime.

inscription.head:
No. 248.
(A.R. No. B. 493 of 1961-62.)
TAUNDI (Stavanidhi), CHIKKODI TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the pedestal of image (2) of Pārśvanātha in the Brahmadēva temple.
It records the Supārśvadēva of draviḷa-Saṁgha.
It is in charactes of the 13th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 249.
(A.R. No. B. 494 of 1961-62.)
TAUNDI, CHIKKODI TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the pedestal of the third Jina image in the Brahmadēva temple.
This epigraph refers to the Balātkāra-gaṇa of Mula-saṁgha.
It is in characters of the 13th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 250.
(A.R. No. B. 510 of 1961-62.)
BHAṄKŪR, CHITAPUR TALUK, GULBARGA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Base of a panel of three images in the Jaina basti.

Of the three names in this label record only Śrī-Kanakakīrti is legible. It is in characters
of the 13th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 251.
(A.R. No. B. 539 of 1961-62.)
BALLAṬGI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Outer wall of the Rāmalinga Temple.

This incomplete fragment probably records the erection of the temple of Śrī-mahā-Basava
by Dasadamōja son of Bimbarasa.
It is in characters of the 13th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 252.
(A.R. No. B. 557 of 1961-62.)
HARVI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the Hanumān temple.

This fragmentary record registers a grant of one out of two oil-mills for a perpetual lamp
to be burnt before the god. The beginning portion of the record probably engraved on the top
of the pillar is broken and missing.
It is in characters of the 13th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 253.
(A.R. No. B. 559 of 1961-62.)
HIRA, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup in a field out side the village.

It records a grant of land sowable with one khanduga seed by mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kumāra
Allahulidēva of the Haihaya family to the temple of Lakshminārāyaṇadēva built by Śarvadēva-seṭṭi
of Baḷagaranūru.
It is in characters of the 13th century A.D.

[Note: The above note is based on the Annual Report, since both the estampage and the transcript
are found missing for verification].

inscription.head:
No. 254.
(A.R. No. B. 155 of 1962-63.)
GAḌIGARĒVULA, NANDYAL TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a below of the first pillar in the Bhōgēśvarasvāmin temple.
It is very much damaged and the purport is not clear.

edition.p: [This seems to be a broken piece].

inscription.head:
No. 255.
(A.R. No. B. 164 of 1962-63.)-(1).
ĀLAMPUR, ALAMPUR TALUK, MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Fragment of slab (1) in the open air Museum, Alampur.
This damaged fragment probably refers to a muni (Śrī-Pāladēva) and to a gift.

inscription.head:
No. 256.
(A.R. No. B. 164 of 1962-63.)-(2).
ĀLAMPUR, ALAMPUR TALUK, MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Another fragment (2) in the open air museum.
Refers to a muni whose name is not clear.

l.note:
This is not listed in the Annual Report. But the estampage is available in the
with No. B. 164.

inscription.head:
No. 257.
(A.R. No. B. 164 of 1962-63.)-(3).
ĀLAMPUR, ALAMPUR TALUK, MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Fragment (3) in the open air museum.

The meaning of this fragment is not clear. Like the previous fragment (No. 255) this also
is not listed in the Annual Report.

inscription.head:
No. 258.
(A.R. No. B. 173 of 1962-63.)
ĀLAMPUR, ALAMPUR TALUK, MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar in the maṇḍapa opposite the image of Mahānandi in the Bāla-Brahmēśvara temple.

Records the gift of a Jaya-ghanṭe (Bell) and a Chhatra (umbrella) to the god Brahmēśvara-
dēva by Murāri.

inscription.head:
No. 259.
(A.R. No. B. 181 of 1962-63.)
BHIMAVARAM, ALAMPUR TALUK, MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar of the Chauḍamma shrine in the Siva temple.
Badly damaged, purport not clear. It mentions [the place] Erugulu maḍu.
It is in characters of the 13th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 260.
(A.R. No. B. 212 of 1962-63.)
SAṄGAMĒŚVARAM [NEAR KŪDAVALLI VILLAGE], ALAMPUR TALUK, MAHABUB-
NAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar of the Nandi maṇṭapa, Sangamēśvara temple.

It registers a grant of an oil mill (Ghāṇa) to the god by Rēchīnāyaka for (burning) a
perpetual lamp (nandā-dīpa).
It is in characters of the 13th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 261.
(A.R. No. B. 818 of 1962-63.)
GUḌIHĀḶ, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built into the wall of the Īśvara temple.

It states that Sōmaya Son-in-law [aḷiya] of mahāsāmanta Dāsaṇna and Māchi gavuṇḍa
together made a gift of six mattar of land and a garden to the god Mahadēva of Gaṅga riṅguṇige.
It is in characters of the 13th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 262.
(A.R. No. B. 822 of 1962-63.)
NUGḌŌṆI, MĀNVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup near the Basavēśvara temple.

The record states that 12 mattar of land belongs to the god Nakharēśvaradēva of Siriyū
ru.
It is in characters of the 13th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 263.
(A.R. No. B. 829 of 1962-63.)
SUṄKĒŚVAR, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup in the field known as suḍugāḍagaddi.

It states that when Mallidēva was ruling granted two mattar of wet land to a person
named Bommaya.

The record, in characters of about the 13th century A.D., is dated in the cyclic year Khara,
Chaitra, śu. 15 Thursday. The cyclic year Khara occurred twice-(1) Śaka 1153 (1231 A.D.) and
(2) Śaka 1213 (1291 A.D.) in that century.

inscription.head:
No. 264.
(A.R. No. B. 830 of 1962-63.)
SUṄKĒŚVAR, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup near the Maruḷayyanaguḍi.

It states that at the instance of mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara Mallidēvarsa, Mādaseṭṭi Pambagōḍe
along with all prabhus made a grant of 15 mattar land to Bomayya.

It is dated in the cyclic year Jaya; Āsāḍa, śu. 11, Monday. It is in characters of the 13th
century A.D. The cyclic year Jaya occurred twice: (1) Śaka 1156 (1234 A.D.) and (2) Śaka 1216
(1294 A.D.) in that century.

inscription.head:
No. 265.
(A.R. No. B. 798 of 1962-63.)
KAMALĀPUR, GULBARGA TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in front of Hazrat Teg Burhanuddin Dargh-(1).

It states that Gaṅgidēva rāṇeya made a gift of land for the maintenance of a dharma-ēta
(charitable Picotta).
It is dated Śaka 1232 Sādhāraṇa corresponding to 1310 A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 266.
(A.R. No. B. 799 of 1962-63.)
KAMALĀPUR, GULBARGA TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in front of the Hazrat Teg Burhanuddin Dargah-(2).

This slab contains two records. The first one is in Nagari Script and Sanskrit language.
This portion of the record is very much damaged. It records a sarvamānya grant [of land] by
Gangi-dēva-rāṇeya. Probably it belongs to the same date as engraved in the previous record, viz.,
1310 A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 267.
(A.R. No. B. 514 of 1961-62.)
MAṄGAḶGI, CHITAPUR TALUK, GULBARGA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in the middle of a road.

It is damaged and illegible and mentions Suratāna Mahamaṁḍa • • • • • It records the grant
of a shop (aṁgaḍi) free from all taxes to heggaḍe Mādiseṭṭi.

It is dated Śaka 1253, Prajāpati, Śrāvaṇa Śu. 5 Vaḍḍavara corresponding to 1331 A.D., July
10, Wednesday.

inscription.head:
No. 268.
(A.R. No. B. 513 of 1961-62.)
KŌRVĀR, CHITAPUR TALUK, GULBARGA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in the ruined Īśvara temple.

It records the gift of a house free from tax to Saṁgamadēva. It is in characters of the
14th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 269.
(A.R. No. B. 515 of 1961-62.)
MOGAL-NĀGĀON, CHITAPUR TALUK, GULBARGA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the Bhogalinga temple.

This fragmentary record refers to an installation which is not clear in the village
Muguḷu-nage which is stated to be amara-grāma by probably Hālabāviyaja.
It is in characters of about the 14th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 270.
(A.R. No. B. 518 of 1961-62.)
SAṄGAI, CHITAPUR TALUK, GULBARGA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in the Harijan vāḍa.

This record in Nagarī script is incomplete and damaged. Hence purport not clear. It refers
to probably the rule of Suratāṇa Mahaṁmada and to Vēṇḍanūr (modern Beṇṇūr) and Sāṁgvi.

In the date portion only Śārvari, Āshāḍha. ba. 5, Friday is available. The cyclic year Śārvari
occured twice during this century [Śaka 1222 and Śaka 1282].
It is in characters of the 14th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 271.
(A.R. No. B. 528 of 1961-62.)
ARNAḶḶI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Fragment of a slab set-up on the bund of a field outside the village.
This fragment seems to refer to the land of a person named Dēvapa.
It is in characters of the 14th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 272.
(A.R. No. B. 524 of 1961-62.)
NĀGANHAḶḶI, GULBARGA TALUK, AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup in the Nagalingēśvara temple.

It refers to Svayaṁbhu Somanāthadēva of Kalabarige. It is in characters of the 14th century
A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 273.
(A.R. No. B. 549 of 1961-62.)
BEṬDŪR, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Here-stone near the school.

This damaged record mentions Manave which is probably the present Manvi. Rest of the
information is lost except Viḷambi. Kartika ba. in the date portion.
It is in characters of the 14th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 274.
(A.R. No. B. 558 of 1961-62.)
HARVI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a boulder in a locality called Ēṇi.

It mentions Māchinamaleya son of Kaṇilakōja with the epithet Śri-Bēchōja Komara was
the prahārī (watchman) of the place. Karaḍikal and Mānuve are mentioned as boundaries.
It is in characters of the 14th century A.D.

l.ka: śrī bēcōja komara biruda prasādhitaḷa prahāri ||

inscription.head:
No. 275.
(A.R. No. B. 588 of 1961-62.)
MĀDALĀPUR, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab near the image of Basavaṇṇa outside the village.
This broken piece refers to the grant of a village Giṟigaḍa by a person Bāḷa-Nayaka-Oḍeya.
It is in characters of the 14th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 276.
(A.R. No. B. 604 of 1961-62.)
MARCHĒḌA, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in a field outside the village.
It records a grant of land to the god Śrīpati-dēva by Malidēva of Maṟichēḍa.
It is in characters of the 14th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 277.
(A.R. No. B. 605 of 1961-62.)
PAḌAGANADOḌḌI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in the field of the village Paṭēl.
This badly damaged record seems to registers a grant made by a person named Timmaya.

It is dated in the cyclic year Durmukhi, Jēshṭa, ba. 30. The cyclic year Durmukhi occurred
twice during the 15th century viz., Śaka 1338 1416 A.D., and Śaka 1398 1476 A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 278.
(A.R. No. B. 619 of 1961-62.)
HOSAKŌṬE, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in a field called Yeḷamakki belonging to the Mūkāmbika temple of Kollūru.

This record is badly worn out and only the imprecatory portion of the writing can be
made out. It seems to have registered a grant.
It is in characters of the 14th century A.D.

l.ka: • • • • • barasi neṭṭa śilāśāsana [|*] svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vāyō harēti vasuṁdharā śaṣṭirvvarṣa
sahasrāṇi viṣṭāyāṁ jāyatē krimi [|*] dānapālanayōrmmadhye dānātsē-
yi śāsanakke • • • • • voḍeyara suhastada baraha śrī śrī śrī śrī [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 279.
(A.R. No. B. 633 of 1961-62.)
VĒṆŪR, KARKALA TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a Kalkuḍa mānasthamba, Mahalingeśvara temple.

This fragmentary record states that a person Biru-Seṭṭi by name got this mānasthambha
prepared (and erected).
It is in characters of the 14th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 280.
(A.R. No. B. 157 of 1962-63.)
GAḌIGARĒVULA, NANDYAL TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Second face of the pillar (No. 1) in the main shrine of Bhōgēśvarasvamin temple.

This tri-lingual record in Kannada, Nagari and Tamil scripts refers to the obeisance of
Vibhūti-Guro, disciple of Guru Panḍitārādhya who is described as Dāsa-Śrī.

l.note: In lines 4-7 Nagari and Tamil scripts-contents being the same.

inscription.head:
No. 281.
(A.R. No. B. 185 of 1962-63.)
CHENNIPĀḌU, ALAMPUR TALUK, MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in front of the Āñjanēya temple.
This incomplete record just mentions Śālivāhana śaka varusham.
It is in characters of about the 14th century A.D.

l.note: Stops here.

inscription.head:
No. 282.
(A.R. No. B. 234 of 1962-63.)
DŪḌKI, BANASWADA TALUK, NIZAMABAD DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar setup by the side of the dharmaśāla in front of the Kēśavanātha temple.

It seems to refer to the pillar as a gift of Pañchāksharāma. It is in characters of the 14th
century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 283.
(A.R. No. B. 492 of 1961-62.)
TAUNDI (Stavanidhi), CHIKKODI TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the pedestal of Parśvanātha image (1) in the Brahmadēva temple.

It states that the image of Parśvanātha was consecrated by Lakshmīsēna-bhaṭṭāraka and
the basadi was constructed by Laḷiyādēvi, the great-grand mother of Sēnarasa and a disciple
of Vīranandisiddhānta-Chakravarti of Mūla-saṁgha, Dēsiya-gaṇa and Pustaka-gachchha. Sculptor
Jinnōja carved the image.

It is dated Śaka 1(3)22, Vikrama, Chaitra Śu. 12 Monday which corresponds to 1400 A.D.,
March 8. [The month Chaitra was adhika].

l.ka: śrī mūla saṁgha dēśiyagaṇa pustakagacchada śrī vīraṇaṁdi
jiṁnōjaṁ māḍida pratime maṁgaḷa mahā śrī śrī ||

inscription.head:
No. 284.
(A.R. No. 37 of 1961-62.)
PĀNUGALLU [Pāngal], KOLLAPUR TALUK, MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in front of the pond called Rāmannaguṇḍam on the hill[fort].

This record partly in Sanskrit and partly in Kannada records the construction of the
temple of Mukti-Ramēśvara probably inside the hill fort of Pāngal by Dēva nārāyaṇa, the
son of Simhanārāyaṇa. Dēvanārāyaṇa was then administering the fort. The record states that
Simhanārāyaṇa, a brahmin belonging to Bhāradwāja gōtra hailed from Kashmira.

Regarding the date the Sanskrit portion of the record mentions rasābdhi-rāma-rāja
krōdhi-nivatsare. The kannada portion mentions Śaka 1346, Krōdhi, Mārggaśira, su. 15. It
corresponds to 1424 A.D., December 6, Wednesday. Lunar eclipse occured on that day but not
mentioned in the record.

l.ka: rō mukti rāmēśvarō mudā || bhāradvāja munīṁ-
dra gōtra vilasatkāśmīra viprānvayē | jātō viṭhṭhala
sucaritra saṁbhṛta guṇaḥ śrī dēvanārāyaṇaḥ [||] pitr
ma birudaṁ perōja sēnā bidaṁ vidyadbā-

inscription.head:
No. 285.
(A.R. No. B. 640 of 1961-62.)
KOKKAḌA, PUTTUR TALUK, SOUTHKANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Fragmentary slab lying in the Vaidyanāthadēva temple.

Excepting a portion of the date rest of the information is lost in this record. The available
information regarding the date is Śaka [1]365 expired 6, Śrāvaṇa, Śu. 11. It may belong to 1443
A.D., August 6, [Tuesday].

inscription.head:
No. 286.
(A.R. No. B. 501 of 1961-62.)
ŚRINGĒRI, SRINGERI TALUK, CHICKMAGALUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab on the southern side out side the Mallikārjuna temple.

It records a gift of land yielding 225 salige of paddy in two villages situated in
Kikunda-nāḍu, after purchase, by Daṇḍu-seṭṭi of Sringeri for feeding 12 brahmanas in the
temple of Mallikārjuna. This gift was made in the presence of the god Vidyaśankara when
Śrī Narasimhabhārati-oḍeya was on the pontifical throne of Śringeri.

It is dated Śaka 1380, Īśvara, Magha ba. 14 Sunday corresponding to 1458 A.D., February
12.

l.ka: lōkyanagarāraṁbhamūlastaṁbhāya śaṁbhavē [||*] svasti śrī jayābhyudaya śakavaruṣa
1380 neya yīśvara saṁvatsarada māgha ba 14 ·ā śivarātriya puṁṇya kāladali śrī-
vidyāśaṁkara dēvara saṁnidiyali śrī narasiṁha bhārati ·oḍeyaru yōga sāṁbrā-
jyava nāḷutiha kāladali siṁgēriya daṁḍu seṭi śrī mallikārjuna dēvara saṁnidhi
• • haṁneraḍu maṁdi brāṁhmarige chatrada dharmavanū māḍi barasi naḍasida śi-
lāśāsanada kramaveṁteṁdarye nānu bhaṁḍārada hiryyaṁṇagaḷa kaiyiṁda nūra-
yippattu hoṁnanū koṭu krayava koṁḍa kikuṁda nāḍoḷagaṇa gobhava
pagaḷa kaiyali ·ayivatayidu hoṁnanū koṭhu grayava koṁḍakāgara ha-
ṟayipattayidu salige bhata baha (e)raḍu haḷiyanū ·ā haḷige ·usava
• • tya sahavāgi haṁneraḍu maṁdi brāṁharige tsatrada dharṁmma ·u ·ā ca-

yida • • ha pālisidavarige vāraṇāsiyali sahasra kavileyanū brā-
kapileya (nū brāhmaranū) vadhisida dōśa yīdharamavanū pālisō • • •

ṁ yukte svāvisvivaḷiyaṁ gatutavaḥ kiṣṭatavoni casvaya ·ādattu sahāra

inscription.head:
No. 287.
(A.R. No. B. 634 of 1961-62.)
VĒṆŪR, KARKALA TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Broken slab in a field called Aṅganthāya.

In this completely worn out record only [Śaka] 1381 Pramāthi can be made out. It
corresponds to 1459 A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 288.
(A.R. No. B. 742 of 1962-63.)
PRINCE OF WALES MUSEUM, BOMBAY TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab in the Museum.

It records the grant of the village Voṟaur to Kēśavayya by Mādana daṇḍanāyaka, in the
presence of the god Nārāyaṇa of Hubbaḷi.
It is dated ś 1397, Jaya. Māgha, śu. 1. It corresponds to 1475 A.D., January 8, Sunday.

l.ka: la dāna dhārāpūrvakavāge dhāreya neṟadu koṭa

l.note: Stops abruptly.

inscription.head:
No. 289.
(A.R. No. B. 782 of 1962-63.)
GOBBARAVĀḌI, GULBARGA TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built into the wall of Anantaliṅga temple.

The record is fragment and incomplete. It records the construction of a corridar (Pauli,
probably to a temple) by Hanumānu, son of Vaijanāth of Kaśyapa gōtra. Hanumānu is described
as dingariga a servant.
It is dated (Śaka) 1410, Kīlaka corresponding to 1488 A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 290.
(A.R. No. B. 133 of 1961-62.)
AMBALAVĀYAL, WYNAD TALUK, KOZHIKODE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Cave in the Eḍakkal rock near the village.
Fragmentary, Reads: Śrī Dīpā.
It is in characters of the 15th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 291.
(A.R. No. B. 487 of 1961-62.)
KERŪR, CHIKKODI TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the base of the Pārśvanātha image in the basadi.

This fragmentary and incomplete record refers to Guṇabhadradēva of Mūlasangha and
Sēnagaṇa and an individual Beseṭṭi.
It is dated in the cyclic year Piṅgaḷa in characters of the 15th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 292.
(A.R. No. B. 506 of 1961-62.)
ŚRINGĒRI, SRINGERI TALUK, CHICKMAGALUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab near the Āñjaneya temple (northern wall) (in Nagari Script).

The record is very badly damaged and mutilated. It opens with an invocatory passage to
the god Gaṇēśa followed by the verse Namastunga etc. The rest is illegible.
It is in characters of the 15th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 293.
(A.R. No. B. 615 of 1961-62.)
RAICHUR, RAICHUR TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built into the hind wall of the Rāmaliṅga temple.

This is a very badly damaged inscription. It mentions Rāyachūra and refers to Keṁḍada
(dēva). Probably the record registered a grant the details of which are lost.

In the date portion it mentions the cyclic year Vyaya, Pushya, Śu (3) Friday. The given
details here are insufficient for verification for the cyclic year Vyaya occurred twice during this
century viz., Śaka 1343 (1421-22 A.D.) and Śaka 1403 (1481-82 A.D.).

l.note: Letter da is writen below the line.

inscription.head:
No. 294.
(A.R. No. B. 616 of 1961-62.)
RAICHUR, RAICHUR TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Another piece of a slab built into the wall of the Rāmaliṅga temple.

This broken and damaged slab seems to have recorded a grant of land to the god
Baṁmadēva. It mentions the place Sukhadare.
It is in characters of the 15th century A.D.

l.note: The top portion are broken and lost.

l.ka: la bāḷage saṁgana saṁ(pa cā)vēvada vinutaṁṇana po-

l.note: The bottom

inscription.head:
No. 295.
(A.R. No. B. 631 of 1961-62.)
MARṆE, KARKALA TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in a field called Naḍibeṭṭu.

This is a completely worn out record. Probably it registered some grants to a deity but
all details are lost. It is in characters of about the 15th century A.D.
The text is not given since only a few letters could be made out of this record.

inscription.head:
No. 296.
(A.R. No. B. 635 of 1961-62.)
BEḶAMAÑJE, PUTTUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A fragment lying out side the Anantēśvara temple.

This fragmentary piece seems to have recorded a land grant probably to the deity of the
place, the details of which are lost.
It is in characters of the 15th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 297.
(A.R. No. B. 636 of 1961-62.)
BEḶAMAÑJE, PUTTUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Another slab lying out side the Anantēśvara temple.

This highly damaged fragment seems to have recorded a land grant probably to the deity
of the place; the details of which are lost.
It is in characters of the 15th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 298.
(A.R. No. B. 742 of 1962-63.)
PRINCE OF WALES MUSEUM, BOMBAY TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab.

It records the grant of the village Voṟaūr as yimmaḍi dharma to Kēśavayya in the
presence of the god Aubaḷa of Bāḍa by Mādana yīṁmadi Siga-nāyaka who is described as
mahānāyakāchārya.
The record is in characters of the 15th century A.D.

l.note: Letterni is engraved above the line.

inscription.head:
No. 299.
(A.R. No. B. 834 of 1962-63.)
RAICHUR, RAICHUR TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Loose slab leaning against the wall near the tomb of Dārā-gāh.
Impression from the Superintendent Arabic and Persian Inscriptions, Nagpur, (Acc. No. 2217).

This fragmentary and damaged record mentions the place Penukōṁḍa and rest of the
information is indiscrete.
It is in characters of the 15th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 300.
(A.R. No. B. 806 of 1962-63.)
SARAḌIGI, GULBARGA TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab outside the Śaraṇa Basavēśvara temple.

This fragmentary record in Nāgari script is highly damaged and incomplete. The word
sahagamana can only be made out of it.
It is in characters of the 15th-16th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 301.
(A.R. No. B. 628 of 1961-62.)
KERAVASE, KARKALA TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab [2] set up in the mūḍabasti of Vardhamānasvāmi temple.

This very much damaged record speaks of a land grant made by Rāmusāla to the various
services in the basati of Vardhamānasvāmi.

It is dated Śaka 1433, Pramōda, Vaiśākha, Śu. 10, Thursday corresponding to 1510 A.D., April
18, Thursday.

l.ka: • bittuva bīja mū • • • • • bittuva bīja mū • • • • •
dōśakke hōharu vāraṇā[si]yali brāhmara koṁda dōśa-
dōśakke hōharu yiṁti śilāśāsanakke maṁgala mahā śrī śrī śrī

inscription.head:
No. 302.
(A.R. No. B. 562 of 1961-62.)
KALLŪR, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built into the wall of the Hanumān temple.

It states that at the instance of Sayiladinga wishing for the rule of Majalasara baini
Lakshaha-voḍeya in perpetuity, certain rules were promulgated to be followed by the people of
Kallūr regarding the treatment of cattle on occassions like kāra-huṇṇime, dīpāvali-pāḍya etc., as
also in respect of the disposal of the dead cattle.

It is dated Śaka 1466, Krōdhi, Mārgaśira śu. 6, Thursday. It corresponds to 1544 A.D.,
November 20, Thursday.

l.ka: śrī matu śālivāhana ṣa(śa)kavaruṣa 1466 neya krōdhināma saṁvatsarada
mārgasira śu 6 guruvāradalu śrīmatu śrīsayiladiṁgalu hiriyaru-
gaḷa nirūpa baralāgi majalasarabayeni lakṣaha voḍeyarige
stira rājyavāgiyirabēkuyaṁdu kalūra grāmake māḍida kaṭṭaḷe
kāra huṁṇavedinadalu yettugaḷanu vōḍisi ·ā[gi] baniyalilau ye-
·u ·āramāseya dinadalu maṁṇayara māḍalāgadu dīpāvaḷi pāḍyada di-
vasa • • yavotavila holadoḷage samagavayāḷu maga basavaṁṇana
·uṇasōdu yettu satare bhaṁḍiya mēle hākivoyabēku vidhiya di-
yalu naṁdiya mukhavāgi yetu ·āu yeṁmegaḷige ·uṇasōdu yetta
sattare ·astiya nikṣipusuvadu yi mariyādeyalu takādinada gere maḷe
beḷe saṁpadina grāmake jaya yidake (yā)ru ·upēkṣe māḍuvaru ·avare ·a-
ṁnyayigaḷu dharmake jaya basavana śrāpa nippārau [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 303.
(A.R. No. 2 of 1961-62.)
MUNIMAḌUGU, PENUKONDA TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab fixed to the proper right of the entrance into the main shrine (facing east) in the
Svayambhuvēśvara temple.

It is a broken slab. Records the grant of some lands to the god Svayambhudēva by
Siṁgapa and Tirumala for the merit of their father Lingarasa of Munimaḍagu.
It is dated Śaka 1479 Na[la], Āshādha śu. 12. It corresponds to 1556 A.D., June 19, (Friday).

l.ka: ṁ śubhamastu [|*] svasti śrī jayābhyudaya śālivāhana śakavaruṣaṁgaḷu 1479 na(la)
saṁvatsara ·āṣāḍha śu 12 lū śrīmatu pratāpa munimaḍagu • • • • •
ju liṁgarasara makkaḷu siṁgapa tirumalaya vīrapa [svaya]-
ṁbhudēvarige samarpisida sēve dēvara kāluve gadde be • •
ḷige saluva hola matu kelau holaṁnu mahi(ja)-

inscription.head:
No. 304.
(A.R. No. B. 632 of 1961-62.)
VEṆŪR, KARKALA TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Dambe pillar in the Mahālingēśvara temple.
In this worn out record excepting the date all other information are lost.

It is dated Śaka 1498, Dhatri, Chaitra, śu. 10, Sunday corresponding to 1576 A.D., March,
10. The weekday was however, Saturday.

inscription.head:
No. 305.
(A.R. No. B. 780 of 1962-63.)
BŌḶĒVĀḌ, GULBARGA TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup near the agasi.

This fragment seems to mention only San[Śaka ?] 1516 Sō[Sōmavāra]-[Monday]. It may
correspondece to 1594 A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 306.
(A.R. No. 3 of 1961-62.)
CHANDRAGIRI, CHANDRAGIRI TALUK, CHITTOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a boulder near a pond called Gurrālakuṇṭa about 3 furlongs to the north-east of the village.

This fragmentary record begins with the praśasti svasti Śrīman mahāmaṅḍaḷēśvara
arirāyavibhāḍa bhāsege • • • and stops abruptly. [Seems to belong to the Vijayanagara rule].
It is in characters of the 16th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 307.
(A.R. No. B. 553 of 1961-62.)
GŌRKAL, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab kept near the agasi.

It states that certain uncultivated lands were assigned to people who, in turn, had to remit
to the government a stipulated amount of money and quantities of ghee and paddy as tax (details
are there). Mentions some officials, of whom mokhāsāyis of Gorkal was one. Dēśa mukhi Bāsha
Ma(ḷa)ya Nārappa wrote this record.

The date śaka 1538, Vyaya, Āśvija ba. 7. Monday given here is irregular. The cyclic year
Vyaya occurred in śaka 1508. The existing cyclic year in Śaka 1538 was Naḷa. The probable date
seems to be 1586 A.D., September 25, [Sunday].

l.ka: maladāraru ma || majakūra gavaḍa mallaṁṇagavata kulaka-
raṇi (na) mēṭigavuḍa bāra balavata muṁttādavarige sāsavahā-
ki koṭa kavuluvūra holagaḷa sahaṁtava baḷasa biṭuyiralāgi yivake

demariyāde mūrane varuṣa ·ade mariyādeyalu tupa bhatta koḍabēku nālku va-
ruṣa modalāgi sālābādudara l 1 ke ·ayidu varuṣa 95 bhatta 818 (kka)ḷa tupayaraḍu

sēru • • 2 mariyāde • • kirdi kavulu koṭṭarevoṁdu bīḷu vokkalaraḷu kirdi māmūli
ā-

bōpaya[ma]-
rika nārapanu vūragavuḍaru kulkūjabigaḷu muṁttāda samasta prajegaḷu biṁnāha māḍalā-

gi manasige taṁdu bāḷubida holagaḷu kirdi māmūli ·āgabēkeṁdu nadarahu
mariyādeyalu

kavulu koḷu yidhevu sukhadalu naṁma kavulu naṁbi kirdi māmūli māḍikoṁḍu
kavulu | (śrī) yā-
ṇisadaru hola bhatta tupa rokka tetu koṁ-
ḍu bīḷu holagaḷa kirdi māḍikoṁḍu paraṁpareli sukha
dalu vakala tanava māḍikoṁḍu yihudu [rū]yeṁdu taṁma sukṛta sākṣiyāgi
barsa sāsana hāksi koṭakavulu yidadārā(ga)le tetu bāradu divāṇadāru baṁda-
re yiṁdhe kavulu naḍesabēku naḍasade tappidare turukarādare musānara ·āṇe brā-
hmarādare gōhatye māḍida hāṁge katti tuṁṇe kalupu kavulu tappidavarige ma-
re māḍikoḷadavarige yikavulu naṁbi māḍadavarige yida kaṇiyēdu
kovulu sahiya sāsana baradāta dēśamukhi bā(ṣa) ma(ḷa)ya || nārappa ||

inscription.head:
No. 308.
(A.R. No. B. 504 of 1961-62.)
ŚRINGĒRI, SRINGERI TALUK, CHICKMAGALUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab on the western side of the Mallikārjuna temple.

This damaged record mentions the setting up of the image of Nandi in front of Mallaiya-
dēva (the god) by a lady in the presence of several officers (named) and mahājanas. The pontiff
of Śringeri is mentioned here.
It is in characters of the 16th-17th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 309.
(A.R. No. B. 584 of 1961-62.)
KURKUND, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup near the well.
It states that the well was constructed by Rudradēva son of Chenavīrasvāmi of Hāganagēri.
It is in characters of the 16th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 310.
(A.R. No. B. 613 of 1961-62.)
VAḌAVĀṬI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a boulder near the Mūḍagiri temple on the hill.

It records a grant of 10 māna (of land) to Timapa by Nāgōjā, the mokhāsi of Vaḍavaṭi
in the presence of nāḍagavuḍa and samasta-praje (the people). There is a mention of maṭhada
Saraya Mōchinayya.

It is dated in the cyclic year Yuva and the month Śrāvaṇa. It is in characters of about
the 16th century. The cyclic year Yuva occurred twice during this century viz., Śaka 1437 (1515
A.D.) and Śaka 1497 (1575 A.D.).

inscription.head:
No. 311.
(A.R. No. B. 602 of 1961-62.)
MĀNVI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a boulder in the tank called Vakrāṇi-Kere.

This inscription in Kannada Script and Telugu language states that Siṅgaṇa, son of
[Chē]gada Mallinēni, installed the image of Hanumanta and setup the lamp-post for the merit of
his father. The present Mānvi was then called Mānuva paṭaṇa.

It is in characters of the 16th century A.D., and is dated in the cyclic year Vikrama,
Karttika, Śu. 11, Thursday. It is difficult to ascertain the exact date in the absence of Śaka year.

l.ka: maṁgaḷa mahā śrī śrī śrī śrī śrī [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 312.
(A.R. No. B. 206 of 1962-63.)
PRĀGAṬŪRU, ALAMPUR TALUK, MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone setup infront of a maṭha.
It seems to state the matha was founded by Vyshyas described as pañchavaṇige.
It is in characters of the 16th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 313.
(A.R. No. B. 820 of 1962-63.)
KĀTARAKI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup near a well outside the village.
Excepting the date rest of the information is lost.

It is dated Śaka 1523 Plava, Chaitra, Su. 15, Friday. It corresponds to 1601 A.D., April 7,
Tuesday.

l.note: Rest is badly damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 314.
(A.R. No. B. 498 of 1961-62.)
ŚRINGĒRI, SRINGERI TALUK, CHICKMAGALUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in front of the samādhi-guḍi behind the Vidyaśankara temple.

The record has expressed salutations to pontiffs Vidyātīrtha and Vidyaraṇya at the
beginning. It registers the gifts of garden and wet lands in Heggadde situated within Keḷanādu
belonging to Śringeri matha as Sarvamānya agrahāra named Narasimhapura to all the mahājanas
of various gōtras and sūtras who were residing in the Vaśisṭhāśrama on the bank of the river
Tungabhadra, also referred to as paśchima-vāhini, by the pontiff of Śringeri, Śri Narasimhabhārati,
disciple of Śri Narasimha-bhārati as an act of pleasing the god Narasimhēśvara. The record
contains details of the shares and names of donees. The gifted gardens contained 5500 areca trees
planted by Appaṇa and Śankaralinga and the wet lands yielded 500 khanḍuga of Paddy.

The record is dated Śaka 1525, Śōbhakṟit, Bhādrapada Śu. 10, Sunday which corresponds to
1603 A.D., September 4.

inscription.head:
No. 315.
(A.R. No. B. 596 of 1961-62.)
MĀNVI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. 6) built into the wall of the well called Jēḍara-bāvi.

In addition to the information as in No. 325 below the present record states that Timmaya
belonged to Mānuve [the present Mānvi] and was pēṭhegavuḍa of Narasapura. Also it refers to a
grant inam given to the person for the work of constructing the well.

It is dated Śaka 1529, Plavaṅga, Śrāvaṇa, śu [15]. It corresponds to 1607 A.D., July 28,
Tuesday. On the basis of this date the cyclic year Krōdhi of the five records [Nos. 325-329]
may be equated with Śaka 1526 and the given tithi their corresponds to 1604 A.D., September 1,
Saturday.

l.ka: na baḷike śiśyanige mudala baniyi cukke bhā-
ga sōnāpura yibāvi muṁde hosa sile •

inscription.head:
No. 316.
(A.R. No. B. 639 of 1961-62.)
KOKKAḌA, PUTTUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the Dambe pillar in the Vaidyanātha temple.

It records the setting up of Dambe [Stone pillar] in the temple of Vaidyanatha Swamy.
Who caused its not known.
It is dated Śaka 1548, Kshaya, Jeshṭha, Śu. 1. It corresponds to 1626 A.D., May 16, Tuesday.

inscription.head:
No. 317.
(A.R. No. B. 569 of 1961-62.)
KALLŪR, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On top of the slab. [No. 1575] First Face.

It states that the gharaḍa (garuḍa)gaṁbha of the Māraṭēśvara temple was set up by
Līngaṇṇa, son of gāṇigara Jābi-Seṭṭi.

It is dated śaka 1559, Īśvara, Māgha (A)māse. It corresponds to 1638 A.D., February 3,
Saturday.

l.ka: ke 1559 neya yīsvara saṁvatsarada māgha sudhamāse
gaṁba niṁditu yī kaṁba gāṇigira jābiseṭiya

inscription.head:
No. 318.
(A.R. No. B. 484 of 1961-62.)
KABBŪR, CHIKKODI TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in front of the Brahmadēva temple to the south east of the village.

It seems to refer to the construction of a temple and to the sculptor by name Krishna
belonging to Pāṇḍurangapura. The record gives an account of the family of the sculptor.

It is dated Śaka 1570, Sarvadhāri, Kārttika ba. 11, Wednesday, corresponding to 1648 A.D.,
November 1.
