inscription.head:
No. 319.
(A.R. No. B. 563 of 1961-62.)
KALLŪR, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in the maṭha (on two sides).

It records a grant of land and certain privileges to Chenna-Virayya of Kallūra-maṭha in
the presence of the gauda, Kulkarni, Seṭṭi, Chaudhari and others.

It is dated śaka 1572 [wrong for 1574] Nandana, Vaiśākha, adhika-ashṭami, Wednesday. It
corresponds to 1652 A.D., April 21. The month Vaiśākha was adhika.

inscription.head:
No. 320.
(A.R. No. B. 523 of 1961-62.)
HĪRĀPUR, GULBARGA TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup in the Śankaraliṅga well.

It states that the Vaishya Bhikkāji Sōmāji Mokhēḍa excavated a well and constructed the
temples of Sōmēśvara and Basavēśvara for the benefit of all with a stīpulation that all including
the maṭha should make use of them. The donor wished that his creations should always remain
the best-sarvōt-krshṭha.

It is dated Śaka 1578, Durmukhi, uttarāyana, Grīshmaṟtu, Jyeshṭha. śu. 2. Thursday. It
corresponds to 1656 A.D., May, 15.

l.ka: śrī gaṇādhipatayē namaḥ || śrī veṁkaṭēśāya namaḥ || svasti śrī nṛpa-
vatsare ·uttarāyaṇi grīṣmaṟtau jyēṣṭha māse śukla pakṣe dvi-
tīyāyāṁ guruvāsare vaiṣya bhikkāji sōmāji mokhēḍa parōpakārā-
rtha kaṭṭisidaṁthā bāvi sōmēśvara [ba]śvēśvara śivālaya sarvōtkṛ-

inscription.head:
No. 321.
(A.R. No. 55 of 1961-62.)
TERPOLE, SANGAREDDI TALUK, MEDAK DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the Dhvaja-stambha in front of the Ānjanēya temple.

It mentions Kāmayya, a disciple of Venkaṭayya and seems to refer to the setting up of
the pillar. Bottom portion of the record is completely damaged.

It is dated Śaka 1594, Virōdhikritu, Chaitra śu. 1, Budhavāra corresponding to 1671 A.D., •
• • • • •

inscription.head:
No. 322.
(A.R. No. B. 561 of 1961-62.)
KALLŪR, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the wooden beam on the ceiling of the primary school.

It states that the Southern door was made probably by Musredi during the regime of
Āgākhusru Sāheb. It is dated Śaka 1605 Rudhirōdgāri, Chaitra. śu. 1, Monday corresponding to 1683
A.D., March 19.

inscription.head:
No. 323.
(A.R. No. B. 521 of 1961-62.)
HĪRĀPUR, GULBARGA TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab on the wall of a well in the village.

The inscription in Nagari Script records the construction of a dharmasarovara (tank) and
a dharmaśālā (choultry) by Raṁgōji of Mudgala-gōtra for his own merit.

It is dated Śaka 1609, Prabhava, Chaitra, Śu. 7, Thursday corresponding to 1687 A.D., March
10.

l.ka: ravāya namaḥ || śrī manmudgala gōtra saṁ-
prabhavanāma saṁvatsara caitra śudha sa(ptami) gu-

inscription.head:
No. 324.
(A.R. No. B. 610 of 1961-62.)
SIRVĀR, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab near the Hanuman temple opposite the Venkateśvara temple.

It states that the God Sañjīva-Hanumantadēva was installed at Siravāra by Gururāya, the
son of Venkappa of Jamadagni-gōtra and hailing from Nāmalagāvi.
It is dated Śaka 1615, Śrīmukha, Chaitra, śu. 1, Monday corresponding to 1693 A.D., March 27.

inscription.head:
No. 325.
(A.R. No. B. 591 of 1961-62.)
MĀNVI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. 1) built into the wall of the well called Jēḍara-bāvi.
It states that gavuḍa Dupada Tiṁmaya of Sarajāpura built this well.

It is dated Krōdhi, Bhādrapada ba. 3. The details are insufficient for verification. The record
is in characters of the 17th century. The cyclic year Krōdhi occurred twice during the 17th century
viz., (1) Śaka 1526 (1604 A.D.) and (2) Śaka 1586 (1664 A.D.) [Refer No. 315 above]. The probable
date of the record may be 1604 A.D., September 1, (Sunday).

inscription.head:
No. 326.
(A.R. No. B. 592 of 1961-62.)
MĀNVI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. 2) built into the wall of the well called Jēḍara-bāvi.
This is a copy of the above [No. 325] inscription.
Text: Same as above.

inscription.head:
No. 327.
(A.R. No. B. 593 of 1961-62.)
MĀNVI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. 3) built into the wall of the well called Jēḍara-bāvi.
This is the second copy of the record No. 325. Text is the same as above.

inscription.head:
No. 328.
(A.R. No. B. 594 of 1961-62.)
MĀNVI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. 4) built into the wall of the well called Jēḍara-bāvi.
This is the third copy of the record No. 325. The text of it is the same as above.

inscription.head:
No. 329.
(A.R. No. B. 595 of 1961-62.)
MĀNVI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. 5) built into the wall of the well called Jēḍara-bāvi.

The information of this record is the same as in No. 325 above. The additional information
of this record is that it states that Tiṁmaya who constructed the well, was the son of Gavuḍa-
Dupada-Chiṭapa of Sarjāpura.
The date is the same as in No. 325.

inscription.head:
No. 330.
(A.R. No. B. 813 of 1962-63.)
ATNŪR, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup in front of the agasi.

This is an incomplete record. It contains only the date portion viz., Āngīrasa, Mārgaśira,
Su. 1 without the Śaka year. It is in 17th century characters. The cyclic year Angīrasa occurred
twice during this century: (1) Śaka 1554 = 1632 A.D., and (2) Śaka 1614 = 1692 A.D.

At the end the word ‘Atanū’ is mentioned. Probably it refers to Atanūru, the place of
the origin of this record.

l.note: Stops here.

inscription.head:
No. 331.
(A.R. No. B. 520 of 1961-62.)
BHĪMNAḶḶI, GULBARGA TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built into the wall of village school.

This fragmentary record seems to register a gift to the god Sōmanāthadēva of Yalade
(modern Aland).

inscription.head:
No. 332.
(A.R. No. B. 554 of 1961-62.)
GŌRKAL, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in the field known as Kere.
It states that the sluice was constructed by Malik-sandal-Rustumkhan.
It is in characters of the 17th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 333.
(A.R. No. B. 585 of 1961-62.)
MĀḌAGIRI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set-up infront of the Hanumān temple.

The beginning and the end of the record are broken and lost. This damaged fragmentary
record seems to refer to the construction of a maṭha and a lease of land for three years.
It is in the characters of the 17th century A.D.

l.note: 'kaTida' written above the line.

inscription.head:
No. 334.
(A.R. No. B. 597 of 1961-62.)
MĀNVI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. 7) built into the wall of the well called Jēḍara-bāvi.

It informs the steps-sōpāna (to the well) were built during the office of Ṭhāṇādār Sheik
Nijām Mujumdār Mudōpa, Nighāvan Nārapa, Śaranōbat Nasaravī and Sabanīsa Antapa.
It is in characters of the 17th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 335.
(A.R. No. B. 814 of 1962-63.)
ATNŪR, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar setup in front of the agasi.

This record mentions only the cyclic year Āngīrasa. It reads the fixation of the quantity
of Paddy and ghee to be given by the vokkalus [cultivators] during the specified period for the
land leased. The fixation seems to be a general assessment for the entire village. Atanura, the
village is mentioned in the record. The latter part of the record is much damaged. [See No. 334
above].
It is in characters of the 17th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 336.
(A.R. No. B. 826 of 1962-63.)
RĀJALADINNI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup near the maṭha.
This record in Nāgari script is completely worn out and mentions a maṭha.
It is in characters of the 17th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 337.
(A.R. No. B. 791 of 1962-63.)
HOLKUNDA, GULBARGA TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built in the floor of the Baḍigēra well (1).

This damaged record probably recorded the excavation of a well by Basavappa son of
Chyanamma.

It is dated in the cyclic year Jaya, Chaitra, śu. 6. The Śaka year is not mentioned. It is
in characters of the 17th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 338.
(A.R. No. B. 792 of 1962-63.)
HOLKUNDA, GULBARGA TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built into the floor of the Baḍigēra well (2).
It states that Basuvapa (was) the son of (the parents) Chanamala and Narsaka.
It is in characters of the 17th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 339.
(A.R. No. B. 586 of 1961-62.)
MĀDALĀPUR, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup at the entrance of the Village No. 1.

The purport of this damaged record is not clear. It mentions a certain Māhavā-gauḍa and
refers to a mānya.
It is in characters of the 17th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 340.
(A.R. No. B. 587 of 1961-62.)
MĀDALĀPUR, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup at the entrance of the village No. 2.
It is a copy of the above record No. 339.

inscription.head:
No. 341.
(A.R. No. B. 522 of 1961-62.)
HĪRĀPUR, GULBARGA TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup on the wall of Gautappa well.

It speaks of excavation of a well, construction of a choultry and development of a garden
for public use by Gautama of Kāśyapa-gōtra, a resident of Hīrāpura. These were dedicated to the
god Śrī-Rāma.

The record gives the dates of commencement and completion of the works of ex-
cavating the dharmasarōvavara (1) Śaka 1651 (wrong for 1653) Virōdhikrit, Chaitra. śu. 5 cor-
responding to 1731 A.D., and (2) Śaka 1656, Pramādi, Māgha śu. 13 corresponding to 1734 A.D.,
February 5, Tuesday, March 31 [Wednesday].

l.ka:
śrī gaṇādhipatayē namaḥ || chā(śā)radāyainamaḥ śaraṇāgatadēvārti haraṇaṁ śaraṇaṁ

inscription.head:
No. 342.
(A.R. No. B. 608 of 1961-62.)
RĀJOḶḶI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying outside the village.

This record is broken and very much damaged. It seems to have recorded a deed. But with
its present condition the exact meaning of it cannot be made out. Gōpāladēva (god), Venkaṇayya,
Hālamattarasa-Rāma, Bhairavana-rēkhu (probably a measurement), Sattāgāla, Vīrayya Aya, a son
(name lost) of Mudalōja and a son (name lost) of Rukmaya are mentioned.

It is dated Śaka 1681, (Pramathi), Jēshṭha ba. 12. It corresponds to 1759 A.D., June 21,
Thursday.

inscription.head:
No. 343.
(A.R. No. B. 609 of 1961-62.)
SIRVĀR, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the wall of the Veṅkateśvara temple.

It states that Mudugala Bhīmarao, the carpenter along with Gautama-gowḍa of Kallūra
grāma prepared the chariot of the temple and got exemption from the payment of the tax birāḍa.
Probably, the seṭṭis (merchants) of the place made this exemption.

It is dated Śaka 1682, Vikrama, Adhikaśrāvana ba. 3, Thursday corresponding to 1760 A.D.,
July 30. The week day was Wednesday.

inscription.head:
No. 344.
(A.R. No. B. 235 of 1961-62.)
GANAPATI, COIMBATORE TALUK, AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built into the west wall of the front maṇḍapa of the Vēṇugōpālasvāmin temple.

Refers to the service (to the god) by Mādayya, the son of Śankarayya and the grand son
of Madanayya of Terakaṇāmbi.

It is dated Śaka 1686 and Kali 4865, Svabhānu, Jyeshṭha, śu. 5. The cyclic year Svabhānu
occurred in Śaka 1685 while in 1686 the next cyclic year Taraṇa occured.

l.note: This line seems to have been rubbed after engraving.

inscription.head:
No. 345.
(A.R. No. B. 505 of 1961-62.)
ŚRINGĒRI, SRINGERI TALUK, CHICKMAGALUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a bell hanging in front of the Bhavānī shrine inside the Mallikārjuna temple.
Reads: Śrī Vidyāśankara | tūka ma 7 || 6 || 0

inscription.head:
No. 346.
(A.R. No. B. 118 of 1961-62.)
GUḌILŌVA, BHIMILIPATNAM TALUK, VISAKHAPATNAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab near the waterfall close to the temple of Ranganātha about two miles from the village.

Records the raising of a garden containing several varieties of trees for the use of the
god Ranganāyaka by Nārāyaṇa Gajapati, son of Nārāyaṇarāju and Buchchi Veṁkamāmba of
Dhananjaya-gotra, for his own welfare and prosperity.
It is in late characters of the 17th-18th century A.D.

l.ka: śrī madaśēṣaprastuti pātra saura[kṣa]tra putra dhanaṁ-
jaya sagōtra maṁtra kṛtṣatrakula pavitra śrīnā-

la tulasī bilyētaru nivōruṁbaka raṁbita ni-

inscription.head:
No. 347.
(A.R. No. B. 800 of 1962-63.)
KAMALĀPUR, GULBARGA TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in the Hiri-maṭha.

This fragmentary record seems to refer to a well, probably excavated by Harabīraya in
the premises of Hiri-maṭa (maṭha). There is a reference to 12 mana (māna) at the beginning and
41 ba (baḷḷa) at the end.
It is in late characters of the 18th century A.D.

l.note: Letter ri is written below the line.

inscription.head:
No. 348.
(A.R. No. B. 801 of 1962-63.)
KAMALĀPUR, GULBARGA TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab fixed into the steps of the Rāmatīrtha tank in the Rāmaliṅga temple.

This one line record in Nāgari late characters of the 17th-18th century A.D., reads
Vīrō-dhāni Mudhōji dēva(gne) (dēvange ?).

inscription.head:
No. 349.
(A.R. No. B. 811 of 1962-63.)
ARŌLI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab setup in front of the Īśvara temple.

Refers to a well probably excavated by Kulakaruṇi Apāji. It mentions Rāmā at the
beginning.
It is in characters of the 18th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 350.
(A.R. No. B. 819 of 1962-63.)
JAMMALADINNI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in the field of Paṭavari.

It refers to the border probably of the village in which the record is found. It is in late
characters.

inscription.head:
No. 351.
(A.R. No. B. 821 of 1962-63.)
KORAVI, MĀNVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a boulder at the entrance of the village.

The purport of the record is not clear as it is highly damaged. It is dated in the Cyclic
year Yuva. Jēshṭha, śu. 1, Śaka year is not mentioned.
It is in late characters of the 18th century A.D.

l.note: Rest is very much damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 352.
(A.R. No. B. 778 of 1962-63.)
DHARWAR, DHARWAR TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Impression received from Dr. G.S. Dixit, (4), Karnataka University, Dharwar
[Findspot: Korōchi (?) Hatkanangale Taluk, Kolhapur District].

This Jaina record is damaged and speaks of a number of events taking place in stages in
between the dates 1799 A.D., and 1820 A.D.

It states that the two brothers Rāyappa and Dēvappa, the two sons of a nāyaka
(Narasimha nāyaka) constructed a jinālaya and consecrated the image of Pārśvanātha and other
images probably under the guidance of Jīnasenāchārya, the jīna muni. The jīnalaya was equipped
with prākāra, gōpuradwāra, māna-sthambha and dhwaja-sthambha. Perhaps this first stage of work
was over by the first date of the record (1799 A.D.). Some more works were completed by 1820
A.D. But the information about which is not clear since the record is very much damaged.

The two dates in the record are:
1 Śaka 1720, Kālayukta, Māga, Śu [|] Monday. corresponds to 1799 A.D., February 5, Tuesday.
2 Śaka 1742, Vikrama [Mārgśi]ra, śu. 14. It corresponds to 1820 A.D., December 18, Monday.

inscription.head:
No. 353.
(A.R. No. B. 178 of 1962-63.)
ĀLAMPUR, ALAMPUR TALUK, MAHABUBNAGAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the pedestal of a Śiva-linga in the Bāla-Brahmēśvara temple.
It reads Valiparappa.
